metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: Manual Updates

body: Sections and Sub Sections New Process Old Process Date 3SquaresVT Time Limited Benefits Time Limits Enrolling in ICAN in Their Third Countable Month Customers enrolling in ICAN in their third countable month may qualify for a temporary exemption to allow time for them to enroll. Customers must complete ICAN orientation in their third countable month to qualify for a temporary exemption. Customers who complete ICAN enrollment may receive up to two additional temporary exemptions (for a maximum of three temporary exemptions) to allow them time to connect to their local ICAN team and complete necessary assessments. The local ICAN team will communicate the need for these additional exemptions to the ICAN team at ESD. The ICAN team will enter the code 15 and CATN. Enrollment means connected to an ICAN partner to complete required paperwork, set up a Vermont Job Links (VJL) account and schedule a meeting with the local employment team. Temporary exemptions can only be used once per enrollment. If the customer fails to enroll in ICAN following the application of an exemption they do not qualify for additional exemptions. Customers who are not eligible for ICAN are not eligible for temporary exemptions. New Process 04/17/2026 Resources Vehicles Excluded Vehicles Exclude vehicles for one of these reasons only: The entire value of one registered or unregistered operable vehicle per adult. The entire value of one registered or unregistered operable vehicle per child of driving age when needed to attend school or work. Exclude vehicles subject to Vermont registration for one of these reasons only: The entire value of one motor vehicle per 3SVT household. 4/17/2026 Expedited Service Verification of Other Eligibility Factors Link to public facing Examples of Verification added. Resource link was not previously included. 4/17/2026 Identity Verification Link to public facing Examples of Verification added. Resource link was not previously included. 4/17/2026 Verification and Case Notes Verification Link to public facing Examples of Verification added. Resource link was not previously included. 4/17/2026 General Work Requirements Exemptions Removed Community Kitchen Academy as an allowable program. Program ended in 2024. Community Kitchen academy met half time school enrollment. 04/17/2026 General Work Requirements Verification Pending SSI application can be used to verify disability Clarification added 04/17/2026 Simplified Reporting No Interim Report Updated date from 6th to 2nd. Date was incorrect 04/17/2026
heading: Clarifications

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: Manual Updates

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 100 General Information

body:
New information in the manual is highlighted and noted with a . See Manual Updates and for details.

Section with expandable text are noted with a throughout the manual.
heading: 100 General Information

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 100 General Information

body:
The Food and Nutrition Act of 2008, as amended, authorized SNAP to help low income persons and families stretch their food budget to put three healthy meals on the table every day.

The federal program is known in Vermont as the 3SquaresVT 3SVT program.
heading: 100.1 3SquaresVT Purpose

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 100 General Information

body: The U.S. Department of Agriculture’s FNS delegates to the Vermont DCF the responsibility for the administration of the 3SVT program within the state. These responsibilities include, but are not limited to: Approval of applicant households in compliance with federal regulations. Issuance, control and accountability of 3SVT benefits. Processing recipient claims and credits for lost benefits. Ensuring the civil rights of applicants/recipients by: maintaining the confidentiality of records; providing notice of eligibility or ineligibility; providing notice of all adverse action; providing information about and assistance in filing a request for a fair hearing, including an explanation of continuation of benefits; and providing equal application of all standards without regard to age, race, color, sex, disability, religion, national origin, political belief or limited English proficiency. Program information activities. Developing and maintaining complaint procedures. Developing, conducting and evaluating training. Conducting performance reporting reviews such as quality control and management evaluation. Submitting accurate and timely financial and program reports. Keeping records necessary to determine whether the program is conducted in compliance with federal regulations.
heading: 100.2 State Agency Responsibilities

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 100 General Information

body: 3SVT case information is confidential under Federal and State law. Use or disclosure of information for 3SVT case records is restricted to the following people who must adequately protect the information against unauthorized disclosure.
heading: 100.3.1 Disclosure Summary

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 100 General Information

body:
People directly connected with the administration of: 3SVT Reach Up , Reach Ahead , and Reach First Medicaid Any state program administered under a plan approved under the Social Security Act, which is required to participate in IEVS Social Security RSDI SSI CS Food Distribution programs The National School Lunch Program for the purpose of approving eligibility of children for free meals based on their receipt of 3SVT Any other federally aided means-tested program

Note : Release of information is limited to the extent that the information is useful in establishing or verifying eligibility or benefit amounts under those programs. In addition, Persons connected with the verification of immigration status of non-citizens through SAVE . Employees of the United State Comptroller General’s Office, upon written request, for audit purposes. Any local, state, or federal law enforcement official, upon a written request, for the purpose of investigating an alleged violation of the 3SVT program. The request must include: the person’s name; violation being investigated; and identity and authority of person making the request. Any local, state, or federal law enforcement official, upon a written request, for purpose of investigation of a felony crime or a probation or parole violation. The information that can be provided is limited to the address, social security number and any available photograph of: a 3SVT recipient who is fleeing to avoid prosecution, custody, or confinement for a felony crime or a violation of parole or probation; or a household member who has information about another household member who is under investigation for a felony crime or probation or parole violation.
heading: 100.3.2 Administrative Disclosure

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 100 General Information

body:
3SVT household members may review materials contained in their case file during normal business hours, if a written request is received from: A responsible member of the household. A current authorized representative. Any person authorized in writing to act on the household’s behalf.

Confidential information may be withheld such as the names of people who have disclosed information about the household without the household's knowledge, or the nature or status of pending criminal prosecutions.
heading: 100.3.3 Household Access

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 100 General Information

body:
A 3SVT applicant or recipient must not be discriminated against in any aspect of program administration including, but not limited to: Approval Issuance of benefits Conduct of fair hearings Conduct of any other program service

3SVT benefits must be extended to all eligible households without regard to age, race, color, sex (including gender identity and sexual orientation), disability, religious creed, national origin, political beliefs or limited English proficiency.

All offices administering the 3SVT program must also prominently display the poster “And Justice for All” provided by the USDA .
heading: 100.4.1 Program Administration Requirements

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 100 General Information

body:
Persons or agencies desiring to file a complaint alleging discrimination on the basis of age, race, color, sex (including gender identity and sexual orientation), disability, religious creed, national origin, political belief or limited English proficiency may contact: USDA, Director, Office of Civil Rights, Room 326-W, Whitten Building, 1400 Independence Avenue, SW, Washington, DC 20250-9410; or call (202) 720-5964 (voice and TDD) and/or Commissioner’s Office, Consumer Concerns Team, 280 State Drive, Waterbury, Vermont 05671-1080

If a person expresses an interest in filing a discrimination complaint: Explain both the FNS and the State of Vermont nondiscrimination complaint systems. Explain what information is necessary for investigation.
heading: 100.4.2 Right to File a Complaint

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 100 General Information

body: Discrimination complaints must be filed not later than 180 days from the date of the alleged discrimination. The complaint must contain the following information: The name, address, and phone number of the person alleging discrimination, or other means of contacting this person. The location and name of the office which is accused of discriminatory action. The nature of the incident, action, or program administration that led to the complaint. The reason for the alleged discrimination (age, race, color, sex (including gender identity and sexual orientation), disability, religious creed, national origin, political belief or limited English proficiency). The names, titles (if appropriate), and addresses of persons who may have knowledge of the alleged discriminatory act. Each date on which the action occurred.
heading: 100.4.3 Discrimination Complaint Requirements

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 100 General Information

body: If a person makes verbal accusations to a DCF employee and is unable or reluctant to put the allegations in writing, the employee must ask the person to contact FNS directly. If the person is unwilling to do so, the employee must document the complaint and forward it to the Director, Office of Civil Rights.
heading: 100.4.4 Verbal Discrimination Complaints

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 100 General Information

body:
Households note in writing the name of the person they have chosen as an AR to act on behalf of the household on a 139REP or 202. If identified on the 202, a 139REP must be sent to the household. A written signature is required for all ARs; electronic signatures are not allowed.

Households can have multiple ARs at a time. ARs must be: an adult, not a member of the household, and aware of the household’s situation.

ARs complete the following actions when acting for the household: apply for 3SVT benefits for the household, complete 3SVT Work Requirements screening, report for the household, and access the benefits to purchase allowable food for the household.

ARs must verify their identity when applying on behalf of a household.

ARs are not issued their own EBT card. ARs use the EBT card issued to the household.

ARs are not limited to the number of households they can represent.

See, Addiction Treatment Centers and Group Living for the Disabled for AR requirements related to these households.
heading: 100.5.1 AR Procedures

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 100 General Information

body: Restrictions apply to who can be an assigned as an AR. People disqualified for IPV may not be an AR for the household unless there is no other adult in the household and a new AR can’t be assigned. Note : Other excluded persons may be an AR for the household if the person is an adult with knowledge of the household’s situation. If a household reports their AR is disqualified, the ES must ask if another adult can act as the AR. If yes, obtain a new written statement. If no, the disqualified AR can remain. Retailers who accept 3SVT or agency employees who approve or issue benefits may only act as an AR if: approved in writing by the agency director, and no one else is available to serve as AR. Homeless meal providers are not eligible to act as an AR for a homeless household. Organizations cannot be an AR. Households must name an individual at the organization as their AR.
heading: 100.5.2 AR Restrictions

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 100 General Information

body:
People are disqualified from acting as an AR for up to one year when the agency determines the AR: Lied about the household’s situation Knowingly provided false information about the household Used the benefits improperly

Exception : ARs for group living for the disabled or addiction treatment centers are not disqualified.

Notify the household and the AR with advance notice when the AR is disqualified. Include in the notice: name and telephone number to contact for questions, fair hearing rights , the reason for the action, and the action that will be taken.
heading: 100.5.3 Disqualifications

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 100 General Information

body:
Households with an AR are responsible for any overpayments due to information the AR provided.

Exception : Households are not responsible for overpayments caused by an AR assigned by an addiction treatment center.
heading: 100.5.4 Overpayments

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 100 General Information

body:
Release #25-6

Effective Date 12/15/2025

Legal Base Food and Nutrition Act of 2008, as amended. 7 CFR § 271.1 7 CFR § 272.1 7 CFR § 272.6 7 CFR § 273.2 (n)
heading: 100.6 Last Update

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 100 General Information

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body:
New information in the manual is highlighted and noted with a . See Manual Updates and for details.

Section with expandable text are noted with a throughout the manual.
heading: 200 Application

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body:
The ESD 202, Application , must be readily available to potentially eligible households. Provide an ESD 202 to anyone who requests the form.

3SquaresVT in a SNAP households have the option to complete a simplified application, the ESD 202 3SNP. If a 3SquaresVT in a SNAP application is submitted for a household that is not 3SquaresVT in a SNAP eligible, the ES will note in CATN all 202 questions during the interview.

Encourage the household to file an ESD 202 the day they contact the agency requesting information about 3SVT. Tell the household that applying for 3SVT does not affect time limits for other programs.

Tell households who contact the agency for 3SVT but do not wish to come to the office: they can apply online at http://dcf.vermont.gov/mybenefits/apply , or an ESD 202 can be mailed to them the same day.
heading: 200.1 Overview

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body:
The application process begins when the household files a complete ESD 202 for 3SVT benefits. Households must complete the following actions as part of the application process: submit and complete the ESD 202, an interview, and verify all required information.

Deny the application if the household refuses to cooperate with any of these actions.
heading: 200.2.1 Application Requirements

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body:
The application process begins when the household submits an ESD 202 for 3SVT benefits with, at a minimum, the: name, address, and signature of the primary person or AR.

Do not require the household to submit the remainder of the ESD 202 when only the minimum information is received. Ask the remaining questions during the interview and document the answers in CATN .

Note: Electronic signatures through online applications are acceptable. Electronic typed signatures, with or without authentication measures, are acceptable. Applications signed by a “mark” do not require the signature of a witness.

Return incomplete ESD 202s to the household for completion. Obtain any other missing information needed to complete the application during the interview.

The ESD 202 may be submitted: In-person, By mail, By fax, or Online electronic application.

Application for 3SVT may be made by any of the following people: Head of Household Spouse of the head of household Other responsible household member Authorized representative (AR) See: General Information for AR qualifications. See: Special Household Situations for AR requirements related to these households.
heading: 200.2.2 Filing and Completing the Application

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body: Provide households who file their ESD 202 online at a district office: An opportunity to review the electronic ESD 202, and A copy of the information submitted on the electronic application.
heading: 200.2.2.1 Online Applications

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body: The application date is the date a complete ESD 202 is received and date stamped in the: district office, ADPC .
heading: 200.2.3 Application Date

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body:
If the ESD 202 is submitted during normal work hours, use that date as the application date.

If the ESD 202 is submitted after hours, weekends or holidays, use the next business day as the application date.

Interviews are not required to be completed on the application date. Accept ESD 202s with only the minimum requirements and record the date accepted as the application date.
heading: 200.2.3.1 Online and FAX Submission

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body:
Enter the application on the date it is received. Use the program identified under the Household Information section of the ESD 202 if the requested program isn’t marked on page one. Screen the application for expedited processing on the day the application is received in the district office.

Indicate on the STAT/STAT panel if the household is eligible for 3SquaresVT or 3SquaresVT in a SNAP.

Review PERS/D/HIST to see if the household members are known to ACCESS.
heading: 200.2.4 ACCESS Entry of the Application

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body: Households may withdraw their application at any time before their eligibility is determined. ES must : Contact the household to confirm the withdrawal, CATN: the contact with the household, and the reason for withdrawal. Send an ESD 220, Notice of Decision to the household to advise: the application was denied due to the withdrawal request, and the household may reapply.
heading: 200.2.4.1 Withdrawal of the Application

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body:
Households may use the same application and apply at the same time for 3SVT and other agency programs, such as Reach Up .

Complete a single interview for households jointly applying.

Note : A 202 received for an active 3SVT case, not due for recertification, must be used as a change report. The certification period cannot be changed.

Process 3SVT benefits following the required 3SVT policies and timeframes. Do not delay processing 3SVT because the other program is pending.

Households are not required to file another application for 3SVT when their application for the other program is denied. 3SVT cannot be denied solely because the other program’s application was denied.

Process 3SVT benefits without including cash assistance when it’s uncertain the cash program will be approved, or the amount is unknown. Process 3SVT to include the cash assistance when that program is approved. Notice of adverse action is not required for joint applications when 3SVT benefits are reduced because a cash assistance program is approved.
heading: 200.2.5 Joint Applications for Agency Programs

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body:
A special application process for offenders who are about to be released.

Follow the normal application process outlined in Application Process and Interview .
heading: 200.2.6 Offender Benefits Enrollment Initiative

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body: Households must complete an interview at application.
heading: 200.3.1 Interview Requirement

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body:
Send the ESD 202C, Benefits Interview Appointment Notice, when you receive the ESD 202. Allow the household five calendar days to complete the interview. Note : The ADPC sends the ESD 202C for applications received by them. Note : The ADPC does not send the ESD 202C when a 3SquaresVT in a SNAP application is received by them. The ES will send the ESD 202C after two cold calls are unsuccessful.

Complete the interview as soon as possible after the application date to allow the household to receive benefits within 30-days of that date.

Exception : Complete an interview immediately for households qualifying for expedited processing .
heading: 200.3.2 Scheduling

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body:
Hold the interview with one of the following persons: Head of Household , Spouse, Other responsible household member, Authorized representative

Note : People being interviewed may bring anyone they choose to the interview.

Note: Do not use children as interpreters.
heading: 200.3.3.1 Who to Interview

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body:
Complete the interview by telephone. Make two cold call attempts within 5 days to the household to complete the interview when they are not at the office.

Note : If the customer does not provide a phone number CATN that cold calls were attempted but could not be completed because a phone number was not provided.

Exception : Complete a face-to-face interview for the following reasons: Household or authorized representative request. Needed to resolve or clarify specific issues.

CATN if the interview was completed by telephone or face-to-face.
heading: 200.3.3.2 How to Complete the Interview

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body:
Review ACCESS for household information known to the agency.

Review the ESD 202 by: going over each question and resolving any unclear or missing information with the person being interviewed.

Advise the person of the household’s: Basic 3SVT facts Processing timeframes How benefits are issued Rights and responsibilities General Work Requirements 3SVT Time Limited Benefits Responsibility to report changes Verification needed to determine eligibility

Explain that time limits and requirements for other programs do not apply to 3SVT.

Provide the household with an ESD 202V, Verification Request, listing the required verifications needed to determine eligibility and the due date.

Advise the household that the verifications must be returned within ten-days of the interview.
heading: 200.3.3.3 Interview Process

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body:
Send Households who miss their scheduled interview an ESD 202D, NOMI via ACCESS. The NOMI tells the household: they missed their interview appointment, they are responsible to reschedule the interview appointment, and the application will be denied on the 30th day if the interview is not completed.

Note : If not already done, make two cold call attempts to the household to complete the interview prior to sending the NOMI.

Deny the application on the 30th day from the application date if the household misses their first interview and does not reschedule.

Deny the application on the next business day when the 30th day falls on a weekend or holiday.

Households that complete their interview within the 30-day application processing period must have their eligibility determined.
heading: 200.3.3.4 Missed Interview

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body:
Required verifications must be provided before approving the application.

Exception: Expedited Service .

See Individual items for verification requirements.
heading: 200.4 Verification

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body:
Deny households refusing to cooperate in completing the application process at the time of refusal. Refusal to cooperate means the household is able to cooperate but shows the ES they will not take required actions to complete the application process. Example Example A household telling you they will not be interviewed shows refusal to cooperate. A household not showing for their scheduled interview is not refusal to cooperate, and the application must not be denied for refusal.

Households are not found uncooperative when someone outside of the household fails to provide requested verifications.

Note : Disqualified and Ineligible Members are part of the household for this purpose.

See Disqualification for Refusal to Cooperate for information about cooperation with Quality Control.
heading: 200.5.1 Denial Procedures

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body: CATN the following information when a household has refused to cooperate: The action the household was asked to take, The reason the action is required to determine eligibility, The household is capable of completing the action, How the agency informed the household of the actions they need to take, and the dates they were informed, and The household has indicated verbally or in writing that it chooses not to take the action.
heading: 200.5.2 CATN Refusal

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body: Review the household’s: financial and non-financial eligibility requirements, data match information , and verifications.
heading: 200.6.1 Application Review Procedures

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body:
Process applications within ten days when all requirements have been met, but not later than 30-calendar days from the application date.

Exception : See Expedited Services, Processing Timeframe .
heading: 200.6.2 Processing Timeframes

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body:
Deny the application if the household has not returned the required verifications within ten days.

Send the household an ESD 220 advising them of the denial and that they may have their application reopened if they provide the required verifications by the 30th day from the application date. If no response at all to verification request additional wording is not needed. If partial verification is received, add additional wording to explain what is still needed. Example: xxxxxx was requested on x date and due by x date, as of the date of this notice the xxxxxx has not been received.

Reopen the application when verifications are provided by the 30th day from the application date. Determine eligibility and provide benefits from the application date, if eligible. Follow the process below when verifications are received 31 to 60-days from the application date.
heading: 200.6.2.1 Verifications Not Returned Within Ten Days

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body: Households eligible for the initial month but not eligible for future months receive benefits for the initial month only and then are denied.
heading: 200.6.3.1 Eligible Initial Month Only

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body: Households ineligible for the initial month but eligible for the next month, or subsequent months do not need to reapply. Begin the household’s certification period the month of application, but issue benefits beginning the month the household is eligible. Do not issue benefits for the initial month or ineligible months.
heading: 200.6.3.2 Ineligible for Initial Month Only

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body:
Review determinations of eligibility not completed within 30-days from the application date for the cause of the delay. CATN the reason for the delay.

ACCESS will create a "daily edit" on the 26th day asking for a reason for the delay in processing. ES must enter the reason in the STAT panel. ACCESS will default to an agency caused delay if ES doesn’t enter a reason in the STAT panel. ACCESS sends the household a 28-day notice advising the household of the delay and the reason.
heading: 200.6.4.1 Review Determinations of Eligibility Not Completed

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body:
Continue to pend the application until eligibility is determined. Do not deny the application when delayed due to the agency or SSA .

Determine eligibility as soon as possible, and if eligible, restore benefits from the application date.
heading: 200.6.4.2 Agency Caused Delays

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body: Delays are the fault of the household if the household failed to complete the application process and ES cannot take any further action.
heading: 200.6.4.3 Household Caused Delays

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body: Reopen the application when required verification is received after denial but 31 to 60-days from the application date. Issue benefits from the date the missing requirements were provided, if eligible. Benefits are not provided for the month of application when the household caused the delay. Households are not required to complete a new ESD 202.
heading: 200.6.4.4 Verification Received 31 to 60-Days from Application Date

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body:
Provide eligible households the opportunity to receive benefits as soon as possible, but no later than 30-calendar days from the application date.

Issue a combined benefit when the household: applies after the 15th of the month, and is found eligible for the initial month and the following month.
heading: 200.7.1 Approval

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body:
Deny the application as soon as possible, but no later than 30-calendar days from the application date for households found ineligible for benefits.

Households must file a new ESD 202 if they wish to receive benefits.

Exception : Verifications Not Returned Within Ten Days
heading: 200.7.2 Denial

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body: Provide the longest certification period possible based on the household’s situation. Assign a 36-month certification period to 3SquaresVT in a SNAP households where all household members are: Senior or disabled disabled , and A person who meets one or more of the following: Receives disability or blindness benefits from any of these programs: Social Security, SSI , or MABD . A person meets this even when the full benefit is withheld or deferred due to recoupment. Receives a federally or state administered SSI supplement based on disability or blindness, or section 212(a) of PL 93-66. Receives a disability retirement benefit from a government agency for a disability considered permanent by SSA disability considered permanent by SSA . Any of the following conditions: Permanent loss of use of both hands, both feet, or one hand and one foot. Amputation of leg at hip. Amputation of leg or foot because of diabetes or peripheral vascular diseases. Total deafness, not correctable by surgery or hearing aid. Statutory blindness, unless caused by cataracts or detached retina. IQ of 59 or less, established after the person becomes 16 years old. Spinal cord or nerve root lesions resulting in paraplegia or quadriplegia. Multiple sclerosis with damage to the nervous system caused by scattered areas of inflammation. The inflammation recurs and has progressed to varied interference with the function of the nervous system, including severe muscle weakness, paralysis, and vision and speech defects. Muscular dystrophy with irreversible wasting of the muscles, impairing the ability to use the arms or legs. Impaired renal function caused by chronic renal disease, resulting in severely reduced function which may require dialysis or kidney transplant. Amputation of a limb of a person at least 55 years old. Is a veteran the VA considers totally (100%) disabled or permanently housebound or in need of regular aid and attendance. Is a veteran’s surviving spouse who the VA considers: in need of regular aid and attendance, or permanently housebound, or approved for benefits because of the veteran’s death and has a disability considered permanent by SSA. Is a veteran’s surviving child who the VA considers: incapable of self-support, or approved for benefits because of the veteran’s death and has a disability considered permanent by SSA all household members have no Earned Income . Note : RUFA and EP households are not eligible for 3SquaresVT in a SNAP. Assign a 24-month certification period to households where all adult members are: Senior or disabled disabled , and A person who meets one or more of the following: Receives disability or blindness benefits from any of these programs: Social Security, SSI , or MABD . A person meets this even when the full benefit is withheld or deferred due to recoupment. Receives a federally or state administered SSI supplement based on disability or blindness, or section 212(a) of PL 93-66. Receives a disability retirement benefit from a government agency for a disability considered permanent by SSA disability considered permanent by SSA . Any of the following conditions: Permanent loss of use of both hands, both feet, or one hand and one foot. Amputation of leg at hip. Amputation of leg or foot because of diabetes or peripheral vascular diseases. Total deafness, not correctable by surgery or hearing aid. Statutory blindness, unless caused by cataracts or detached retina. IQ of 59 or less, established after the person becomes 16 years old. Spinal cord or nerve root lesions resulting in paraplegia or quadriplegia. Multiple sclerosis with damage to the nervous system caused by scattered areas of inflammation. The inflammation recurs and has progressed to varied interference with the function of the nervous system, including severe muscle weakness, paralysis, and vision and speech defects. Muscular dystrophy with irreversible wasting of the muscles, impairing the ability to use the arms or legs. Impaired renal function caused by chronic renal disease, resulting in severely reduced function which may require dialysis or kidney transplant. Amputation of a limb of a person at least 55 years old. Is a veteran the VA considers totally (100%) disabled or permanently housebound or in need of regular aid and attendance. Is a veteran’s surviving spouse who the VA considers: in need of regular aid and attendance, or permanently housebound, or approved for benefits because of the veteran’s death and has a disability considered permanent by SSA. Is a veteran’s surviving child who the VA considers: incapable of self-support, or approved for benefits because of the veteran’s death and has a disability considered permanent by SSA all household members have no Earned Income . Households who do not qualify for a 36-month or 24-month certification period receive a 12-month certification period. Do not change a certification period based solely on a household applying for another program .
heading: 200.8 Certification Periods

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body:
Provide an ESD 220 advising the household of their approval or denial for 3SVT benefits as soon as possible, but no later than 30-days after the application date .

Exception : Expedited Service, Processing Timeframes .
heading: 200.9.1 Determination Notice Procedures

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body: Approval notices must contain the following information: benefit amount, certification period, benefit level changes at certification, combined benefit amounts when appropriate, fair hearing rights, and contact name and number for questions.
heading: 200.9.2 Approval Notices

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body: Denial notices must contain: the reason the household was denied, their right to a fair hearing, their right to reapply, contact name and number for questions, actions the household must take to reactivate the application within 30-days of the notice date, if applicable, and a new application must be submitted if the household failed to complete an interview after two interview requests and made no further contact with the agency within 30-days of the application date.
heading: 200.9.3 Denial Notices

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body:
Provide benefits to households found eligible for 3SVT.

See Benefits for issuance information for: Initial Months , Combined Benefits , Suspended Benefits , and Prorated Benefits .
heading: 200.10 Benefits

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

body:
Release #25-6

Effective Date 12/15/2025

Legal Base Food and Nutrition Act of 2008, as amended. 7 CFR § 273.2 7 CFR § 273.10 Vermont Elderly Simplified Application Project, Approved Waivers.
heading: 200.12 Last Update

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 200 Application

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 300 Expedited Service

body:
New information in the manual is highlighted and noted with a . See Manual Updates and for details.

Section with expandable text are noted with a throughout the manual.
heading: 300 Expedited Service

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 300 Expedited Service

body: Expedited service allows households to quickly receive an initial benefit with fewer required verifications.
heading: 300.1 Overview

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 300 Expedited Service

body: Screen the ESD 202, Application , the day received. Use the Expedited Service Calculator .
heading: 300.2.1 Screening Procedures

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 300 Expedited Service

body:
Households must meet one of the following criteria to receive expedited service: Countable income for the month of application is less than $150 and liquid resources are not more than $100, or Combined countable income for the month of application , and liquid resources are less than the household’s monthly: shelter expenses including but not limited to rent, mortgage, lot rent, property taxes, home insurance, condo fees; and utility expenses using the appropriate utility standard, or Migrant Worker or Seasonal Farmworker households who: are Destitute , and have less than $100 in liquid resources.

Review PERS/D and/or INQD/D to see if the household is known to ACCESS and whether they receive or have received benefits in Vermont.
heading: 300.2.2 Expedited Eligibility

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 300 Expedited Service

body:
Households are not eligible for expedited service at recertification when they reapply before the end of the current certification period.

Households are not eligible for expedited service if they received benefits in another household or another state in the month of application. Exception : Households in shelters for battered persons can receive benefits in the same month. Please send to AOPS to review.

Households that reapply after failing to complete postponed requirements are not eligible for expedited service. See Reapplication after Expedited service for more information.

Process households who are not eligible for expedited service following normal 30-day application processing.
heading: 300.2.3 Households Not Eligible for Expedited Service

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 300 Expedited Service

body: Enter one of the following reason codes in the STAT/C/STAT panel to indicate the household’s determination for expedited service: Code G = Gross Income and Liquid Resources. Households with countable gross income less than $150, and their liquid resources are not more than $100, or Code S = Shelter Costs. Households countable income for the month of application and liquid resources less than the household’s monthly: shelter expenses including but not limited to rent, mortgage, lot rent, property taxes, homeowners insurance, condo fees; and utility expenses using the appropriate utility standard , or Code D = Destitute and Liquid Resources. Migrant or seasonal farmworker households who: are destitute, and have less than $100 in liquid resources Code N = Not eligible for expedited service. This includes households that did not complete the minimum action requirements by day 7.
heading: 300.2.4 Coding ACCESS

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 300 Expedited Service

body:
Households must complete the following minimum actions for expedited service: Complete an interview , and Verify identity

ESD must complete an eDRS on all adult household members prior to granting expedited service. See eDRS if an eDRS cannot be completed.

See Postponed Verification and Actions for actions the household must complete in order to continue benefits beyond the first or second month.
heading: 300.3.1 Minimum Actions Requirements

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 300 Expedited Service

body:
Households must complete an interview before benefits are issued. Interview the household the same day the application is received, if possible.

Interviews not completed the same day must be completed as soon as possible to meet expedited processing timeframes.
heading: 300.3.2 Interview

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 300 Expedited Service

body:
Two cold call attempts must be made to the household within 5 days to complete the interview when they are not at the office.

Note : If the customer does not provide a phone number CATN that cold calls were attempted but could not be completed because a phone number was not provided.

If you cannot reach the household when the application was received, send the ESD 202C, Benefits Interview Appointment Notice, advising the household to contact Economic Services to complete the interview within 5 days.
heading: 300.3.2.1 Telephone Interview

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 300 Expedited Service

body:
Send households who don’t complete an interview an ESD 202D, NOMI via ACCESS. The NOMI tells the household: they missed their interview appointment, they are responsible to reschedule the interview appointment, and their application will be denied on the 30th day if the interview is not completed.

Note: If cold calls were not previously completed, they must be completed prior to sending the NOMI.

Deny the application if the household fails to reschedule and complete the interview by the 30th day. Deny the application on the next business day when the 30th day falls on a weekend or holiday.
heading: 300.3.2.2 Interview Not Completed

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 300 Expedited Service

body:
Households qualifying for expedited service must verify the identity of the person applying if not already known to ACCESS. See Identity for requirements.

Note : Numident for persons with an SSN verifies identity.
heading: 300.3.3 Verification of Identity

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 300 Expedited Service

body:
Review Electronic Disqualified Recipient System (eDRS) on each adult household member to ensure they are not disqualified for IPV. If an eDRS match is returned and secondary verification is required, the household member cannot be approved until secondary verification is provided.

Exception : If an eDRS cannot be completed because the eDRS system is down, process without the eDRS. The eDRS must be completed once the system is running.
heading: 300.3.4 eDRS

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 300 Expedited Service

body:
Households lose their entitlement to expedited service if they fail to complete the minimum requirements within the seven-day timeframe. Process the application under normal 30-day timeframes.

Initial screening and two cold call attempts must be made to the household within 5 days prior to determining that the household failed to complete the minimum requirements.
heading: 300.3.5 Minimum Actions Not Completed

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 300 Expedited Service

body:
Agency failure occurs when ESD does not do the following: screen an initial application for expedited service accurately and timely, and/or complete cold calls within five days.

Households do not lose their entitlement to expedited service due to agency failure.

The date of discovery cannot be updated for a household in which agency failure has occurred.
heading: 300.3.6 Agency Failure

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 300 Expedited Service

body:
All 3SVT requirements must be completed before providing future months' benefits.

All verification other than identity can be postponed. .

Attempt to verify as many 3SVT eligibility factors as possible within the expedited service timeframes . However, do not delay benefits once the expedited service minimum actions are completed.

Enter verified information into ACCESS. Enter information that has not been verified into ACCESS using the customer's self-reported statement.
heading: 300.4.1 Verification Prioritization

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 300 Expedited Service

body:
Screen for general work requirement and time limited benefit exemptions during the interview. A customer who reports an exemption from the general work requirement and/or time limit can be processed expedited pending verification of the exemption. See Postponed verifications and Actions .

A customer who has used all countable months and does not report an exemption from the time limits is not eligible for expedited services.
heading: 300.4.2 General Work Requirement and Time Limited Benefit Exemption

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 300 Expedited Service

body:
Screen for eligible non-citizen criteria during the interview. A customer who reports eligible non-citizen criteria can be processed expedited pending verification of the eligibility criteria. See Postponed verifications and Actions .

A non-citizen who does not report meeting eligible non-citizen criteria is an ineligible member .
heading: 300.4.3 Eligible Non-Citizen

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 300 Expedited Service

body:
Screen for eligible student criteria during the interview. A customer who reports meeting eligible student criteria can be processed expedited pending verification of the eligibility criteria. See Postponed verifications and Actions .

A student who does not report meeting eligible student criteria is a non-member .
heading: 300.4.4 Student Eligibility

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 300 Expedited Service

body:
Required 3SVT actions and verifications not completed during expedited service processing must be completed to receive continued benefits.

All other actions and verifications that are not required for ongoing eligibility and not completed during expedited service processing will be removed from the case.
heading: 300.4.5 Postponed Verifications and Actions

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 300 Expedited Service

body:
Advise the household during the interview which actions and verifications are required to receive continued benefits. Explain to the household that future months' benefits will not be issued if the actions and verifications are not completed. Issue form ESD 202VCR, Verification Change Request, advising the household: which verification and requirements are needed, and the due date when the requirements must be completed to receive continued benefits.

See Examples of Accecptable Verification.
heading: 300.4.5.1 Notification for Required Postponed Verification

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 300 Expedited Service

body: Advise the household during the interview of all other actions and verifications needed. Explain to the household how their future benefit will be affected if they do not complete the requested actions and verifications. Issue form ESD 202VCR, Verification Change Request, advising the household of: the verification and information needed, and the date the verification and information must be provided by.
heading: 300.4.5.2 Notification for All Other Postponed Verification

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 300 Expedited Service

body:
Close the case if the household fails to complete the required postponed verifications and actions by the ESD 202VCR due date.

Remove all other information and verification that was not provided during expedited service processing and is not required for ongoing eligibility.

See Delayed Processing if the household completes the postponed requirements within 60 days of the date of application.
heading: 300.4.6 Household Fails to Complete Postponed Requirements

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 300 Expedited Service

body:
Expedited service applies for the month of application only. Exception : applications submitted after the 15th of the month are determined for combined benefits.

Do not delay issuing expedited benefits if the household has applied for other programs that are still pending.

Issue benefits for eligible households who have completed the minimum requirements the day of the interview, or the next work day, but no later than five days after the application date to ensure they can access benefits within seven days. The seven-day timeframe begins the day after the ESD 202 application date.

Send the household an ESD 220 to advise the household of the results of their determination of eligibility.
heading: 300.5.1 Issuance of Benefits

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 300 Expedited Service

body:
The date of discovery for applications screened as not meeting expedited service requirements that are later found eligible for expedited processing.

The date of discovery is entered into the 'EB Discovery Date' field on STAT/STAT.

The seven-day processing timeframe begins the day after the date of discovery.

The date of discovery cannot be updated for a household in which agency failure has occurred.
heading: 300.5.2.1 Application Date for Late Determination of Expedited Service Eligibility

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 300 Expedited Service

body:
Enter one of the following codes in the STAT/C/STAT panel when the expedited service application has not been completed within five days: Code V = Lacking verification of identity Code C = Could not contact the household or conduct the interview Code A = Agency Failure

Update the expedited code to 'N' on STAT/STAT when: two cold calls were completed within five days, and the household did not complete the minimum action requirements within the seven-day timeframe.
heading: 300.5.3 Coding ACCESS with Late Expedite Reason

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 300 Expedited Service

body: Provide the longest certification period possible based on the household’s situation. See Certification Periods .
heading: 300.6 Certification Periods

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 300 Expedited Service

body: Households who file their applications before the 15th of the month receive benefits for the initial month of eligibility.
heading: 300.7.1 Initial Benefit

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 300 Expedited Service

body: Applications filed after the 15th of the month receive a combined benefit.
heading: 300.7.2 Combined Benefit

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 300 Expedited Service

body:
Cash-Out households qualifying for expedited service receive their benefits by check; printed and mailed each night by Buildings and General Services.

See Benefits .
heading: 300.7.3 Cash Out

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 300 Expedited Service

body:
When a household that failed to complete postponed requirements reapplies for benefits the 30-day application processing standard applies.

Exception : If the household completes the postponed requirements , provide expedited service if the household is eligible for it.

Households can be approved for expedited service unlimited times as long as the household: completes the postponed requirements from the last expedited certification, or has been approved under normal 30-day timeframes since the last expedited certification.
heading: 300.8 Reapplication After Expedited Service

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 300 Expedited Service

body: Enter the expedited service determination in CATN at the time of the interview, including the expedited service criteria met by the household, and when the process is delayed due to: minimum actions not completed, or late determinations .
heading: 300.9 CATN

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 300 Expedited Service

body:
Release # 25-2

Effective Date: 07/08/2025

Legal Base Food and Nutrition Act of 2008, as amended. 7 CFR § 273.2(i) 7 CFR § 273.2(k)(1) 7 CFR § 273.10(e)(3) 7 CFR § 273.10(g)(i)(B) 7 CFR § 273.11(g)(4) 7 CFR § 273.13(b)(8) 7 CFR § 273.14(f) Vermont Elderly Simplified Application Project, Approved Waivers.
heading: 300.11 Last Update

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 300 Expedited Service

citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN)
heading: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN)

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN)

body:
New information in the manual is highlighted and noted with a . See Manual Updates and for details.

Section with expandable text are noted with a throughout the manual.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN) 1
heading: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN)

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN)

body:
Verification is used to confirm the accuracy of statements or information. Verification may be submitted by mail, fax, in-person or by telephone for collateral contact verifications.

Each item specifies the verification requirements at application, recertification, and reported changes.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN) 2
heading: 400.1.1 Overview

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN)

body:
The household has the primary responsibility to provide verification to support statements on the application and to resolve any questionable information.

If a household refuses to provide required verification or fails to respond to a request for verification without good cause, take the following actions: Applications: Deny for failure to cooperate; or approve benefits without the expense. Recipients: Close the case or adjust benefits as specified in the eligibility item that changed.

CATN why the action was taken.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN) 3
heading: 400.1.2 Household Responsibility

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN)

body: If the household reports difficulty in getting verification, or requests help to get the verification, the agency must offer to help.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN) 4
heading: 400.1.3 Agency Assistance

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN)

body:
Request verification in writing. You may discuss the verification needed with the household, but you must follow up in writing. In the written verification request, the due date is 10 days from the date the request is typed. If issued on a weekend, still use 10 days. Allow two full business days after the due date before determining non-cooperation. Use Form ESD 202VCR, Verification Change Request .

Avoid requesting unnecessary verification, rejecting applications, or closing cases when verification is already available from electronic sources, imaging, or has been submitted as a hard copy.

See Examples of Accecptable Verification.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN) 5
heading: 400.1.4 Request for Verification

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN)

body:
Written evidence

Collateral contacts

Home visits

Automated resources

Self-Declaration
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN) 6
heading: 400.2.1 Sources of Verification Include

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN)

body: The most common verification is written evidence of the household’s circumstances, such as wage stubs, rental agreements, award letters, etc.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN) 7
heading: 400.2.2 Written Evidence

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN)

body: A collateral contact may be used instead of written evidence. A collateral contact is an oral confirmation of the household’s circumstances by a person outside of the household. Make the collateral contact by telephone or in person with anyone who can be expected to give reliable information. Acceptable collateral contacts include, but are not limited to: Employers Landlords Dependent care providers Community Service Agencies
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN) 8
heading: 400.2.3 Collateral Contacts

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN)

body: Home visits are also an acceptable verification if written evidence or collateral contacts are not sufficient to make an eligibility determination. The home visit must always be scheduled in advance with the household. CATN why the home visit was completed.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN) 9
heading: 400.2.4 Home Visits

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN)

body:
Use automated resources for verification, whenever possible. However, Give the household the opportunity to provide other verification if they disagree with the automated information; and Do not delay timely authorization of benefits if automated resources are unavailable.

Automated matches are only used to obtain information on applicants, recipients, ineligible members and disqualified household members whose income and resources are needed to determine household eligibility.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN) 10
heading: 400.2.5 Automated Resources

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN)

body:
IEVS has the following matches using the applicant's or recipient's SSN to obtain income and resource information. Household members, including a disqualified or ineligible household member, are included in the match request. Vermont Department of Labor Quarterly wage, run monthly UCB , ran twice a month SSA BENDEX , run monthly RSDI benefits Medicare Part A and Part B payer and premium amount SDX SSI , run daily IRS 1099 income: Income reported to IRS that is not from wages such as, but not limited to: interest, dividends, cash prizes, royalties, pensions, annuities, contract income. The match is run monthly.

Review all information available from data exchanges within 10 days of notification of a match. Compare it to case record information and act on the information, as required.

Information provided by BENDEX, SDX, UCB, and SAVE is "verified upon receipt". Use this information without further verification unless the household disagrees with the information and provides alternate verification.

Quarterly wages and IRS information reported must be reviewed to determine if the income was unreported. If more information is needed, request verification from the household or third-party source. Allow at least 10 days for the household to return the information.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN) 11
heading: 400.2.5.1 Income Eligibility and Verification System (IEVS)

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN)

body:
eDRS is a nationwide database of persons disqualified for an Intentional Program Violation .

Complete a match at application and when a new adult member is added to the 3SVT household. Complete a match for each household member age 18 and under age 80.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN) 12
heading: 400.2.5.2 Electronic Disqualified Recipient System (EDRS)

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN)

body:
A monthly match from a national directory that identifies wage and income information.

Complete a match at application and recertification on members 18 years of age or older.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN) 13
heading: 400.2.5.3 National Directory of New Hire (NDNH)

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN)

body:
The deceased person match is completed through the automated match with SVES , BENDEX , and SDX .

If a match occurs, verification is required prior to removing the person from 3SVT benefits. The deceased person is not eligible for 3SVT.

Note: Close the case for the entire household when the head of household is deceased. Transfer the case into a surviving household member's name. Retain the original recertification date. Do not require a new application.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN) 14
heading: 400.2.5.4 Deceased Person Match

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN)

body:
PARIS is a quarterly match to verify income from: Veteran’s Administration Department of Defense – including active or retired military and Federal employees

Also verifies out-of-state participation in: Medicaid TANF Worker’s Compensation Child Care, and SNAP

If a match occurs, additional verification is required. Fraud and Claims Unit handles this.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN) 15
heading: 400.2.5.5 PARIS

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN)

body:
PVS is completed manually by the Benefit Programs Specialist through INFC/D/SVES. The incarcerated individual will not be eligible for 3SVT if they are incarcerated for longer than 30 days.

If a match occurs, additional verification is required.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN) 16
heading: 400.2.5.6 Prisoner Verification System (PVS)

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN)

body:
SAVE is a program that verifies the validity of non-citizen immigrant documents. After the USCIS documentation is received, use the SAVE system to verify the documentation is accurate.

Exception : American Indians may not be listed on SAVE.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN) 17
heading: 400.2.5.7 Systematic Alien Verification for Entitlements (SAVE)

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN)

body:
The DOC match occurs when DOC notifies DVHA that an individual has been incarcerated for 28 days. DVHA notifies ESD by entering information into ACCESS.

If a match is received, additional verification is required.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN) 18
heading: 400.2.5.8 Department of Corrections (DOC) Match via Department of Vermont Health ACCESS (DVHA)

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN)

body:
Oral or written confirmation of the household’s circumstances from an individual in the household.

Self-declaration of required verification should be the last resort and should only be accepted after all other options have been attempted.

Information typically requiring verification from another source can be taken self declared after the household and ESD have attempted to gather the information. This includes: Income Deductio ns Questionable Information

Note: Self-Employment income and expenses can be taken self declared when taxes are not available or completed for the business.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN) 19
heading: 400.2.6 Self-Declaration

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN)

body:
The goal of case notes is to establish a clear and complete record that follows the case actions. Complete case notes CATN to support eligibility, ineligibility, and benefit level determinations.

Provide a clear explanation in CATN of what you did and why you did it, including: Information used to determine eligibility and benefit amount including: the source of information; the verification that supports the information; the method used to determine monthly income and expense amounts such as conversion, averaging, etc.; and why the information was used. Actions taken and the reasons to support those actions, such as, but not limited to: Differences: In order of the STAT, the resolution of any differences between information provided at the interview and the ESD 202, Application. Questionable Information: Why is it questionable and how was it resolved? Collateral Contacts: who the collateral contact is, the telephone number, date of the contact, and the information the collateral contact provided. Interviews: who was interviewed, when the interview was completed, how completed (in-person or telephone).
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN) 20
heading: 400.3 Case Notes

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 400 Verification and Case Notes (CATN)

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
