metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 500 Household Composition

body:
New information in the manual is highlighted and noted with a . See Manual Updates and for details.

Section with expandable text are noted with a throughout the manual.
heading: 500 Household Composition

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 500 Household Composition

body:
To determine who is non-financially eligible for 3SVT you must : Start with everyone who lives together. Determine who purchases and prepares (shares food) together for home consumption. Apply relationship rules. Apply non-financial eligibility rules to determine who is an eligible or excluded member. No one can be an eligible member of two or more households in any month.

Exception : Residents of Shelters for Battered Persons provided, the battered person was a member of the same household as the person who abused him or her.
heading: 500.1 Eligible member Determination

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 500 Household Composition

body:
Evaluate everyone living together to determine who must be included in the 3SVT household.

Living together means sharing a home where people usually sleep and share any common living spaces such as kitchen, bathroom, bedroom or living room. People who share only an access area such as an entrance or hallway or non-living areas such as a laundry room or garage are not considered living together.

An individual spending any amount of time in the household can be considered living in the household.
heading: 500.2.1 Determination

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 500 Household Composition

body:
A person who is temporarily absent from the household is considered living with the household.

A person’s absence is temporary if all the following are true: the person’s location is known, the person lived with the household before the absence (consider newborns to have lived with the household), there is a definite plan for return, and the absence has lasted or is expected to last 30 days or less.

Note : People that maintain Vermont residency and vacation or temporarily live out of state are still considered as living together. See Residency .
heading: 500.2.2 Temporary Absence

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 500 Household Composition

body:
People who live together and share food must be included in the 3SVT household. People share food if they: share in the cost of buying food, and/or share in preparing the food, regardless of who paid for it, and/or eat from the same food supply, regardless of who paid for it.

Note : A disabled person who is unable to purchase and prepare their own food and has arranged to have their food purchased and prepared separately is not considered as sharing food. They can have separate 3SVT status from others in the household.

Types of households are: A person living alone. A group of people living together who share food. A person (or group of people) living with others, but who do not share food with the others.

Note : The intent to purchase food and prepare meals separately also establishes separate household status, even if the intent depends on receiving 3SVT.
heading: 500.3.1 3SVT Household Determination

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 500 Household Composition

body:
A person and spouse may be a separate household even if they purchase and prepare with others if all the following are true: one of them is 60 years or older; and that person cannot purchase and prepare meals because of: a disability the SSA considers permanent, or another permanent physical or mental non-disease related disability; and the gross monthly income for the number of others in the household does not exceed 165% of the federal poverty level 165% of the federal poverty level . Income Limits and Maximum Benefit 3SVT Household Size Expanded Categorical Eligibility ( ECE ) Senior & Disabled Seeking Separate Household Status Income Change Reporting Threshold And Gross Income Limit For Non- SCE or ECE Eligible Net Income Limit For Non- SCE or ECE eligible Maximum Monthly Benefit 3SVT House-hold Size Gross Monthly Income Limit (185% FPL ) Gross Monthly Income Limit (165% FPL) Gross Monthly Income Limit (130% FPL) Net Monthly Income Limit (100% FPL) Thrifty Food Plan 1 $2,413 $2,152 $1,696 $1,305 $298 2 $3,261 $2,909 $2,292 $1,763 $546 3 $4,109 $3,665 $2,888 $2,221 $785 4 $4,957 $4,421 $3,483 $2,680 $994 5 $5,805 $5,177 $4,079 $3,138 $1,183 6 $6,653 $5,934 $4,675 $3,596 $1,421 7 $7,501 $6,690 $5,271 $4,055 $1,571 8 $8,349 $7,446 $5,867 $4,513 $1,789 9 $9,197 $8,203 $6,463 $4,972 $2,007 10 $10,044 $8,960 $7,059 $5,431 $2,225 Each Additional Member +$848 +$757 +$596 +$459 +$218

Note : People in the specified relationships (see below) must be included in the 3SVT household.
heading: 500.3.2 Senior and Unable to Purchase/Prepare Food

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 500 Household Composition

body: People in the specified relationships must be included in the 3SVT household
heading: 500.4.1 Inclusion in 3SVT Household

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 500 Household Composition

body:
People who are legally married and living together must be in the same household.

Note : Couples who are in a civil union are not spouses.
heading: 500.4.2 Spouses

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 500 Household Composition

body:
Children include natural, step and adopted children.

Parents and their children 21 years of age and younger who live together must be in the same household even if the child(ren) have their own spouse or child who lives in the same household.

Children living in the home any amount of time can be screened for benefits.

Note : Children residing in a household less than half time are not mandatory household members but can be included at the household’s request. Children residing in a household half time or more are mandatory members if not on 3SVT in another household. See Joint or Shared Physical Custody below.

Note : The step-parent relationship ends with the death of or divorce from the parent. The Natural Parent relationship ends with the adoption of the child.

Exception : If a child, biological parent and guardian of the child all live in the same household, then the 3SVT should be with the guardian unless the guardian is not seeking 3SVT for the child.
heading: 500.4.3 Parents and Children

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 500 Household Composition

body:
A child may only be a member of one 3SquaresVT household. The first household to apply may include the child regardless of the percentage of custody. If a child spends an equal amount of time in each household, the custodians decide which household can claim the child. If the custodians cannot decide, include the child in the household that applies first.

Transfer the child to the other household if: the parent dies, or the second household, after applying for benefits, provides verification that the child primarily resides with them.

Note : A child residing in the home 50% or more of the time and who is not on 3SVT in another household must be considered a mandatory household member.
heading: 500.4.3.1 Joint or Shared Physical Custody

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 500 Household Composition

body:
A minor is a person under 18 years of age. A minor under the parental control of an adult must be in the same household as the adult. Parental control means the minor is dependent on the adult for financial or other support.

Exceptions: Foster care providers and their foster children, see Foster Children and Adults . A minor living with his or her own spouse or child and with an adult who is not the minor’s parent. The minor and spouse/children can be a separate household if they do not purchase and prepare food with others.
heading: 500.4.4 Minor Under Parental Control

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 500 Household Composition

body:
The household may choose to include or exclude a foster child or foster adult who lives with them. If excluded the foster care payment is not income to the household. If excluded, the foster child or foster adult is not eligible as a separate group.

If the household excludes the foster child and the foster child spends any amount of time with their biological parent, then the biological parent can apply for 3SVT for the child.
heading: 500.4.5 Foster Children and Adults

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 500 Household Composition

body:
The household may choose to include or exclude the DCP recipient that lives with them and for whom they are providing care. If excluded, the DCP recipient is not eligible as a separate group.

See Difficulty of Care Program (DCP) Payments for information about how to handle the DCP payments.
heading: 500.4.6 Difficulty of Care Program (DCP) Recipients

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 500 Household Composition

body:
There are three types of persons excluded from the household: Disqualified members Ineligible members Non-members

Sometimes entire households are ineligible for program benefits.
heading: 500.5.1 Types of Persons Excluded in Households

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 500 Household Composition

body: These are people who would be household members but are not allowed to be. Their resources, income and deductions are counted as if they were eligible members, see resources and income of Disqualified Members . Criminal Justice Issues - Persons who are fleeing felons. Persons who are probation/parole violators. See Fleeing Felons and Probation/Parole Violators . IPV - Person who signed an IPV hearing waiver, or who was found guilty of an IPV by an administrative disqualification hearing or court of law. See Intentional Program Violations . Quality Control Non-cooperation - Households that do not cooperate with either state or federal quality control reviewers. See Quality Control . Work Requirements- Persons who failed to meet Work Requirements .
heading: 500.5.2 Disqualified Members

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 500 Household Composition

body: These are people who would be household members, but they did not meet one of the non-financial eligibility requirements in the sections noted below. Resources are counted as if they were eligible; income and deductions are prorated. See Special Household Situations, Ineligible Members . Non-Citizens - Persons who failed to meet eligibility rules in Citizen/Non-Citizen . SSN - Persons who failed to provide or apply for an SSN as per Social Security Number . Time Limited Benefits - Persons who are not meeting the work requirement and who have used-up their 3-month eligibility. See 3SquaresVT Time Limited Benefits .
heading: 500.5.3 Ineligible Members

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 500 Household Composition

body: Certain persons who live with and may even share food with the household are treated as non-members for purposes of determining eligibility and benefits. Non-members’ income and resources are not included when determining eligibility and benefits for the household.
heading: 500.5.4 Non-members

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 500 Household Composition

body:
A boarder is anyone (or group) who resides with a household and pays reasonable compensation for lodging and meals. The household may choose to include or exclude a boarder (or boarder group) that pays reasonable compensation. If excluded, the boarder (or group) is not eligible for 3SVT as a separate household.

Reasonable compensation exists when the total paid by the boarder (or group) is equal to: The maximum benefit for the boarder (group) size when the boarder (group) pays for more than 2 meals per day. 2/3 of the maximum benefit for the boarder (group) size when the boarder (group) pays for 2 meals or less per day.

See Reasonable Room and Board Compensation Reasonable Room and Board Compensation Reasonable Room and Board Compensation B oarder Group Size More than Two Meals per Day Two meals or Less per Day 1 $552 $453 2 $1011 $829 3 $1454 $1192 4 $1,841 $1510 5 $2,191 $1797 6+ $2,632 $2158

Spouses, and parents and children under 22 are not treated as boarders even if a board payment is being made.

Persons paying less than reasonable compensation are not boarders, they are household members.
heading: 500.5.4.1 Boarders

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 500 Household Composition

body:
Live-in attendants live with the household to provide medical, housekeeping, dependent care or other similar personal services. Live-in attendants may be eligible for 3SVT as a separate household.

Spouses, and parents and children under 22 are not treated as live-in attendants even if they are providing these types of services.

Note : A spouse may be an essential person for purposes of the Essential Persons Program. See Essential Person Program Payments for treatment of the income.
heading: 500.5.4.2 Live-In Attendants

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 500 Household Composition

body:
People who reside in a commercial boarding house are not eligible for 3SVT.

A commercial boarding house is a business that offers meals and lodging for payment with the intent of making a profit. Determine intent to make a profit from a statement by the applicant or the owner.
heading: 500.5.4.3 Residents of Commercial Boarding Houses

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 500 Household Composition

body:
People who receive over 50% of three meals daily from an institution (college and reside in campus housing, nursing home, etc.) where they live, are residents of institutions. Residents of institutions are not eligible for 3SVT.

Note: This rule does not apply to people who receive less than 50% of three meals daily.

Exception : Residents of institutions described in Special Household Situations .
heading: 500.5.4.4 Residents of Institutions

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 500 Household Composition

body:
People who pay the household a flat-fee for lodging. They do not pay a separate amount for utilities. They do not purchase and prepare food with the household they rent from. Roomers may be eligible for 3SVT as a separate household.

Spouses, and parents and children under 22 are not treated as roomers even if they pay each other for lodging.

Note : Many separate households live together and share shelter expenses. This is not a roomer situation. Click for Shared Households definition. Examples: Shared Households vs Roomers .
heading: 500.5.4.5 Roomers

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 500 Household Composition

body: A household with a striking member(s) may or may not be eligible for benefits, see Strikers .
heading: 500.5.4.6 Strikers

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 500 Household Composition

body: Students in higher education who do not meet the rules in Students are not eligible for 3SVT.
heading: 500.5.4.7 Students

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 500 Household Composition

body: People who receive more than 50% of their meals while living in the following institutions may be eligible if they meet program requirements.
heading: 500.6.1 Institutional Eligibility

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 500 Household Composition

body:
A public or private nonprofit residential facility for the treatment of drug addicts or alcoholics. The children of a resident (but not the spouse) who lives with them in the center are part of the resident’s household.

See Addiction Treatment Centers for special requirements.
heading: 500.6.2 Addiction Treatment Centers

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 500 Household Composition

body:
A public or nonprofit residence that provides housing for no more than 16 disabled persons. The resident must be Disabled Disabled to be eligible for 3SVT.

See Group Living for the Disabled for special requirements.
heading: 500.6.3 Group Living for the Disabled

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 500 Household Composition

body:
A public or private nonprofit facility that provides temporary housing for homeless people. The household is defined as a single person or a person and spouse and children .

See Homeless Shelters and Meal Providers for special requirements.
heading: 500.6.4 Homeless Shelters

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 500 Household Composition

body:
A public or private nonprofit residential facility that provides temporary housing for battered persons. The household is the battered person and his/her children.

See Shelters for Battered Persons for special requirements.
heading: 500.6.5 Shelters for Battered Persons

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 500 Household Composition

body: Federally subsidized housing for seniors built under either Section 202 of the Housing Act of 1959 or Section 236 of the National Housing Act. The household is the senior and spouse. Determine eligibility as for any other household.
heading: 500.6.6 Subsidized Housing for Seniors

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 500 Household Composition

body: Verify if questionable.
heading: 500.7.1 Basic Household Composition

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 500 Household Composition

body: Verification requirements are in the specified item. If no item is specified, verify if questionable.
heading: 500.7.2 Excluded Persons

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 500 Household Composition

body: In a shared custody situation, if the non-assigned household applies for benefits and wants to claim the child as a member, it must verify the child primarily resides with it.
heading: 500.7.3 Primary Residence to Transfer a Shared-Custody Child

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 500 Household Composition

body:
Release # 25-2

Effective Date: 07/08/2025

Legal Base Food and Nutrition Act of 2008, as amended. 7 CFR § 273.1
heading: 500.9 Last Update

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 500 Household Composition

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 600 Residency

body:
New information in the manual is highlighted and noted with a . See Manual Updates and for details.

Section with expandable text are noted with a throughout the manual.
heading: 600 Residency

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 600 Residency

body:
The household must live in Vermont when applying for 3SVT. People living in Vermont for any reason meet the residency requirement, regardless of the length of time they have been here or plan to stay. Exception : People on vacation in Vermont do not meet the residency requirement.

Note : People that maintain Vermont residency and vacation or temporarily live out of state still meet the residency requirement.

When a person moves to Vermont from another state and reports SNAP closure in the previous 30 days, verify the effective closure date. Contact the other state using the national directory . Exception : If the person is currently a resident in a Vermont battered persons shelter , duplicate benefits may be issued.

The household is not required to have a permanent dwelling or fixed residence. For example, a person still meets the residency requirement if the person lives in a car, camper, tent, or Homeless Shelter .

Note : Ask the household to provide a mailing address where notices can be mailed. The household may use any address as long as there is no threat of mail being lost or stolen. For example, the address can be a friend or relative’s address or a post office box.
heading: 600.1 Overview

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 600 Residency

body: Verify Vermont residency if questionable. Accept any document or collateral contact that reasonably proves the person’s residency.
heading: 600.2.1 Guidelines to Verify Vermont Residency

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 600 Residency

body:
Deny the application if not verified by the due date.

Postpone verification for expedited service month(s), unless verification can be provided in time to meet the seven-day standard of promptness.
heading: 600.2.2 Application

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 600 Residency

body:
Release # 24-1

Effective Date: 04/15/2024

Legal Base Food and Nutrition Act of 2008, as amended. 7 CFR § 273.1
heading: 600.4 Last Update

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 700 Identity

body:
New information in the manual is highlighted and noted with a . See Manual Updates and for details.

Section with expandable text are noted with a throughout the manual.
heading: 700 Identity

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 700 Identity

body:
Verify the identity of the person applying.

Verify the identity of an AR applying on behalf of a household and the Head of Household .

Identity is the only mandatory verification when providing Expedited Service . See Expedited Service, Verification of Identity .
heading: 700.1 Overview

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 700 Identity

body: Verify identity using readily available documentary evidence, or if unavailable, through a collateral contact. Accept any documents which establish the applicant or authorized representative’s identity. Note: Numident for persons with an SSN verifies identity. See Examples of Accecptable Verification.
heading: 700.2.1 Documentation

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 700 Identity

body:
Deny the application if not verified by the due date.

Verify identity before providing expedited service.
heading: 700.2.2 Application

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 700 Identity

body:
Release # 26-1

Effective Date: 04/17/2026

Legal Base Food and Nutrition Act of 2008, as amended. 7 CFR § 273.2(f)(1)(vii)
heading: 700.4 Last Update

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 700 Identity

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 800 Citizen/Non-Citizen

body:
New information in the manual is highlighted and noted with a . See Manual Updates and for details.

Section with expandable text are noted with a throughout the manual.
heading: 800 Citizen/Non-Citizen

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 800 Citizen/Non-Citizen

body:
A non-citizen is anyone living in the United States who does not meet the Citizen requirements. Citizenship/non-citizenship status is attested to for each member of the household when the citizen question is completed on the 3SVT form.

A non-citizen must be an eligible non-citizen to receive 3SVT benefits. If members do not meet the eligible non-citizen criteria, they are ineligible members. See Ineligible Members .
heading: 800.1 Overview

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 800 Citizen/Non-Citizen

body: People who are United States citizens are eligible for food assistance.
heading: 800.2.1 Eligibility

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 800 Citizen/Non-Citizen

body: United States citizens are people born in one of the 50 states, the District of Columbia, Puerto Rico, Guam, the U.S. Virgin Islands, or the Northern Mariana Islands. Non-citizen nationals born in American Samoa or Swain’s Island are also U.S. citizens.
heading: 800.2.2 Natural Born Citizens

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 800 Citizen/Non-Citizen

body: Naturalized Citizen s are United States citizens. Children of naturalized citizens are eligible if the child was; under age 18 when the parent was naturalized on or after 10/14/1940 or under age 21 when the parent was naturalized before 10/14/1940.
heading: 800.2.3 Naturalized Citizens

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 800 Citizen/Non-Citizen

body:
Automatic U.S. citizenship is granted to children born in a foreign country upon entry to the U.S. if the child is: under 18 years of age, admitted to the U.S. as a lawful permanent resident, and in the legal and physical custody (as a natural or adopted child) of at least one parent who is a U.S. citizen.

Note: The adoption must be finalized prior to the child entering the U.S.
heading: 800.2.4 Natural or Adopted Children of a Citizen

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 800 Citizen/Non-Citizen

body:
A non-citizen must meet the following criteria for eligible member status. A non-citizen may be lawfully present in the United States but is not an eligible member unless one of the eligible non-citizen criteria is met. See Non-immigrant .

Eligible Non-Citizen Eligibility Table:

Non-Citizen Status Eligible If Lawful Permanent Resident Disabled Disabled , or A person who meets one or more of the following: Receives disability or blindness benefits from any of these programs: Social Security, SSI , or MABD . A person meets this even when the full benefit is withheld or deferred due to recoupment. Receives a federally or state administered SSI supplement based on disability or blindness, or section 212(a) of PL 93-66. Receives a disability retirement benefit from a government agency for a disability considered permanent by SSA disability considered permanent by SSA . Any of the following conditions: Permanent loss of use of both hands, both feet, or one hand and one foot. Amputation of leg at hip. Amputation of leg or foot because of diabetes or peripheral vascular diseases. Total deafness, not correctable by surgery or hearing aid. Statutory blindness, unless caused by cataracts or detached retina. IQ of 59 or less, established after the person becomes 16 years old. Spinal cord or nerve root lesions resulting in paraplegia or quadriplegia. Multiple sclerosis with damage to the nervous system caused by scattered areas of inflammation. The inflammation recurs and has progressed to varied interference with the function of the nervous system, including severe muscle weakness, paralysis, and vision and speech defects. Muscular dystrophy with irreversible wasting of the muscles, impairing the ability to use the arms or legs. Impaired renal function caused by chronic renal disease, resulting in severely reduced function which may require dialysis or kidney transplant. Amputation of a limb of a person at least 55 years old. Is a veteran the VA considers totally (100%) disabled or permanently housebound or in need of regular aid and attendance. Is a veteran’s surviving spouse who the VA considers: in need of regular aid and attendance, or permanently housebound, or approved for benefits because of the veteran’s death and has a disability considered permanent by SSA. Is a veteran’s surviving child who the VA considers: incapable of self-support, or approved for benefits because of the veteran’s death and has a disability considered permanent by SSA under age 18, or meets Work Quarter Eligibility Work Quarter Eligibility ,or Non-citizens who have 40 qualifying work quarters are eligible. There is not a time requirement for residency. A qualifying work quarter includes one worked by: The non-citizen A parent of a non-citizen before the non-citizen reached their 18 th birthday, including quarters worked before the non-citizen was born. A spouse of the non-citizen during their marriage as long as the non-citizen remains married to the spouse or the spouse is deceased. Note: Each spouse can claim the quarters of the other spouse, and the children can claim the quarters worked by their parents. Exception: A person can earn only one quarter per quarter. Therefore, if both spouses or both parents worked the same quarter, it would only count as one quarter. Beginning 1/1/97, a quarter in which the non-citizen received Federal means-tested assistance Federal means-tested assistance is not counted as a qualifying work quarter. Federal programs which have limits on resources and/or income of applicants/recipients. Examples include, but are not limited to: Medicaid TANF (Reach Up, Reach Ahead) Dr. Dynasaur (children’s health program) Supplemental Security Income (SSI) Exception: Federal means-tested public assistance programs do not include the following when crediting a qualifying work quarter to a non-citizen: Emergency medical assistance Short-term, non-cash, in-kind emergency disaster relief National School Lunch Program Public health assistance for immunizations or communicable diseases not paid by Medicaid Foster care and adoption assistance Student educational assistance Mean-tested program under the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 Head Start Act Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (WIOA) Count both qualifying quarters of work covered and not covered by Title II of the Social Security Act. meets Military Requirement Military Requirement Non-citizens identified in this chart who meet one of these criteria are eligible , or has lived in the US as a qualified non-citizen for 5 years from the date of entry, or a legal resident on August 22, 1996, and born before August 22, 1931, or Admitted to the United States as: Afghan Immigrant paroled into the U.S. between 07/31/2021 and 09/30/2023. Afghan Special Immigrant (SIV) Amerasian Asylee Canadian Born Native American Compacts of Free Association (COFA) Citizens Cuban Entrant Deportation or Removal Withheld Haitian Entrant Hmong or Highland Laotian Tribal Member Iraqi Special Immigrant (SIV) Native Americans with Treaty Rights Refugee Trafficking Victim Ukrainian nationals, citizens, or those who last habitually lived in Ukraine who are granted parole between 02/24/2022 and 09/30/2024. Afghan Special Immigrant (SIV) Amerasian Compacts of Free Association (COFA) Citizens Cuban Entrant Haitian Entrant Iraqi Special Immigrant (SIV) Iraqi Special Immigrant (SIV) Native Americans with Treaty Rights Eligible Note : These non-citizens retain their eligibility without having to meet the above requirements if/when converted to Lawful Permanent Residence status. Note: SIV holders are granted Lawful Permanent Residence status upon entry into the U.S.
heading: 800.3.1 Criteria to Determine Eligibility

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 800 Citizen/Non-Citizen

body: Click on the terms in the chart above for definitions of non-citizens.
heading: 800.3.2 Non-Citizen Status Defined

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 800 Citizen/Non-Citizen

body:
Non-citizens identified in the chart above who meet one of these criteria are eligible.

Status Criteria Military Active Duty On active duty with the U.S. Armed Forces (excluding National Guard or Army Reserve) for reasons other than training. Includes the active duty person’s spouse and dependent children aged 21 or under. Veteran A veteran of the U.S. Armed Forces who was honorably discharged for reasons not related to non-citizen status. Includes: Spouse of the veteran Un-remarried surviving spouses Unmarried dependent children under the ago of 18 (or age 22 if a full-time student) Unmarried children under the age of 18 (or age 22 if a full-time student) who was dependent on the veteran at the time of the member's death.
heading: 800.3.3 Military Requirement

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 800 Citizen/Non-Citizen

body: Non-citizens who have 40 qualifying work quarters are eligible. There is not a time requirement for residency. A qualifying work quarter includes one worked by: The non-citizen A parent of a non-citizen before the non-citizen reached their 18 th birthday, including quarters worked before the non-citizen was born. A spouse of the non-citizen during their marriage as long as the non-citizen remains married to the spouse or the spouse is deceased. Note: Each spouse can claim the quarters of the other spouse, and the children can claim the quarters worked by their parents. Exception: A person can earn only one quarter per quarter. Therefore, if both spouses or both parents worked the same quarter, it would only count as one quarter. Beginning 1/1/97, a quarter in which the non-citizen received Federal means-tested assistance Federal means-tested assistance is not counted as a qualifying work quarter. Federal programs which have limits on resources and/or income of applicants/recipients. Examples include, but are not limited to: Medicaid TANF (Reach Up, Reach Ahead) Dr. Dynasaur (children’s health program) Supplemental Security Income (SSI) Exception: Federal means-tested public assistance programs do not include the following when crediting a qualifying work quarter to a non-citizen: Emergency medical assistance Short-term, non-cash, in-kind emergency disaster relief National School Lunch Program Public health assistance for immunizations or communicable diseases not paid by Medicaid Foster care and adoption assistance Student educational assistance Mean-tested program under the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 Head Start Act Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (WIOA) Count both qualifying quarters of work covered and not covered by Title II of the Social Security Act.
heading: 800.3.4 Work Quarter Eligibility

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 800 Citizen/Non-Citizen

body:
Non-citizens who are not identified as eligible non-citizens are ineligible non-citizens for 3SVT benefits.

If a person indicates inability or unwillingness to attest to the non-citizen status for any household member, the person whose non-citizen status is in question is an ineligible (not illegal) non-citizen. If the person is determined ineligible, no further action to obtain documentation of their status is allowed. The household is required to answer questions and submit verifications about the ineligible member’s income, resources, etc. per Ineligible Members .

Ineligible Non-Citizen Table:

Ineligible Non-Citizen Status Eligibility Status Afghan Immigrant paroled into the U.S. between 07/31/2021 and 09/30/2023 Asylee Deportation or Removal Withheld Refugee Trafficking Victim Ukrainian nationals, citizens or those who last habitually lived in Ukraine who are granted parole between 02/24/2022 and 09/30/2024. Note: Unless Lawful Permanent Resident If LPR eligible immediately. Battered Non-Citizen Conditional Entrant Parolee Note: Unless Lawful Permanent Resident . If LPR then must meet eligible if criteria.
heading: 800.4.1 Determination of Eligibility

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 800 Citizen/Non-Citizen

body:
Report known illegal non-citizens who apply for 3SVT benefits to the USCIS . “Illegal status” is known only when a formal determination, subject to administrative review, was completed and is supported by the Department of Homeland Security or the Executive Office for Immigration Review, such as a Final Order of Deportation.

If there is not a formal determination, do not report the non-citizen as an illegal non-citizen. The non-citizen is ineligible for 3SVT benefits.
heading: 800.4.2 Reporting Known Illegal Non-Citizen

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 800 Citizen/Non-Citizen

body: Non-citizens may have been granted a permanent resident status to enter and reside in the United States because a Sponsor has signed an agreement to guarantee financial support of the non-citizen. Counting the sponsor’s income and resources toward the sponsored non-citizen is called deeming. Deeming may be required for non-citizens who had a sponsor sign the Affidavit of Support , Form I-864 December 19, 1997 or later. The I-864 form is also known as a 213A affidavit.
heading: 800.5.1 Sponsor Deeming Definition

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 800 Citizen/Non-Citizen

body: Some non-citizens are exempt from sponsor deeming even if an Affidavit of Support was signed on their behalf. Non-citizens exempt from sponsor deeming are: Children under age 18. Refugee, parolee, asylee, Cuban or Haitian entrant, persons granted withholding of deportation, Amerasian, trafficking victims, and Afghan/Iraqi special immigrants. Non-citizens sponsored by organizations or groups. Non-citizen is residing in the same house as the sponsor because they are one 3SVT household. Non-citizens whose sponsor signed the Affidavit of Support, Form I-134, prior to 12/19/1997. Lawful permanent residents credited with 40 qualifying work quarters. See Work Quarter Eligibility . Ineligible non-citizens. Battered non-citizen, parent of a battered child, or spouse of a battered non-citizen for 12 months. At the end of 12 months, the exemption continues if a court or USCIS determines the battering is substantially connected to the need for benefits. The non-citizen cannot live with the batterer during the exemption period. Indigent non-citizens: A non-citizen is indigent if the total of the non-citizen’s income and any cash assistance or in-kind support provided by the sponsor is less than 130% of the federal poverty level. If the non-citizen is eligible for 3SVT because his total income is under 130%, sponsor deeming is not required unless the non-citizen chooses to opt out of indigence determined and be subject to regular sponsor deeming rules. The indigence exclusion is for one year and then must be re-evaluated. Written notification of indigence determinations must be sent to the USCIS, Office of Policy and Strategy, 20 Massachusetts Avenue, N.W., Washington DE 20529. The notification must contain the name of the sponsored non-citizen and of the sponsor. It should reference “Determination under 421(e) of the Personal Responsibility and Work Opportunity Reconciliation Act of 1996”.
heading: 800.5.2 Non-Citizen Exempt from Sponsor Deeming

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 800 Citizen/Non-Citizen

body:
Deem a portion of the sponsor’s income and resources to the non-citizen, regardless of whether the income or resources are paid to the non-citizen. If the sponsor is living with a spouse, the spouse’s income and resources are also deemed to the non-citizen. More than one person may sponsor a non-citizen by signing an Affidavit for Support. If there are joint sponsors, count the deemed income and resources of all sponsors for the non-citizen.

It is the non-citizen’s responsibility to provide information or documentation about the sponsor(s) and to obtain the cooperation from the sponsor. Information must be provided at application and recertification. Allow the non-citizen at least 10 days to provide the information up to the 30th day for application processing. If it is not provided within that time frame, consider the sponsored non-citizens as ineligible non-citizens and determine eligibility and benefits for the remaining members.
heading: 800.5.3.1 Sponsor Deeming Procedures

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 800 Citizen/Non-Citizen

body: The deemed resource amount is determined as follows: Add allowable resources Subtract $1,500 Divide by the number of non-citizens sponsored. If the number is not known, the entire amount counts The result is the deemed resource amount per sponsored member No resources are deemed to children under age 18
heading: 800.5.3.2 Deeming of Sponsor’s Resources

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 800 Citizen/Non-Citizen

body:
The deemed income amount is determined as follows: Add earned income Subtract 20% of the earned income Add unearned income Subtract the gross monthly income limit for the sponsor’s household size and any other person who is claimed or could be claimed as a dependent for federal income tax purposes Divide by the number of non-citizens sponsored. If the number is not known, the entire amount counts The result is the deemed income amount per sponsored member. No income is deemed to children under age 18

Money actually paid to the non-citizen by a sponsor is not income to the non-citizen unless the amount paid exceeds the deemed amount. Consider any excess paid as an unearned income contribution to the non-citizen.
heading: 800.5.3.3 Deeming of Sponsor’s Income

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 800 Citizen/Non-Citizen

body: Deeming of income and resources ends when: The non-citizen becomes exempt from deeming requirements ; The non-citizen gains U.S. citizenship; or The sponsor’s death.
heading: 800.5.3.4 Expiration of Sponsor Deeming

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 800 Citizen/Non-Citizen

body: If verification is required and not provided on a timely basis, determine the eligibility of the remaining members. Budget the income, resources, and expenses of the ineligible member as per Ineligible Members .
heading: 800.6.1 Verification Requirements

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 800 Citizen/Non-Citizen

body: Verify if questionable.
heading: 800.6.2 Citizenship

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 800 Citizen/Non-Citizen

body:
Verification is required to determine eligible non-citizen status before approving or adding a non-citizen to 3SVT benefits. After the USCIS documentation is received, use the SAVE system to verify the documentation is accurate. Exception : American Indians may not be listed on SAVE. Use the SAVE system at: initial application; when adding a non-citizen; and at recertification.

Note: Run SAVE at recertification even if the customer does not report a change in immigration status.
heading: 800.6.3 Eligible Non-Citizen

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 800 Citizen/Non-Citizen

body: Verification of the active duty or veteran status is required. Veteran status verification may be a discharge certificate (DD Form 214 or the equivalent) and character of Honorable discharge. Active duty verification may be a current Military Identification Card (DD Form 2 Active) or a copy of current military orders. If the person does not have either of these, refer them to the VA .
heading: 800.6.4 Military Requirement

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 800 Citizen/Non-Citizen

body: Do not request verification/documentation of non-citizen status for persons who are not participating in the program. This is true whether the person claims to be lawfully or illegally present in the U.S.
heading: 800.6.5 Ineligible Non-Citizen

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 800 Citizen/Non-Citizen

body:
Verification is required for the sponsor (and spouse if appropriate) as follows: Income and resources Name, address, and phone number

Exception : If the non-citizen is categorically eligible, verification of sponsor information may be waived and the information from the categorical eligible program is used (Example: Reach Up).

If the information and/or verification is not provided, the non-citizen is not eligible and their income, resources and expenses are budgeted as per Ineligible Members.
heading: 800.6.6 Sponsored Non-Citizen

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 800 Citizen/Non-Citizen

body:
Verification of qualifying work quarters is required. To obtain current verification, the person may use W-2s, income tax forms, wage stubs, etc. Verification may also be obtained through the SVES . Prior to requesting the information from SVES, a release form, Consent for Release of Information ( Form SSA-3288 ), must be signed by the person with earnings. If the information from SVES is incomplete, incorrect, or discrepant, or the non-citizen cannot get the release form signed, contact the SSA for assistance.

See Determining Qualifying Quarters for the amount of earnings needed for each quarter.
heading: 800.6.7 Work Quarter Eligibility

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 800 Citizen/Non-Citizen

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 900 Students

body:
New information in the manual is highlighted and noted with a . See Manual Updates and for details.

Section with expandable text are noted with a throughout the manual.
heading: 900 Students

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 900 Students

body:
Students must meet eligible student criteria to be a household member . A student who does not meet these criteria is ineligible for 3SVT benefits. See Non-members .

A student is a person at least 18 years old and under 50 years old who is enrolled at least half-time in post-secondary education institution. The student is under age 18 until the month following the month they turn 18. The student is age 50 the first day of the month they turn 50.

See Student Income for guidance on excluded and countable income.
heading: 900.1 Overview

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 900 Students

body: The student is enrolled as of the 1st day of the school term through normal scheduled class periods, vacation, and recess unless they: graduate; are suspended, expelled, or drop out; or don’t intend to register for the next school term (excluding summer school).
heading: 900.2 Enrolled

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 900 Students

body: The school defines the number of credit hours to qualify a student as at least half-time.
heading: 900.3 Half-Time

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 900 Students

body:
Any public or private educational institution (including correspondence or on-line institutions) which; normally requires a high school diploma or equivalency certificate for enrollment; or admits persons who are beyond the age of compulsory school attendance in Vermont; and the institution is legally authorized or recognized by Vermont; or the institution is accredited or pre-accredited by a national accrediting agency.

Examples : colleges, universities, business, vocational, trade, and technical schools.

An institution of higher education does not include any adult basic education program.

The following are not considered a post-secondary education institution: New England Center for Circus Arts.
heading: 900.4 Post-secondary Education Institution

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 900 Students

body: To be eligible for 3SVT, a student must meet at least one of the following criteria.
heading: 900.5.1 Criteria

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 900 Students

body: Under age 18 or age 50 or older.
heading: 900.5.2 Age

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 900 Students

body:
Responsible for the care of dependent household member under age six; If two adults are responsible, the exemption is restricted to one person per child.

Responsible for the care of a dependent household member at least age six but under age 12 if the student does not have adequate child care; or

Full-time student who is a single parent responsible for the care of dependent child under the age of 12. The single parent and child must be in the same 3SVT household. Includes an adult with Parental Control over the child as long as the adult is not residing with a spouse.
heading: 900.5.3 Caring for a Dependent Child

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 900 Students

heading: 900.5.4 Employment

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 900 Students

body: Meets work registration definition of physically or mentally unfit .
heading: 900.5.5 Physically or Mentally Unfit for Work

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 900 Students

body: The student gets cash benefits from the Reach Up program.
heading: 900.5.6 Reach Up Participant

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 900 Students

body:
The student is assigned or placed in school through or complying with: WIOA ; a program under Section 236 of the Trade Act of 1974, which includes TAA and TRA ; a 3SVT employment and training program, see Individual Career Advancement Network (ICAN) ; or Email the ICAN email group to confirm compliance. an on-the-job training program where the employer pays wages to the student for the training.

Self-initiated education placements while enrolled in a training program are considered complying with the program’s requirements.

Customers who voluntarily participate in an approved employment and training program and are placed in an institution of higher education meet this criteria.
heading: 900.5.7 Training

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 900 Students

body:
The student is participating in a state or federally funded work study program. The student must be approved for work study at the time of application; The work study must be approved for the school term; and the student must anticipate working during that time.

Once allowed, the exemption continues until: the end of the month in which the school term ends; it is reported the student refused a work study assignment; or there is a break between terms of a full month or longer unless the student participates during the break.
heading: 900.5.8 Work Study

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 900 Students

body: The student is enrolled in one of the following Community College of Vermont programs: any two-year associate degree program, with the exception of the liberal studies program; or any certificate program.
heading: 900.5.9 Career and Technical Education Program under the Carl D. Perkins Career and Technical Education Improvement Act of 2006

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 900 Students

body:
Verify the student’s enrollment status. Verify the student’s eligible exemption criteria.

Exception : verify caring for a dependent child only if questionable.
heading: 900.6 Verification

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 900 Students

metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1000 Social Security Number

body:
New information in the manual is highlighted and noted with a . See Manual Updates and for details.

Section with expandable text are noted with a throughout the manual.
heading: 1000 Social Security Number

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1000 Social Security Number

body:
All household members applying for 3SVT must provide their SSN . A household member with more than one SSN must provide all of them. If a household member does not have an SSN, they must apply for one before approval.

Exception : See Newborn and Religious Beliefs

Providing the SSN is voluntary, however, failure to provide or apply for an SSN without good cause will result in ineligibility for that person.

Note : The 3SVT application cannot be delayed pending the verification of an SSN by SSA. The person is allowed to participate pending verification.
heading: 1000.1 Overview

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1000 Social Security Number

body: Refer household members who do not have an SSN to their local SSA office to apply for an SSN. Once proof of the SSN application is provided, allow the household member to participate while SSA processes the application.
heading: 1000.2.1 Referral to SSA

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1000 Social Security Number

body:
Parents of a newborn child may apply for an SSN for the child at the hospital, when available. A household must provide proof of application for an SSN for a newborn child within six months following the month the baby is born or at its next recertification, whichever is later.

If at the next 3SVT recertification or within 6 months from the date of the child’s birth the child has not met the social security requirement determine if good cause exists.
heading: 1000.2.2 Newborn

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1000 Social Security Number

body: Household members who have a religious objection to applying for or providing an SSN would not be required to do so to be eligible for 3SVT.
heading: 1000.2.3 Religious Beliefs

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1000 Social Security Number

body: If a household member has refused or failed without good cause to provide or apply for an SSN, that household member is ineligible to participate in the 3SVT program. Only the member without the SSN is ineligible, not the entire household. Resources are counted as if they were eligible; income and deductions are prorated. See Ineligible Members .
heading: 1000.3 Failure to Comply

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1000 Social Security Number

body:
Documentary evidence or other information that shows the household has applied for or made every effort to supply the necessary information for an SSN is proof of Good Cause .

This information may come from any of the following: The household member SSA DCF staff

Note : Good cause does not include delays caused by illness, lack of transportation or temporary absences from the home as SSA allows mail-in applications.
heading: 1000.4.1 Proof of Good Cause

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1000 Social Security Number

body: Allow a member who can show good cause for not timely completing an SSN application to participate for the month of application and one additional month. After this, the household must show good cause monthly in order to continue participating.
heading: 1000.4.2 Continued Participation

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1000 Social Security Number

body: An ineligible member may become eligible by providing their SSN or by providing proof of application for SSN.
heading: 1000.5 Regaining Eligibility

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1000 Social Security Number

body: When determining eligibility for 3SVT, SSNs may be used to: Determine amount of benefits Prevent duplicate participation Facilitate mass changes in federal benefits Determine the accuracy or reliability of information given by households Request and exchange information on persons through IEVS
heading: 1000.6 Use of the SSN

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1000 Social Security Number

body:
Verify SSN at application or if a new member is added to the household.

Exception : See Newborn
heading: 1000.7 Verification

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1000 Social Security Number

body:
Release# 17-1

Effective Date: 12/01/2017

Legal Base Food and Nutrition Act of 2008, as amended. 7 CFR § 273.6

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1100 3SquaresVT Work Requirements

body:
New information in the manual is highlighted and noted with a . See Manual Updates and for details.

Section with expandable text are noted with a throughout the manual.
heading: 1100 3SquaresVT Work Requirements

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1100 3SquaresVT Work Requirements

body: The 3SVT program has two different types of work requirements: General work requirements Time limited benefits work requirements
heading: 1100.1 Overview

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1100 3SquaresVT Work Requirements

body: The following must be completed at application, recertification, when changes are reported, and when a member is added to the household: Determine the general work requirements and time limited benefits work requirements status of each person in the household. Note: Some exemptions require verification. See: General work requirements verification requirements. Time limited benefits work requirements verification requirements. Explain the work requirements, exemptions, and procedures to customers subject to the general work requirements or time limited benefits work requirements. See Oral Explanation . ACCESS will generate the Consolidated Work Notice .
heading: 1100.2 Procedure and Notification

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1100 3SquaresVT Work Requirements

body:
The Consolidated Work Notice is a written notice sent identifying the members subject to the general and time limited benefits work requirements.

This written notice must be provided at: Application approval; Recertification approval; When a member loses an exemption; and When a new member is added and is subject to work requirements. Note: To mail the notice at another time enter "Issue 3SVT Work Requirement Notice" into CATN and the notice will generate over night.

Note: If all members are exempt from the time limited benefits work requirements, the notice would include only the general work requirement information.

The notice must : Identify which members are subject to which work requirements. Include the following general work requirements information: How to meet; What happens if someone does not meet; How to be exempt; and What is considered good cause. Include the following time limited benefits work requirements information as appropriate: What the time limited benefits work requirements are; How to meet; What happens if someone does not meet; How to be exempt; and What is considered good cause.
heading: 1100.3.1 Consolidated Work Notice

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1100 3SquaresVT Work Requirements

body:
The Oral explanation of the work requirements is provided to households that include individuals subject to the general and time limited benefits work requirements.

Provide to the household at: Application Interview; Recertification Interview; When a member loses an exemption; and When a new member is added and is subject to work requirements. Note : Provide the oral explanation at the time the change is reported verbally. For changes reported in writing or discovered by ESD, one collateral call to the household is required and must be documented in CATN.

Oral explanation must : Identify which members are subject to which work requirements. Include the following general work requirements information: How to meet; What happens if someone does not meet; How to be exempt; and What is considered good cause. Include the following time limited benefits work requirements information as appropriate: What the time limited benefits work requirements are; How to meet; What happens if someone does not meet; How to be exempt; and What is considered good cause.
heading: 1100.3.2 Oral Explanation

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1100 3SquaresVT Work Requirements

body:
ICAN is Vermont’s Employment and Training Program . Customers who want to get a job or gain skills to increase employability may qualify for ICAN. To qualify, a customer must be active 3SVT and cannot be receiving: Reach First; Reach Up; Reach Ahead; or PSE .

Note : Households eligible for Reach Up but not receiving it will be required to apply to be eligible for ICAN participant reimbursements .

Educate qualifying customers about ICAN during the interview at application and recertification . The household must be informed of the following: What ICAN is; ICAN activities ; Participant reimbursements ; and Rights and responsibilities, if applicable.

A customer interested in ICAN must be given an ICAN referral .

Note: Inform customers who are over the age of 60 or disabled of ICAN only if they express interest in getting a job. See ICAN Process Flowchart and Individual Career Advancement Network: Quick Reference guide for process and referral guidance.

Participation in ICAN is voluntary for all 3SVT recipients.

Encourage 3SVT customers to go to the ICAN webpage for detailed information about employment services, available opportunities, and to view the ICAN orientation.

If the household includes a customer with time limited benefits, see 3SquaresVT Time Limited Benefits.
heading: 1100.4.1 ICAN Qualification Procedures

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1100 3SquaresVT Work Requirements

body:
The ESD 218FS-1 Individual Career Advancement Network (ICAN) is the form used to refer customers to ICAN orientation.

The form notifies the customer: How to complete ICAN orientation; Details regarding ICAN activities; and How ICAN may help someone meet their work requirements.

Mail the ESD 218FS-1 if the household is interested in ICAN.
heading: 1100.4.2 ICAN Referral

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1100 3SquaresVT Work Requirements

body:
To participate in ICAN customers must: Complete the ICAN orientation; Complete a Career Assessment and plan; Engage with ICAN providers according to career plan; and Provide verification as required for participant reimbursements .

Note : Participant reimbursements are available for specific documented out of pocket costs. Examples: Transportation and dependent care.
heading: 1100.4.3.1 Participation Requirements

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1100 3SquaresVT Work Requirements

body: ICAN has six allowable activities (also called components): Supervised Job Search Receive support during job search activities. Job Search Training Enhanced job search readiness programs. Learn job seeking techniques. Self-Employment Training Assistance in starting or growing a small business. Education GED, High School Diploma, Certificate Programs or Higher Education. Work Experience Provides actual work experience and training. Job Retention Services Allowed for 90 days after a participant has gained employment. Must have participated in an ICAN activity prior to being employed. Participants are eligible for continued support from ICAN case managers/providers and participant reimbursements while engaged in job retention services.
heading: 1100.4.3.2 ICAN Activities

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1100 3SquaresVT Work Requirements

body:
Participant reimbursements are payments or reimbursements for allowable costs that are reasonable, necessary, and directly related to ICAN participation. A cost is allowable if it is identified in the current spending guidelines. A cost is reasonable if it’s what someone else would pay in the same situation. A cost is necessary and directly related if it’s essential to participating in approved ICAN activities

Participant reimbursements aren't guaranteed and are subject to available funding; they're temporary and are meant to help with costs related to ICAN participation.

Participant reimbursements can include transportation, dependent care and other necessary cost to participate in the program.

Participant reimbursements cannot: Be available through another government program or available at no cost through a private source; Customers will be required to apply to other state or local programs to be eligible for specific reimbursements. This includes but is not limited to: Reach Up, Unemployment Benefits, Fuel Assistance, Crisis Fuel, HireAbility Vermont, Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (WIOA), Microbusiness Development Programs, and Childcare Assistance programs. Be used to support regular employment unless enrolled in the job retention component and the participant reimbursements are linked to participation in that component; Be used to pay bad debt such as outstanding fines or fees; and Be used to make on-going regular payments. Note: If a participant reimbursement request is denied the ICAN team will send a denial notice. Any customer with questions about a denial must have their information forwarded to the ICAN email for follow up.
heading: 1100.4.3.3 ICAN Participant Reimbursements

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1100 3SquaresVT Work Requirements

body:
An ICAN provider determination is when an ICAN provider determines that a customer is not a good fit for participation in their available ICAN services and components.

When a provider determination is made a customer is no longer able to access ICAN services from that provider unless re-screened and referred by ESD.

The ICAN provider will submit an ICAN provider determination (PD) form to the ICAN email within 10 days of the determination.

ESD will send the customer a provider determination notice. The notice will include the following information: What a provider determination is; The next steps the state will take; Confirmation the individual is not being sanctioned; and TLBWR information if applicable. Note: TLBWR customers are not exempt from the work requirement because of a provider determination. They must continue to meet their work requirements.

At recertification, or sooner at the customer’s request: Screen and refer the individual to ICAN. Screen for an exemption from the work requirements due to the customer’s physical and mental fitness . Coordinate with other Federal, State, or local employment and training programs to identify work opportunities or assistance for the individual. Vermont Department of Labor: (802) 828 – 4394 or online at https://labor.vermont.gov/JobSearchVermont Vermont 211: Dial 2-1-1 or online at https://vermont211.org/ HireAbility Vermont find local contact information at https://www.hireabilityvt.com/get-started/ Local Community Action Agencies find local resources at https://dcf.vermont.gov/contacts/partners/caps
heading: 1100.4.3.4 ICAN Provider Determination
