metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1100 3SquaresVT Work Requirements

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1200 General Work Requirements

body:
New information in the manual is highlighted and noted with a . See Manual Updates and for details.

Section with expandable text are noted with a throughout the manual.
heading: 1200 General Work Requirements

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1200 General Work Requirements

body:
Persons applying for, or receiving, 3SVT benefits must meet the general work requirements, unless they are exempt to continue receiving 3SVT benefits.

Review general work requirements with households that include customers who are subject to the requirements. See 3SVT Work Requirements Procedures and Notification .
heading: 1200.1.2 Overview

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1200 General Work Requirements

body: Persons subject to the general work requirements must: Not quit a job or reduce hours below 30 per week without good cause if already working; Give Economic Services Division (ESD) enough information to determine employment status or job availability; and Accept a job offer unless it is unsuitable .
heading: 1200.1.3 The General Work Requirements

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1200 General Work Requirements

body: Exempt the following people from general work requirements. A person who is: Younger than 16. (Code 06) Age 16 or 17 and residing with a parent or with a person with parental control. (Code 06) A student enrolled at least half-time in a school, training program, or Institution of Higher Education. (Code 06) This includes adults going to high school, or VAL classes. The exemption continues during vacations and other breaks if the student intends to register and attend at least half-time for the next school term. The exemption ends when the student graduates, is suspended or expelled, or drops out. Note: See Students for eligibility requirements for students enrolled at least half-time in an institution of higher education. Employed or self-employed and working at least 30 hours per week or receiving gross weekly earnings at least equal to the Federal Minimum Wage multiplied by 30 hours per week. (Code 07) Fluctuating work hours should be averaged to arrive at a reasonable estimate of hours expected to be worked in a week. Volunteer hours or hours worked for In-Kind Benefits are not included in the 30 hours per week calculation. Includes migrant or seasonal farm workers who are under contract (or similar agreement) with an employer or crew chief to start work within 30 days. Age 60 or older. (Code 08) A person is exempt the month they turn 60. Responsible for the care of a dependent child under the age of 6. Note: Verification required. (Code 09) The household must report that the parent or caretaker is providing the care to receive the exemption. Excluded Foster Children and ineligible members can still give a household this exemption. Only one parent or caretaker per child under 6 may receive this exemption. For households with multiple children under 6, the household must report which adult is providing care to each child(ren). If the child turns 6 in the certification period, the person’s exemption remains until the next recertification. Receiving or has applied for (UCB). (Code 10) Responsible for the care of another person who needs help caring for themselves. Note: Verification required. (Code 11) The individuals providing and receiving care do not need to live in the same household. Physically or mentally unable to work. Note: Verification required. (Code 12) Receiving Reach First, Reach Up, Post-Secondary Education or Reach Ahead benefits. (Code 14) A regular participant in a drug or alcohol addiction program. (Code 16) Participation can be either as a resident of the treatment center or an outpatient basis. Among others, the following meet this exemption: Participants in the Department of Corrections ISAP and CBISA programs. Residents of Teen Challenge. Persons treated through methadone clinics.
heading: 1200.1.4 General Work Requirement Exemptions

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1200 General Work Requirements

body: Customers subject to the general work requirements must not voluntarily: Quit a job, or Reduce work hours.
heading: 1200.2.1 Voluntary Quit or reduction in hours

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1200 General Work Requirements

body:
A customer voluntarily quit a job if: The customer worked at least 30 hours a week, or the job provided weekly earnings equal to the federal minimum wage times 30 hours; and The quit/job termination occurred within 60 days of the application, or anytime thereafter; and The reason for the job termination was caused by the customer. Participation in a strike by a Federal, State, or local government employee is a voluntary quit without good cause.

A customer did not voluntarily quit a job if: The stopped job was a self-employment business. The customer resigned from the job at the employer’s request for reasons that are beyond the person’s control, such as unable to complete the required work assignments. The customer did not reenlist in the military.

Apply disqualification penalties if the customer voluntarily quit a job without good cause .

Note : Voluntary quit applies to a person whose only exemption from the general work requirements was employment of at least 30 hours a week (or equivalent earnings).
heading: 1200.2.2 Job Quit

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1200 General Work Requirements

body:
A customer voluntarily reduced their work hours if: The hours were reduced to less than 30 hours a week; or The hours were reduced, and the new weekly earnings are below the federal minimum wage times 30 hours; and The reduction was requested by the employee, not the employer; and The reduction occurred within 60 days of the application, or anytime thereafter.

A customer did not voluntarily reduce work hours to less than 30 hours a week if the reduction in hours is temporary for reasons such as illness, vacation or personal business.

Apply disqualification penalties if the customer voluntarily quit a job without good cause .

Note: Voluntary reduction in work hours applies to a person whose only exemption from the general work requirements was employment of at least 30 hours a week (or equivalent earnings).
heading: 1200.2.3 Reduced Work Hours

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1200 General Work Requirements

body:
Customers have good cause for quitting a job or reducing work hours if: There were circumstances beyond the customer’s control. Examples include, but are not limited to: Illness of the customer or another household member requiring the customer’s presence Household emergency The job was unsuitable . There was discrimination by an employer based on: age; race; sex (including gender identity and sexual orientation); color; disability; marital status; family/parental status; income derived from public assistance programs; religious beliefs national origin; or political beliefs Work demands or conditions make it unreasonable to continue employment, such as working without being paid on schedule. They accepted another job of at least 30 hours per week (or equivalent) or enrolled in school at least half-time. Another household member accepted a job or enrolled in school at least half-time in another county or state, causing the household to move. They are under age 60, retire, and the employer recognizes it as retirement. They accepted a job offer that provides at least 30 hours a week, or weekly earnings at least equal to federal minimum wage times 30 hours, and that job did not materialize or meet the hourly criteria or weekly earnings through no fault of their own. They leave a job that requires frequent moves, such as Migrant Worker or Seasonal Farmworker . When adequate dependent care is reported as unavailable for children at least six and under 12. Record that a provider could not be located and do not sanction for voluntary quit. The job quit does not meet the definition of voluntary job quit or reduced work hours .

Always consider all the facts when good cause is claimed. CATN the person’s claim of good cause identifying the facts and why a disqualification was imposed or not imposed.
heading: 1200.2.4 Good Cause

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1200 General Work Requirements

body: Do not impose a voluntary quit or reduction-in-job-hours disqualification if the job meets one of the following: The wage offered is less than: The applicable federal or Vermont Minimum Wage - For the current Vermont minimum wage click here (whichever is higher), or 80 percent of the federal minimum wage , if neither the federal nor Vermont minimum wage is applicable. The job is on a piece-rate basis and the average hourly earnings are less than the hourly wage as defined above. The person must join, resign from, or refrain from joining any legitimate labor organization to get or keep the job. The job offered is at a site currently subject to a strike or lockout, unless : the strike has been enjoined under Section 208 of the Labor-Management Relations Act (commonly known as the Taft-Hartley Act), or an injunction has been issued under Section 10 of the Railway Labor Act. Daily commuting time exceeds 2 hours a day, not including taking a child to and from childcare. Note : When a household move results in more than 2 hours commuting time, the job is unsuitable. This includes moves within the state as well as from state to state. This applies whether a person leaves a job before or after the move. The distance to the place of employment is too far to walk, and neither public nor private transportation is available to get the person to the job site. The person is physically or mentally unfit to perform the job. Note : Verification is required. The job is offered within the first 30 days of registration and is not in the person’s major field of experience. The working hours or nature of the job interferes with the person’s religious observations, convictions, or beliefs.
heading: 1200.2.5 Unsuitable Employment

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1200 General Work Requirements

body: Disqualify a customer when they: Does not provide employment status or availability for work information, upon request; Does not accept a job offer unless the job is unsuitable ; Voluntary quits a job without good cause ; or Voluntarily reduces hours of employment without good cause .
heading: 1200.3.1 Disqualification Determination

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1200 General Work Requirements

body:
The customer is ineligible to participate in 3SVT as follows: For the first violation: 1 month or until the person complies, whichever is later. For the second violation: 3 months or until the person complies, whichever is later. For the third and subsequent violations: 6 months or until the person complies, whichever is later.

Note : The disqualification penalty follows the disqualified person if they join a new household.
heading: 1200.3.2 Disqualification Periods

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1200 General Work Requirements

body:
The disqualified person can become eligible again by: Becoming exempt from the General Work Requirements ; or Serving the minimum disqualification period and complying with the failed requirement. If the person has not complied with the failed requirement by the time the minimum disqualification period ends, the disqualification remains in effect until the person does comply with the requirement.

Exception : Persons disqualified for voluntary quit or a reduction in work hours may reapply after the end of the disqualification period.

The household must reapply if the 3SVT case was closed.
heading: 1200.3.3 Ending the Disqualification

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1200 General Work Requirements

body: Verification and documentation are required prior to coding a person exempt from work registration for the following exemptions. If the verification is not returned within 10 days, keep the work registration code as “02” unless the person meets a different exemption. Do not deny or close benefits for failure to verify an exemption.
heading: 1200.4.1 Exemptions that Require Verification

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1200 General Work Requirements

body: The household must report that the parent or caretaker is providing the care to receive the exemption. The following must be asked and noted in CATN: Which adult(s) in your household is providing care for the child(ren) under 6? CATN must include which adult is providing care. If the household has multiple children under 6 the adult providing care for each child must be noted. If an individual is not providing care to the child under 6 then they are not eligible for this exemption.
heading: 1200.4.1.1 Responsible for the Care of a Dependent Child Under Age of Six

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1200 General Work Requirements

body: A statement from a Qualified Health Professional regarding who is being cared for and who is needed to provide the care. Exception : No verification is needed for a designated EP on an active essential person grant.
heading: 1200.4.1.2 Responsible for the Care of Another person

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1200 General Work Requirements

body: To be deemed physically or mentally unfit for work the following can be used as verification: Meet the definition of Disabled Disabled . A person who meets one or more of the following: Receives disability or blindness benefits from any of these programs: Social Security, SSI , or MABD . A person meets this even when the full benefit is withheld or deferred due to recoupment. Receives a federally or state administered SSI supplement based on disability or blindness, or section 212(a) of PL 93-66. Receives a disability retirement benefit from a government agency for a disability considered permanent by SSA disability considered permanent by SSA . Any of the following conditions: Permanent loss of use of both hands, both feet, or one hand and one foot. Amputation of leg at hip. Amputation of leg or foot because of diabetes or peripheral vascular diseases. Total deafness, not correctable by surgery or hearing aid. Statutory blindness, unless caused by cataracts or detached retina. IQ of 59 or less, established after the person becomes 16 years old. Spinal cord or nerve root lesions resulting in paraplegia or quadriplegia. Multiple sclerosis with damage to the nervous system caused by scattered areas of inflammation. The inflammation recurs and has progressed to varied interference with the function of the nervous system, including severe muscle weakness, paralysis, and vision and speech defects. Muscular dystrophy with irreversible wasting of the muscles, impairing the ability to use the arms or legs. Impaired renal function caused by chronic renal disease, resulting in severely reduced function which may require dialysis or kidney transplant. Amputation of a limb of a person at least 55 years old. Is a veteran the VA considers totally (100%) disabled or permanently housebound or in need of regular aid and attendance. Is a veteran’s surviving spouse who the VA considers: in need of regular aid and attendance, or permanently housebound, or approved for benefits because of the veteran’s death and has a disability considered permanent by SSA. Is a veteran’s surviving child who the VA considers: incapable of self-support, or approved for benefits because of the veteran’s death and has a disability considered permanent by SSA A statement from a Qualified Health Professional . Receipt of VA disability compensation regardless of the individual’s disability percentage rating. Receipt of temporary disability income from any agency. Pending SSI Application.
heading: 1200.4.1.3 Physically or Mentally Unfit for Work

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1200 General Work Requirements

body: The household must report if an individual is a regular participant in drug and alcohol treatment. The following must be in CATN to allow the exemption: Customer’s statements regarding participation including the name of the treatment program.
heading: 1200.4.1.4 Participant in Drug and Alcohol Treatment

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1200 General Work Requirements

body: Verify any other information that is questionable.
heading: 1200.4.2 Questionable Information

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1200 General Work Requirements

body:
Release # 26-1

Effective Date: 04/17/2026

Legal Base Food and Nutrition Act of 2008, as amended. 7 CFR § 273.7
heading: 1200.6 Last Update

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1200 General Work Requirements

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1300 3SquaresVT Time Limited Benefits

body:
New information in the manual is highlighted and noted with a . See Manual Updates and for details.

Section with expandable text are noted with a throughout the manual.
heading: 1300 3SquaresVT Time Limited Benefits

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1300 3SquaresVT Time Limited Benefits

body:
Time limited benefits are a required federal policy. Customers who are not exempt must satisfy these requirements to continue receiving 3SVT.

Review the time limited benefits work requirements with households that include customers subject to the time limits. See 3SVT Work Requirements Procedures and Notification.
heading: 1300.1 Overview

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1300 3SquaresVT Time Limited Benefits

body:
A customer is time limited if they: Are at least age 18 and under age 65; Have no child under 14 in their 3SVT household; and Are able to work, which means the customer: Is satisfying the time limited benefits work requirement ; or Does not have any exemptions from the time limited benefits work requirements or general work requirements; This includes customers who were working/self-employed and lost the general work requirement exemption because: Their work hours fall below 20 hours per week/80 hours per month; Their earnings fall below $935.25 per month; and They have no other general work requirement exemptions.

A time limited customer is limited to receiving three countable months of benefits within a three-year (36-month) period, unless they: Are exempt from time limits; Satisfy the time limited benefit work requirements ; or Qualify for the cure .
heading: 1300.2 Time Limited Customer

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1300 3SquaresVT Time Limited Benefits

body:
Exempt the following the people from time limited benefits work requirements. A person who is: Pregnant. (Code 24) Exempt from the 3SVT general work requirement for a reason other than age. (Code 25) Under Age 18 or 65 or older. (Code 25). A member of a 3SVT household that includes a child under 14 who is receiving benefits. (Code 27) One child can exempt the entire household. Exception : Excluded Foster Children and ineligible members can still give a household this exemption. Children under 18 can receive this exemption if they do not meet a general work requirement exemption. Temporarily unable to participate in training or employment due to a personal obstacle or barrier. (Code 55) A personal obstacle or barrier means a physical, mental or emotional situation that prevents an individual from being able to meet the monthly work requirement. Individuals who are Indians, Urban Indians, California Indians, and other Indians who are eligible for the Indian Health Services. (Code 60) Tribes and Nations eligible for this exemption can be found here: Federally Recognized Indian Tribes in the United States

A customer does not have a Countable Month when they are exempt.
heading: 1300.3 Exemptions from Time Limit

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1300 3SquaresVT Time Limited Benefits

body:
Customers with time limited benefits work requirements who do not meet their work requirement are only eligible for three full months ( countable months ) of 3SVT benefits every three-year period.

The three-year period is the same for all customers in Vermont. The periods are: November 1, 2025 through October 31, 2028 November 1, 2028 through October 31, 2031 November 1, 2031 through October 31, 2034

The countable months are electronically tracked. When the three-month limit has been used, the customer is no longer eligible for 3SVT benefits. At the end of the three-month limit, the system automatically removes the person or closes the case. See Ineligible Members for treatment of the income and resources of an ineligible customer who is a member of an eligible 3SVT household.

After using their initial three countable months or second three months ( the cure ), a customer must meet their time limited benefits work requirements to continue to receive 3SVT benefits.
heading: 1300.4.1 Three Countable Month Limitation

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1300 3SquaresVT Time Limited Benefits

body:
A month is a countable month unless the household: receives prorated benefits; is exempt from time limit; or meets the work requirement.

Countable months can be removed from the current three-year period if customers provide verification that they were exempt or meeting the work requirement for the received countable month(s).

When contacting other states for duplicate participation, check for time limit countable months. Out of State Verification of Countable Months
heading: 1300.4.2 Countable Month

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1300 3SquaresVT Time Limited Benefits

body: A time limited customer must meet one of the following work requirements to avoid using a countable month . Working a total of 80 hours or more in 30-day period; Work means employment, self-employment, volunteer work, or working in exchange for In-Kind Benefits . Participating for a total of 80 hours or more in a 30-day period in: WIOA activities; Section 236 of the Trade Act of 1974 activities, which include TAA and TRA ; Employment and training programs for veterans operated by Department of Labor or Department of Veterans Affairs; or ICAN. Note : Ineligible time limited customers cannot use ICAN to meet their TLBWR. Workfare participation. Workfare in volunteering at any public or private non-profit agency. Examples: Church, senior centers, town offices, food shelf, etc. Hours are determined by the 3SVT benefit amount divided by the Vermont minimum wage . Note : Zero benefit households: $0 benefit = 0 hours required Households with multiple workfare participants can split hours as they see fit as long as: Total household workfare hours are met; and All workfare customers participate. A non-participating customer will receive a countable month. If the total household workfare hours are not met, all workfare customers in that household receive a countable month.
heading: 1300.4.3.1 Satisfying the Monthly Time Limited Benefits Work Requirements

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1300 3SquaresVT Time Limited Benefits

body:
To qualify for good cause, the customer must : still retain the job, work program or Workfare site; plan to continue to participate in the activity that meets the work requirement after the temporary absence; and have a good reason that is beyond the person’s control such as, but not limited to: A household emergency Unavailability of transportation Illness of the customer or another household member requiring the customer’s presence

ES must refer a case with a customer who seems to meet good cause to the Supervisor. The Supervisor will send the case to AOPS to decide if good cause is met.

CATN the good cause reason or why that good cause was not accepted.

Any month that a good cause reason is granted is not a countable month.
heading: 1300.4.3.2 Good Cause for Not Meeting the Work Requirement

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1300 3SquaresVT Time Limited Benefits

body:
Customers enrolling in ICAN in their third countable month may qualify for a temporary exemption to allow time for them to enroll.

Customers must complete ICAN orientation in their third countable month to qualify for a temporary exemption.

Customers who complete ICAN enrollment may receive up to two additional temporary exemptions (for a maximum of three temporary exemptions) to allow them time to connect to their local ICAN team and complete necessary assessments. The local ICAN team will communicate the need for these additional exemptions to the ICAN team at ESD. The ICAN team will enter the code 15 and CATN. Enrollment means connected to an ICAN partner to complete required paperwork, set up a Vermont Job Links (VJL) account and schedule a meeting with the local employment team.

Temporary exemptions can only be used once per enrollment. If the customer fails to enroll in ICAN following the application of an exemption they do not qualify for additional exemptions.

Customers who are not eligible for ICAN are not eligible for temporary exemptions.
heading: 1300.4.4.3 Enrolling in ICAN in their Third Countable Month

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1300 3SquaresVT Time Limited Benefits

body:
A customer who has exhausted their three countable months may regain eligibility at any time. A customer regains eligibility: By meeting the time limited benefits work requirement for 30 consecutive days; By meeting an exemption from the time limited benefits work requirement; or When the 36-month time limit period ends and a new 36-month period begins.

At application, if a customer reports they are exempt or meeting the work requirement request required verification .

If verification is provided for the exemption, work program hours, or workfare participation, determine if the verification confirms that the customer: Is exempt , or Is meeting the work requirement ; and If they will continue to meet the work requirement. If verification does not include confirmation of continued participation, determine the work requirement met only for the months verified. Update the MEMB panel and process the case including the time limited customer.

If the required verification is not returned within 10 days, consider the person to be time limited and not meeting their work requirement. Deny or exclude the time limited customer for not meeting their work requirement. Exception: If the required verification was for earned income, deny the case for non-cooperation.

Reinstate a time limited customer’s case if they verify they have met the time limited benefits work requirement within 30 days from the date of application. Provide benefits from the date of application.

Time limited customers can be added to an active case at any time if they become exempt or have met their work requirement for 30 days.

Screen for the cure when a customer: regains eligibility and then stops meeting their work requirement; or loses an exemption.
heading: 1300.4.4 Regaining Eligibility

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1300 3SquaresVT Time Limited Benefits

body:
A customer with time limited benefits can be eligible for the cure. The cure is an additional three countable months. To be eligible a customer has to have met the time limited benefits work requirements after receiving their initial three countable months.

These additional countable months must be used consecutively.

Note : The customer does not have to be meeting the time limited benefits work requirements at the time of application. They must have met the requirement sometime after using their initial three countable months.

The three additional countable months are allowed once in the 36-month period. Once started, the additional countable month period must continue uninterrupted. Unused additional months, due to case closure or other reasons, cannot be used at another time.
heading: 1300.4.4.1 Regaining Eligibility for a Second 3-Month Period (The Cure)

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1300 3SquaresVT Time Limited Benefits

body:
At application and recertification verification is required prior to coding a person as meeting the time limited benefits work requirement.

Allow the household 10 days to provide the required verification.

If the required verification is not returned within 10 days: Consider the person to be time limited and not meeting their work requirement. Change the time limited benefits work requirements code to a countable month . Do not deny or close benefits solely for failure to verify an exemption, work program hours, or workfare participation. Deny for non-cooperation if verification requested is for earned income and hours.

See Regaining Eligibility for customers with no countable months remaining.
heading: 1300.5.1 Work Requirement Verification

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1300 3SquaresVT Time Limited Benefits

body:
When a customer is meeting the work requirement, verification could include, but is not limited to: Work hours Verify hours when verifying income. Collateral Contact Paystubs Employment verification form Self-employment hours are self-reported by the household. In-Kind hours must be verified. Monetary value not required. Collateral Contact Statement from individual allowing In-Kind hours. Volunteering/unpaid work Collateral contact Statement from individual they are working for Work program participation Collateral contact Employment Program Verification Form Time sheets Letter from the program Workfare Participation Collateral contact Workfare Verification Form Time sheets Letter from the work site

If verification is provided for employment, work program, or workfare participation, determine if the verification confirms that the customer: Is meeting the work requirement; and Is expected to continue to meet the work requirement. If verification does not include confirmation of expected continued participation, only allow it for the months determined by the verification. Update the MEMB panel and approve the case including the time limited customer.
heading: 1300.5.1.1 Meeting the Work Requirements Verification

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1300 3SquaresVT Time Limited Benefits

body:
Verification is required prior to coding a person exempt due to a personal obstacle or barrier that prevents the person from meeting the time limited benefits work requirements.

If the required verification is not returned within 10 days, keep the customer’s code as 21 ( time limited ) unless the person meets a different exemption.

Acceptable verification is a statement from a provider who has details on the customer`s situation. Examples: Housing Case Managers, ICAN Case Managers, or Department of Corrections representatives, etc.
heading: 1300.5.2.1 Personal Obstacle or Barrier

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1300 3SquaresVT Time Limited Benefits

body: Verify any other information that is questionable.
heading: 1300.5.3 Questionable Information

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1300 3SquaresVT Time Limited Benefits

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1400 Categorical Eligibility

body:
New information in the manual is highlighted and noted with a . See Manual Updates and for details.

Section with expandable text are noted with a throughout the manual.
heading: 1400 Categorical Eligibility

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1400 Categorical Eligibility

body:
There are two different types of categorical eligibility: SCE and ECE . SCE and ECE households are not required to meet certain income and resource tests and have less verification requirements. Categorical eligibility applies to the household, not to the person. Households must follow and meet 3SVT rules unless specifically identified in this item.

Determine if a household is SCE first.
heading: 1400.1 Types of Categorical Eligibility

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1400 Categorical Eligibility

body:
Determine household composition before determining SCE.

Households are SCE when all members are eligible for any of the following qualifying programs: SSI / AABD PSE Reach Ahead Reach First Reach Up Vermont EITC in the past 12 months. Household must have included a child for the tax year in which Vermont EITC was provided. Household must have a child under 18 included in their current 3SVT household.

Households are considered eligible for the qualifying programs upon approval, including when benefits: have not been received yet, are suspended, or are withheld to repay an overpayment.
heading: 1400.2.1 Procedures for Determining SCE

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1400 Categorical Eligibility

body:
Households are not SCE when:

The household loses eligibility from the program making them SCE.

A member is disqualified for IPV .

The Head of Household is disqualified for failing to comply with work requirements .

Determine eligibility under regular 3SVT requirements.

Note : A household member who fails to comply with workfare is ineligible for SCE. Remaining household members are eligible for SCE.
heading: 1400.2.2 Ineligible for Standard Categorical Eligibility (SCE)

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1400 Categorical Eligibility

body: Do not include the following people as a member in a household otherwise SCE eligible: Non-member students Ineligible non-citizens Institutionalized in a non-exempt facility Disqualified non -head of household for failure to comply with a work requirement
heading: 1400.2.3 Ineligible Household Members

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1400 Categorical Eligibility

body:
Households not SCE are ECE because all applicant and recipient households are authorized to receive TANF-funded referral services. Households are notified of the referral services on notices at application and recertification. The notices include the following information in Additional Resources: Go to http://dcf.vermont.gov/benefits to find out about benefits available through the Department of Children and Families. Go to http://vermont211.org or call 2-1-1 toll free from anywhere in Vermont to find out about hundreds of community and statewide resources.

ECE households’ gross income must be below 185% of the FPL .

Households are considered ECE at application.
heading: 1400.3.1 Procedures for Determining ECE

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1400 Categorical Eligibility

body:
Households are not ECE eligible when: Gross income is over 185% of the FPL. Households with no senior or disabled members are not eligible for 3SVT. Determine eligibility for households with a Senior or disabled member under regular 3SVT requirements. These households do not have a gross income test. They have a resource test and a net income test only. A member is disqualified for Intentional program violation (IPV) . The Head of Household is disqualified for failing to comply with work requirements .

Determine eligibility under regular 3SVT requirements.

Note : A household member who fails to comply with workfare is ineligible for ECE. Remaining household members are eligible for ECE.
heading: 1400.3.2 Ineligible for Expanded Categorical Eligibility (ECE)

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1400 Categorical Eligibility

body: Do not include the following people as a member in a household otherwise ECE eligible: Non-member students Ineligible non-citizens Institutionalized in a non-exempt facility Disqualified non -head of household for failure to comply with a work requirement
heading: 1400.3.3 Ineligible Household Members

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1400 Categorical Eligibility

body:
SCE and ECE households are not required to meet the following tests. 130% gross income Net income Resource limit including transferred resources policy

Follow the other 3SVT eligibility rules to determine the household’s eligibility.
heading: 1400.4 Eligibility Factors

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1400 Categorical Eligibility

body:
Verification is waived for the following eligibility factors for SCE households: SSN Residency Sponsored non-citizen information

Verify the following factors: Each member of a potential SCE household receives one of the qualifying programs , Household’s countable gross income does not exceed 185% for ECE households. Eligibility factors not waived for SCE or ECE, if required.

Note : SCE and ECE households applying for or receiving other benefit programs in addition to 3SVT must follow those programs’ information and verification requirements.
heading: 1400.5 Verification

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1400 Categorical Eligibility

body: Benefits for both SCE and ECE are determined following 3SVT eligibility rules.
heading: 1400.6.1 Benefits Summary

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1400 Categorical Eligibility

body: SCE and ECE households of one or two members receive no less than the minimum monthly benefit, except when prorating the initial month. Benefits are not issued when the prorated initial month’s issuance is less than $10. See Initial Month and Prorated Benefits .
heading: 1400.6.2 Households of One and Two Members

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1400 Categorical Eligibility

body:
SCE and ECE households of three or more receive benefits based on the monthly benefit tables . Suspend benefits if the household is SCE or ECE and eligible for a zero benefit. Do not deny the household. Notify the household their benefits are suspended, but they must continue to follow these requirements: Report changes, Complete recertification requirements.

As long as the household follows these requirements the household remains SCE or ECE until the household’s situation changes allowing the household to receive benefits, or case closure. See Suspended Benefits .
heading: 1400.6.3 Eligible for Zero Benefits

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1400 Categorical Eligibility

body:
SCE or ECE simplified reporting households are not required to report any income changes when: the household reported income over the 130% gross income limit , and remained eligible.

The household has fulfilled their income reporting requirement until completing the interim report or recertification , whichever comes first.

SCE or ECE households are not required to report changes in resources .

See Reduced Reporting Requirements for further reporting requirements.
heading: 1400.7 Reporting Requirements

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1400 Categorical Eligibility

body:
SCE and ECE cannot be cancelled retroactively.

Households cannot be over the income or resource limits in any month they were SCE or ECE.

Do not establish a 3SVT claim when an overpayment is: due to excess resources for any household determined SCE or ECE, or because a household should not have been categorically eligible due to a mistake in granting eligibility for the program that made it SCE or ECE.

Claims can be made against SCE or ECE households if an overpayment occurred because: the benefit determination was not correct, or the household was not eligible for SCE or ECE as described in Ineligible Households above.
heading: 1400.8 Claims

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1400 Categorical Eligibility

body: CATN categorical eligibility. Note SCE or ECE eligible.
heading: 1400.9 CATN

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1400 Categorical Eligibility

body:
Release # 25-3

Effective Date: 08/22/2025

Legal Base Food and Nutrition Act of 2008, as amended. 7 CFR § 273.2(j) 7 CFR § 273.18(a)
heading: 1400.10 Last Update
