metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body:
New information in the manual is highlighted and noted with a . See Manual Updates and for details.

Section with expandable text are noted with a throughout the manual.
heading: 1500 Resources

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body:
Use countable resources at the time the household is interviewed to determine eligibility.

Households that are SCE or ECE are resource eligible.

Exclude resources of any household member if that member receives any of the following: SSI /AABD PSE Vermont EITC Reach Ahead Reach First Reach Up TANF-funded referral services

Note : TANF-funded referral services benefit the entire household. This includes ineligible and disqualified members who made the household ineligible for SCE or ECE.
heading: 1500.1 Determining Resource Eligibility

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body:
The remainder of this section applies only to households with a Senior or Disabled Disabled member with Gross Income greater than 185% of the FPL. A person who meets one or more of the following: Receives disability or blindness benefits from any of these programs: Social Security, SSI , or MABD . A person meets this even when the full benefit is withheld or deferred due to recoupment. Receives a federally or state administered SSI supplement based on disability or blindness, or section 212(a) of PL 93-66. Receives a disability retirement benefit from a government agency for a disability considered permanent by SSA disability considered permanent by SSA . Any of the following conditions: Permanent loss of use of both hands, both feet, or one hand and one foot. Amputation of leg at hip. Amputation of leg or foot because of diabetes or peripheral vascular diseases. Total deafness, not correctable by surgery or hearing aid. Statutory blindness, unless caused by cataracts or detached retina. IQ of 59 or less, established after the person becomes 16 years old. Spinal cord or nerve root lesions resulting in paraplegia or quadriplegia. Multiple sclerosis with damage to the nervous system caused by scattered areas of inflammation. The inflammation recurs and has progressed to varied interference with the function of the nervous system, including severe muscle weakness, paralysis, and vision and speech defects. Muscular dystrophy with irreversible wasting of the muscles, impairing the ability to use the arms or legs. Impaired renal function caused by chronic renal disease, resulting in severely reduced function which may require dialysis or kidney transplant. Amputation of a limb of a person at least 55 years old. Is a veteran the VA considers totally (100%) disabled or permanently housebound or in need of regular aid and attendance. Is a veteran’s surviving spouse who the VA considers: in need of regular aid and attendance, or permanently housebound, or approved for benefits because of the veteran’s death and has a disability considered permanent by SSA. Is a veteran’s surviving child who the VA considers: incapable of self-support, or approved for benefits because of the veteran’s death and has a disability considered permanent by SSA

Households are not eligible for 3SVT benefits if countable resources total more than $4,500. Exception : If the only Senior or Disabled Disabled member is ineligible or disqualified , the limit is $3,000 A person who meets one or more of the following: Receives disability or blindness benefits from any of these programs: Social Security, SSI , or MABD . A person meets this even when the full benefit is withheld or deferred due to recoupment. Receives a federally or state administered SSI supplement based on disability or blindness, or section 212(a) of PL 93-66. Receives a disability retirement benefit from a government agency for a disability considered permanent by SSA disability considered permanent by SSA . Any of the following conditions: Permanent loss of use of both hands, both feet, or one hand and one foot. Amputation of leg at hip. Amputation of leg or foot because of diabetes or peripheral vascular diseases. Total deafness, not correctable by surgery or hearing aid. Statutory blindness, unless caused by cataracts or detached retina. IQ of 59 or less, established after the person becomes 16 years old. Spinal cord or nerve root lesions resulting in paraplegia or quadriplegia. Multiple sclerosis with damage to the nervous system caused by scattered areas of inflammation. The inflammation recurs and has progressed to varied interference with the function of the nervous system, including severe muscle weakness, paralysis, and vision and speech defects. Muscular dystrophy with irreversible wasting of the muscles, impairing the ability to use the arms or legs. Impaired renal function caused by chronic renal disease, resulting in severely reduced function which may require dialysis or kidney transplant. Amputation of a limb of a person at least 55 years old. Is a veteran the VA considers totally (100%) disabled or permanently housebound or in need of regular aid and attendance. Is a veteran’s surviving spouse who the VA considers: in need of regular aid and attendance, or permanently housebound, or approved for benefits because of the veteran’s death and has a disability considered permanent by SSA. Is a veteran’s surviving child who the VA considers: incapable of self-support, or approved for benefits because of the veteran’s death and has a disability considered permanent by SSA

Note: Households who are not categorical eligible have a resource limit of $3,000 or $4,500 for households with a senior or disabled member.
heading: 1500.2 Resource Limits

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: Count resources of households with a Senior or Disabled Disabled household member with income greater than 185% of the FPL, including the ineligible and disqualified household members. A person who meets one or more of the following: Receives disability or blindness benefits from any of these programs: Social Security, SSI , or MABD . A person meets this even when the full benefit is withheld or deferred due to recoupment. Receives a federally or state administered SSI supplement based on disability or blindness, or section 212(a) of PL 93-66. Receives a disability retirement benefit from a government agency for a disability considered permanent by SSA disability considered permanent by SSA . Any of the following conditions: Permanent loss of use of both hands, both feet, or one hand and one foot. Amputation of leg at hip. Amputation of leg or foot because of diabetes or peripheral vascular diseases. Total deafness, not correctable by surgery or hearing aid. Statutory blindness, unless caused by cataracts or detached retina. IQ of 59 or less, established after the person becomes 16 years old. Spinal cord or nerve root lesions resulting in paraplegia or quadriplegia. Multiple sclerosis with damage to the nervous system caused by scattered areas of inflammation. The inflammation recurs and has progressed to varied interference with the function of the nervous system, including severe muscle weakness, paralysis, and vision and speech defects. Muscular dystrophy with irreversible wasting of the muscles, impairing the ability to use the arms or legs. Impaired renal function caused by chronic renal disease, resulting in severely reduced function which may require dialysis or kidney transplant. Amputation of a limb of a person at least 55 years old. Is a veteran the VA considers totally (100%) disabled or permanently housebound or in need of regular aid and attendance. Is a veteran’s surviving spouse who the VA considers: in need of regular aid and attendance, or permanently housebound, or approved for benefits because of the veteran’s death and has a disability considered permanent by SSA. Is a veteran’s surviving child who the VA considers: incapable of self-support, or approved for benefits because of the veteran’s death and has a disability considered permanent by SSA
heading: 1500.3.1 Countable Resources

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body:
Do not count resources of the following people: Boarders, unless included in the household Ineligible students Residents of commercial boarding houses Roomers Live-in attendants SSI/Senior Cash Out recipients Residents of institutions Strikers Other people living with the household who: are not mandatory household members , and do not purchase and prepare meals with the household.

For a full description, see Non-members .
heading: 1500.3.2 Non-member Resources

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body:
Count the equity value of all countable resources unless excluded . The equity value is the fair market value minus: the amount owed on the resource, and the cost to sell or transfer the resource.

Exception : The value of vehicles is determined based on how the vehicle is used. See Vehicles .

The fair market value is the amount of money the resource can be sold for on the open market.
heading: 1500.4.1 Equity Value Determination

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: Liquid resources are resources the household can easily convert to cash, such as: Cash on hand Credit card company gift cards Checking accounts Savings accounts Debit accounts Savings certificates Stocks Bonds Lump sum payments Cryptocurrency
heading: 1500.4.2 Liquid Resources

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body:
Non-liquid resources are resources the household cannot easily convert to cash, such as: Personal property Buildings Land Other property not listed under Excluded Resources Recreational vehicles, such as, but not limited to: Boats Four wheelers Snowmobiles Vacation homes, such as, but not limited to: Cabins Campers Mobile homes Travel trailers

Vehicles unless excluded.
heading: 1500.4.3 Non-liquid Resources

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body:
Resources owned by two or more households, or resources showing more than one name are entirely available to each household.

Exception: Exclude jointly owned resources: the household verifies all or part are inaccessible; that cannot be subdivided, and the household cannot access or sell the resource unless the joint owner approves; or are jointly owned by: people living in a shelter for battered persons and someone living in the abuser’s household, and the resource is controlled by the person living in the abuser’s household.

Households may claim less than full ownership or even no ownership of jointly owned resources, such as a bank account. Verify the household’s claim. Based on the verification obtained, determine if the resource is: counted, partially excluded, or fully excluded.

Supervisor approval is required if a jointly owned resource is partially or fully excluded. The owners of the resource must sign a statement attesting to the fact that: the person’s name is on the asset solely for a specified reason, and the person owns none or less than the full resource amount.
heading: 1500.4.4 Jointly Owned Resources

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body:
Excluded resources commingled with countable resources remain excluded for six months from the date the resources are commingled.

Exceptions: Prorated income, such as self-employment or student income. Prorated income remains an excluded resource for the entire proration period. EITC are excluded for a specific period of time, regardless if the money is commingled. See Excluded Resources, Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC). Federal Income Tax Refunds.
heading: 1500.4.5 Commingled Funds

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: Exclude the following resources:
heading: 1500.5.1 Resources to be Excluded

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: Achieving a Better Life Experience savings accounts that provide secure funding for disability-related expenses on behalf of designated beneficiaries deemed disabled disabled before age 26. Section 529A of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986.
heading: 1500.5.2 ABLE Accounts

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: One burial plot for each household member.
heading: 1500.5.3 Burial Plot

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: Exclude funds in a burial trust.
heading: 1500.5.4 Burial Trusts

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: The value of a Cafeteria Plan if the household cannot withdraw funds from the plan.
heading: 1500.5.5 Cafeteria Plans

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: Treat conservatorship funds the same as an irrevocable trust. See Trust Funds.
heading: 1500.5.6 Conservatorship Funds

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: Exclude funds until they are accessible to the household. Examples: Go Fund Me, Kickstarter, Indiegogo and Tilt
heading: 1500.5.7 Crowdfunding

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: Payments from a crime victim compensation program funded under the Victims of Crime Act of 1984.
heading: 1500.5.8 Crime Victim’s Payments

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body:
Income for the current month, even if deposited into a checking or savings account. This includes prorated income during the months it is intended to cover. See Determining Income and Deductions.

When determining resources at application, subtract all income received in the month, up to and including the date of interview.
heading: 1500.5.9 Current Month’s Income

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body:
All plans with funds contributed by the employer or the employee. Exclude as long as the funds remain in a deferred compensation plan, regardless: if accessible, or whether the person is still employed.

See Pension Plans/Retirement Accounts .
heading: 1500.5.10 Deferred Compensation Plans

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: Disaster and emergency assistance payments as provided under the Disaster Relief Act of 1974, and as amended by Public Law 100-107, the Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Amendments of 1988. See Disaster Assistance.
heading: 1500.5.11 Disaster and Emergency Assistance Payments

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: EITC payments are excluded as follows: Federal EITC payments under section 3507 of the Internal Revenue Code for the month of receipt and the following month. Federal, State or local EITC for 12-months if the household: participated in SNAP /3SVT when the EITC was received, and continues to participate during the 12-month period. Breaks of one month or less are not non-participation.
heading: 1500.5.12 Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC)

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body:
Money in qualified tuition programs under Internal Revenue Code, Sections: 529, (the Vermont Higher Education Investment Plan), or 530, Coverdell Education Savings Account.

Money deposited into non-tax exempt accounts is not excluded.

See Students .
heading: 1500.5.13 Education Accounts

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: Vermont Seasonal Fuel Assistance Program payments (including the $21 payment).
heading: 1500.5.14 Energy Assistance Payments

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: Property needed for the employment or self-employment of a household member.
heading: 1500.5.15 Employment Property

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body:
Property needed for self-employment farming including, but not limited to: Land Equipment Supplies Tractors

Exclude self-employment farming property for one year after the household stops self-employment farming.
heading: 1500.5.16 Farm Property

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: Exclude as a resource for 12-months after receipt.
heading: 1500.5.17 Federal Income Tax Refunds

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body:
Federal payments excluded by law include but are not limited to: Federal tax refunds, Public Law 112-240, exclude for 12-months; AmeriCorps Payments; Domestic Volunteers Services Act payments, Public Law 93-113; Uniform Relocation Assistance and Real Property Acquisition Policy Act of 1970 reimbursements, Public Law 91-646, Section 216; Agent Orange Settlement, Public Laws 101 and 201; Nazi Persecution, Public Law 103-286; Student income under: Title IV of the Higher Education Act, or Bureau of Indian Affairs; Special Supplemental Food Program for Women, Infants, and Children (WIC) benefits, Public Law 92-433, Section 9; Japanese and Aleuts Wartime Relocation of Civilians payments, Public Law 100-383; Radiation Exposure Compensation Act payments, Public Law 101-425; Children of Women Vietnam Veterans Born with Certain Birth Defects payments, Public Law 106-419.

For more information, see Types of Income .
heading: 1500.5.18 Federal Payments

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: The value of a flexible benefit plan (Cafeteria Plan) if the household cannot withdraw funds from the plan.
heading: 1500.5.19 Flexible Benefit Plan

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: Exclude the value of burial trusts.
heading: 1500.5.20 Funeral Contract

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: Exclude establishment specific gift cards.
heading: 1500.5.21 Gift Cards

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body:
The home the household lives in and the Surrounding Property. Homestead property includes rights of way, such as roads running through the property. Exception: Count any part of the household’s equity value of property separated from the home and lot by another person’s property.

A partially completed home, unless the household: owns, or is buying a home elsewhere.

A lot the household plans to build a home on, unless the household: owns, or is buying a home elsewhere.

Buildings on the property, other than homes.

The homestead remains excluded when the household is temporarily away from home due to: Employment Training for future employment Illness The home and lot are unlivable due to Household Misfortune

If the homestead contains two or more homes, exclude the home where the household lives. Exclude only one home at a time, unless the home meets another exclusion.
heading: 1500.5.22 Homestead

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: Household Goods and Personal Effects, regardless of their value.
heading: 1500.5.23 Household Goods/Personal Effects

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: Money held in a family self-sufficiency account as long as the money is not withdrawn. Treat as a non-recurring lump sum when the money is withdrawn.
heading: 1500.5.24 HUD Family Self-Sufficiency Program

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: Resources the household cannot access, such as, but not limited to: Security deposits Crowdfunding accounts Prepaid irrevocable burial trust Prepaid irrevocable funeral contract Certain vehicles (See Vehicles) Property in probate Unpaid portion of loans owed to a household member Certain trust funds (See Trust Funds) Resources discovered by the agency but unknown to the household Real property the household is making a good effort to sell Certain jointly owned resources (See Jointly Owned Resources) Resources with a lien prohibiting the household from selling Resources with an Equity Value of $1,500 or less Exception: Does not include: Stocks, Bonds, or Negotiable financial instruments.
heading: 1500.5.25 Inaccessible Resources

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: Property annually producing income consistent with the property’s Fair Market Value, even if only used on a seasonal basis. Income producing property includes but is not limited to: Rental homes Vacation homes
heading: 1500.5.26 Income Producing Property

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: Individual Development Account is an optional, interest-bearing account, through Community National Bank. IDAs encourage people to save for long term goals without the savings affecting their 3SVT eligibility or benefit amount. The IDA program matches every $1 saved with $2, up to $2,000.
heading: 1500.5.27 Individual Development Accounts (IDA)

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: The value of installment contracts for land or buildings if: the contract produces income consistent with the fair market value of the property, or is held as security in exchange for a purchase price consistent with the Fair Market Value of the property.
heading: 1500.5.28 Installment Sales Contracts

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body:
A leased vehicle is not a resource to the household during the time the vehicle is under contract, even if part of the lease payments are applied to the principal. The vehicle becomes a resource at the end of the contract if retained.

Note: Add CARS panel to STATs for a leased vehicle. Be sure the panel is not countable and noted as a leased vehicle.
heading: 1500.5.29 Leased Vehicles

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: The cash value of a life insurance policy.
heading: 1500.5.30 Life Insurance Policy Cash Value

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: Loans for business purposes that legally bans the household from using for a reason other than the business.
heading: 1500.5.31 Loans

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: Native American gambling payments made to children placed in an inaccessible trust.
heading: 1500.5.32 Native American Gambling Operations

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: Land that: is held jointly with the tribe, or can only be sold with the approval of the Bureau of Indian Affairs.
heading: 1500.5.33 Native American Tribal Land

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body:
Funds from judgment awards due to breaches of treaty provisions, and funds held in trusts by the Secretary of the Interior. Native American tribal judgment funds are not from Native American gambling operations. The funds come from the sale or lease of: oil, gas, and other tribal assets, such as land.

Native American tribal judgment funds payments include, but are not limited to: Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act Sac and Fox Indian Claims Agreement Public Law 94-114, Section 6, regarding sub-marginal land held in trust by the United States Relocation assistance to members of the Hopi and Navajo Tribes under Public Law 93-531 Disposition of funds to the Grand River Band of Ottawa Indians, Public Law 94-540 Confederated Tribes and Bands of the Yakim Indian Nation from the Indian Claims Commission, Public Law 95-433, Section 2 Confederated Tribes and Bands of the Apache Tribe of the Mescalero Reservation from the Indian Claims Commission, Public Law 95-433, Section 2 Passamaguoddy Tribe or any of the members pursuant to the Maine Indian Claims Settlement Act of 1980, Public Law 96-420, Section 5. Penobscot Nation or any of the members pursuant to the Maine Indian Claims Settlement Act of 1980, Public Law 96-420, Section 5

Exclude payments of: $2,000 or less for each household member, per payment, per calendar year, and any amount placed in an inaccessible trust.
heading: 1500.5.34 Native American Tribal Judgment Funds

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: Other resources sold under contract by installment payments, such as personal goods.
heading: 1500.5.35 Other Resources Sold Under Installment Contract

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: Money deposited in a special PASS account.
heading: 1500.5.36 Plan for Achieving Self-Support (PASS) Account

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body:
All tax-exempt plans or accounts with funds contributed by the employer or the employee. Exclude as long as the funds remain in a deferred compensation plan, even if accessible, and regardless of whether the person is still employed. See Deferred Compensation Plans.

Pension plans/retirement accounts include, but are not limited to: IRA SEP Keogh Plan 401K SIMPLE 401 (k)

Accounts under Internal Revenue Code of 1986, Sections 403(a) 403(b) 408 408A 457(b) 501(c)(18)

Money in a non-tax exempt pension plan or retirement account is not excluded.
heading: 1500.5.37 Pension Plans/Retirement Accounts

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: The value of pets and property used for the pet, such as but not limited to: Saddles Bridles Leashes
heading: 1500.5.38 Pets and Property Used for the Pet

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body:
Property needed to maintain an excluded vehicle. Exclude only the piece of the land used for storage or repair of the vehicle. The vehicle must be used for one of the following reasons: Produce income more than 50 percent of the time it is used. Produce income consistent with its Fair Market Value, even if only used on a seasonal basis. For long distance travel (not daily commuting) needed for the employment of a household member or another person whose resources are available to the household. Used as the household’s home. Transport a physically Disabled household member. Carry the primary source of fuel for heating for the household. Carry the primary source of water for the household.

For a full description, see Excluded Vehicles .
heading: 1500.5.39 Property to Maintain Excluded Vehicles

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: The value of resources under a lien or security agreement placed as a condition for a business loan. The security or lien agreement must prohibit the household from selling the resources.
heading: 1500.5.40 Resources Under a Lien

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body:
Student income received under programs in Title IV of the Higher Education Act or under Bureau of Indian Affairs student assistance programs. These include but are not limited to: BEOG s (BEOG or PELL Grants) Presidential Access Scholarships (Super Pell Grants) SEOGs SSIGs Federal Family Education Loan Program (Formally GSL) Supplemental Loans for Students PLUS Loans for parents Robert T. Stafford Student Loans Direct Federal Loans to students in institutions of higher education Perkins Loans, formerly NDSL Federal Work Study Funds Note: Not all Federal work study funds come under Title IV of the Higher Education Act Trio Grants Upward Bound Student Support Services Robert E. McNair Post-Baccalaureate Achievement Robert C. Byrd Honors Scholarship Program CAMP for students whose families are engaged in migrant and seasonal farm work HEP National Early Intervention Scholarship and Partnership Program

Exclude all loans and scholarships received under Titles III, VII and VIII of the Public Service Health Act. These include, but are not limited to: National Health Service Corps NURSE Corps Scholarship Program Scholarships for Disadvantaged Students Loans for Disadvantaged Students Health Professions Student Loans Nursing Student Loans Primary Care Loans NIH Undergraduate Scholarship NIH Clinical Research Loan Repayment Indian Health Service Scholarships Indian Health Service Loan Repayment Native Hawaiian Scholarship Program
heading: 1500.5.41 Student Income

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body:
Trust funds and income from trust funds that are inaccessible to the household. To be inaccessible, all of the following rules must be met: During the certification period: the trust arrangement is not likely to end, and no household member has the authority to end the trust arrangement or change the beneficiary.

The trustee handling the trust must be a: Court appointee with court-imposed limits placed on the use of funds, or Institution, court, corporation, or organization not owned by or under the control of a household member. Trust investments must not directly involve or help any business or corporation under the control, direction or influence of a household member. The irrevocable trust must be established from: non-household funds by a non-household member, or the household’s funds, if the trustee uses the funds solely to: make investments on behalf of the trust; or pay the educational expenses of any person named by the household creating the trust; or pay the medical expenses of any person named by the household creating the trust.

Types of trusts include, but are not limited to: Federal Thrift Savings Plans, Section 8439, Title 5, United States Code Student income accounts in a qualified tuition programs under Internal Revenue Code, Sections: 529 (the Vermont Higher Education Investment Plan), or 530 Coverdell Education Savings Account
heading: 1500.5.42 Trust Funds

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body:
The value of US Savings Bonds that cannot be cashed in.

Count the amount of bonds that can be cashed in based on the bond's worth at the time of the determination, not on the bond's face value.
heading: 1500.5.43 US Savings Bonds

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body:
A vehicle means any motorized means of transportation that requires a Vermont vehicle registration. Vehicles which do not require a Vermont registration are counted based on total fair market value. Exception: Treat unregistered vehicles on Native American tribal reservations not requiring a Vermont registration to be driven by tribal members as vehicles requiring Vermont registration.

Vehicles include but are not limited to: Cars Trucks Vans Motorcycles Snowmobiles Four wheelers Motor homes Boats

Vehicles may be: excluded, counted based on the Equity Value, counted based on Fair Market Value over $4,650, or counted based on whichever is higher: the fair market value over $4,650 or the equity value.
heading: 1500.6.1 Definition of Vehicles

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body:
Exclude vehicles for one of these reasons only : The entire value of one registered or unregistered operable vehicle per adult. The entire value of one registered or unregistered operable vehicle per child of driving age when needed to attend school or work. The entire value of any remaining registered vehicle if it: Produces income more than 50 percent of the time it is used. Produces income consistent with its Fair Market Value , even if only used on a seasonal basis. Is used for long distance travel (not daily commuting) needed for employment of a household member or another person whose resources are available to the household. Examples: Traveling sales person, Migrant Worker following the work stream. Is used as the household’s home. See Homestead. Transports a physically disabled person for any reason. Exclude one vehicle per physically disabled person. The vehicle does not require special equipment to be excluded. Carries the primary source of heating fuel for the household. Carries the primary source of water for the household. Inaccessible because its Equity Value is $1,500 or less. Has a Fair Market Value less than $4,650. Exclusion applies to one vehicle per adult. If the fair market value is over $4,650 see Determining the Countable Value. Has a Fair Market Value less than $4,650 and is used by a household member under age 18 to drive to or from: Work, Training for education to prepare for work, or To look for work. If the Fair Market Value is over $4,650 see Determining the Countable Value.

These exclusions also apply: when the vehicle is not in use during periods of temporary unemployment, and to unregistered vehicles on Indian reservations not requiring a Vermont registration to be driven by tribal members.
heading: 1500.6.2 Excluded Vehicles

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: The following sections address the amount to count for non-excluded vehicles: Determining the Fair Market Value Determining the Countable Value Jointly Owned Vehicles
heading: 1500.6.3.1 Determining Value of Non-Excluded Vehicles

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body:
When determining the Fair Market Value (FMV), use the wholesale or average trade-in value, not the retail value. Do not adjust the value based on: mileage, options, or special equipment.

FMV is usually found from vehicle pricing sources, such as the NADA Used Car Guide.

Accept the value the household gives for older vehicles no longer listed in the NADA book, unless the: estimated value is questionable, and the value will affect the household’s eligibility.

Verify new car or questionable FMVs not listed in the NADA book using other reputable sources, such as, but not limited to a car dealership.

Verify the FMV using a reliable source familiar with these types of vehicles: classic, antique, or custom built.

Allow the household the opportunity to provide proof of a lower FMV when the household disputes the assigned value.

CATN your source for the FMV used in your determination, as values may change in the future.
heading: 1500.6.3.2 Determining Fair Market Value (FMV)

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: Follow the steps below to determine the countable value of vehicles not excluded. Count towards the household’s resource limit the amount exceeding $4,650 fair market value for the following vehicles: One vehicle per adult household member. Any other vehicle used by a household member under age 18 to drive to or from: work, training for education to prepare for work, or to look for work. Determine the countable value of all other non-excluded vehicles. Determine the fair market value amount greater than $4,650 of each registered vehicle. Determine the Equity Value amount for the registered vehicles and any unregistered vehicles. Count towards the household’s resource limit whichever amount is greater: the fair market value greater than $4,650, or the equity value.
heading: 1500.6.3.3 Determining the Countable Value

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body:
Exclude jointly owned vehicles that are inaccessible following the Jointly Owned Resources Exception policy.

Follow the steps below to determine the value of accessible jointly owned vehicles: Determine the household’s share based on Jointly Owned Resources policy. Using the Fair Market Value policy, subtract $4,650 per vehicle, before determining the household’s countable share. Using the Equity Value policy, count only the household’s share of the vehicle’s equity.
heading: 1500.6.3.4 Jointly Owned Vehicles

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: Verify if countable resources were transferred meeting allowable transfer policy. Ineligible transfers of resources disqualify the entire household for up to 12-months.
heading: 1500.7.1 Allowable Transfer Policy Requirement

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: Allowable resource transfers are those that: Are made by categorically eligible households. Would not affect eligibility, such as excluded personal property. Are sold or traded at or near Fair Market Value . Are transferred between members of the household, including ineligible and disqualified people. Are transferred for reasons other than to qualify or attempt to qualify for 3SVT, such as putting money in a student income account.
heading: 1500.7.2 Allowable Transfers

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: Ineligible resource transfers occur at application if the: resources are transferred within the three-month period before the date of applying for 3SVT, and the transfer was done solely to qualify for 3SVT, or during the current certification period if done solely to remain eligible for 3SVT.
heading: 1500.7.3 Ineligible Transfers

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body:
An applicant household’s disqualification period begins the month of application.

An active household’s’ disqualification period begins the month following the month timely notice expires, unless the household requested: a Fair Hearing, and continued benefits pending the hearing decision.

Use the following steps to determine the disqualification period: Add the value of the countable transferred resources to the value of the household’s other countable resources. Subtract the appropriate resource limit for the household from the amount in step 1. Compare the result to the table below to determine the period the household is disqualified from receiving 3SVT benefits. Notify the household of their disqualification, and how long the disqualification will last. Disqualification Period Table. Amount in Excess of Resource Limit Disqualification Period $0 – 249.99 One month $250 – 999.99 Three months $1,000 – 2,999.99 Six months $3,000 – 4,999.99 Nine months $5,000 or more Twelve months Changes in the household’s situation do not change or end the disqualification period.
heading: 1500.7.4 Determining the Disqualification Period

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: Verify the following: The total countable resource value when it is within $200 of the maximum resource limit; Exception: Do not verify for 3SquaresVT in a SNAP households. Transferred resources; Inaccessible resources; Jointly owned inaccessible resources. Verify the household: can only access a portion, and the value of the portion; or cannot subdivide the property without the joint owner’s approval. Inaccessible real property. Verify the household is making a good faith effort to sell the property at a reasonable price. Use collateral contacts and documents, such as but not limited to: Newspaper ads Real estate broker listings Vehicle’s fair market value when the household disputes the assigned value; New or older vehicles whose values are not available through usual sources, such as the NADA book; Fair Market Value for the following vehicles: classic, antique, or custom built. Questionable resource information.
heading: 1500.8.1 Resources Requiring Verification

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body:
Verify when the resource amount is within $200 of the maximum for non-3SquaresVT in a SNAP households, or if questionable. Deny the application if not verified by the due date.

Postpone verification for expedited service month(s), unless verification can be provided in time to meet the seven-day standard of promptness.
heading: 1500.8.2 Application

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1500 Resources

body: Verify when the resource amount is within $200 of the maximum for non-3SquaresVT in a SNAP households, or if questionable. Deny the recertification application if not verified by the due date.
heading: 1500.8.3 Recertification

metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
New information in the manual is highlighted and noted with a . See Manual Updates and for details.

Section with expandable text are noted with a throughout the manual.
heading: 1600 Income

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
To determine a household’s eligibility and benefit amount you must count all Earned Income and Unearned Income not specifically excluded in this item.

Households that contain a Senior or Disabled Disabled member must only meet the net income eligibility standards for the 3SVT program. Households that do not contain a senior or disabled member must meet both the net income and gross income eligibility standards for the 3SVT program. See Income Limits and Maximum Benefit Table . A person who meets one or more of the following: Receives disability or blindness benefits from any of these programs: Social Security, SSI , or MABD . A person meets this even when the full benefit is withheld or deferred due to recoupment. Receives a federally or state administered SSI supplement based on disability or blindness, or section 212(a) of PL 93-66. Receives a disability retirement benefit from a government agency for a disability considered permanent by SSA disability considered permanent by SSA . Any of the following conditions: Permanent loss of use of both hands, both feet, or one hand and one foot. Amputation of leg at hip. Amputation of leg or foot because of diabetes or peripheral vascular diseases. Total deafness, not correctable by surgery or hearing aid. Statutory blindness, unless caused by cataracts or detached retina. IQ of 59 or less, established after the person becomes 16 years old. Spinal cord or nerve root lesions resulting in paraplegia or quadriplegia. Multiple sclerosis with damage to the nervous system caused by scattered areas of inflammation. The inflammation recurs and has progressed to varied interference with the function of the nervous system, including severe muscle weakness, paralysis, and vision and speech defects. Muscular dystrophy with irreversible wasting of the muscles, impairing the ability to use the arms or legs. Impaired renal function caused by chronic renal disease, resulting in severely reduced function which may require dialysis or kidney transplant. Amputation of a limb of a person at least 55 years old. Is a veteran the VA considers totally (100%) disabled or permanently housebound or in need of regular aid and attendance. Is a veteran’s surviving spouse who the VA considers: in need of regular aid and attendance, or permanently housebound, or approved for benefits because of the veteran’s death and has a disability considered permanent by SSA. Is a veteran’s surviving child who the VA considers: incapable of self-support, or approved for benefits because of the veteran’s death and has a disability considered permanent by SSA

The income tests are applied at application, recertification and whenever there is a change that causes redetermination of eligibility.

The following section lists in alphabetical order the most frequent types of income and how to treat them.
heading: 1600.1 Determination of Household Income

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
Exclude all income directly deposited into or transferred into the account. Verify contributions to the account prior to excluding the income.

Achieving a Better Life Experience savings accounts provide secure funding for disability-related expenses on behalf of designated beneficiaries deemed Disabled Disabled before age 26. Section 529A of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986. A person who meets one or more of the following: Receives disability or blindness benefits from any of these programs: Social Security, SSI , or MABD . A person meets this even when the full benefit is withheld or deferred due to recoupment. Receives a federally or state administered SSI supplement based on disability or blindness, or section 212(a) of PL 93-66. Receives a disability retirement benefit from a government agency for a disability considered permanent by SSA disability considered permanent by SSA . Any of the following conditions: Permanent loss of use of both hands, both feet, or one hand and one foot. Amputation of leg at hip. Amputation of leg or foot because of diabetes or peripheral vascular diseases. Total deafness, not correctable by surgery or hearing aid. Statutory blindness, unless caused by cataracts or detached retina. IQ of 59 or less, established after the person becomes 16 years old. Spinal cord or nerve root lesions resulting in paraplegia or quadriplegia. Multiple sclerosis with damage to the nervous system caused by scattered areas of inflammation. The inflammation recurs and has progressed to varied interference with the function of the nervous system, including severe muscle weakness, paralysis, and vision and speech defects. Muscular dystrophy with irreversible wasting of the muscles, impairing the ability to use the arms or legs. Impaired renal function caused by chronic renal disease, resulting in severely reduced function which may require dialysis or kidney transplant. Amputation of a limb of a person at least 55 years old. Is a veteran the VA considers totally (100%) disabled or permanently housebound or in need of regular aid and attendance. Is a veteran’s surviving spouse who the VA considers: in need of regular aid and attendance, or permanently housebound, or approved for benefits because of the veteran’s death and has a disability considered permanent by SSA. Is a veteran’s surviving child who the VA considers: incapable of self-support, or approved for benefits because of the veteran’s death and has a disability considered permanent by SSA
heading: 1600.2.1 ABLE Accounts

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
Count as unearned income.

Adoption subsidies are payments made to adoptive parents. These payments come from federal funds and are based on need. The Adoption Assistance Agreement shows the subsidy. There is a separate agreement for each child. The agreement may be amended from time-to-time.
heading: 1600.2.2 Adoption Subsidy

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
Exclude payments received from the Agent Orange Settlement Fund or any other fund established for the settlement in the In Re Agent Orange product liability litigation, M.D.L. No. 381 (E.D.N.Y.) only if the payment is by the Aetna Insurance Company.

See Veteran’s Benefits for payments for disabilities based on Agent Orange exposure.
heading: 1600.2.3 Agent Orange Settlement Fund

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
Count as unearned income any alimony payments (court-ordered or voluntary) received from someone outside the household, excluding any fees withheld. Voluntary support payments are payments that are not legally ordered. They include payments made over and above the court-ordered amount. See Costs Withheld From Unearned Income .

Exclude voluntary support payments that are paid to a third party for a household expense. These are vendor payments and are not legally obligated to the household.
heading: 1600.2.4 Alimony Payments

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
Exclude payments and allowances from AmeriCorps State, NCCC , without exception.

Note : The Dream program is an AmeriCorps National program.
heading: 1600.2.5 AmeriCorps Payments (State, National and NCCC )

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
Exclude payments and allowances from AmeriCorps VISTA (formerly known as VISTA) if the person was receiving one of the following benefits when they joined AmeriCorps VISTA: SNAP , TANF , SSI , or State SSI Supplementary Income

Count as earned income if a person begins receiving SNAP benefits after joining AmeriCorps VISTA.

AmeriCorps Vista join date is the start date of the actual contract and not the date of the offer letter.

Note : When joined and application date are the same, consider the customer as receiving benefits.
heading: 1600.2.6 AmeriCorps VISTA Payments

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
Count payments from an annuity as unearned income. An annuity is purchased from an insurance company or by an employer as consideration for services provided. It may be paid yearly or at other specified intervals.

Note : Annuities should be averaged over the period of time they are intended to cover. Example: annuities that are paid annually should be averaged over 12-months. See Determining Income and Deductions .
heading: 1600.2.7 Annuities

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body: Count income from the sale of blood plasma as earned income. The plasma center is the employer.
heading: 1600.2.8 Blood Plasma Sale

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body: Count income from boarders as earned income (RBIN).
heading: 1600.2.9 Boarder Income

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
Count as earned income if received while the person is employed.

Note : If paid less often than monthly see Income Received Less Often Than Monthly

Count as follows if received after employment has ended: Paid as a single payment, exclude as a lump sum (nonrecurring) Paid in more than one payment, count as unearned income
heading: 1600.2.10 Bonuses

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
Count cafeteria or flexible benefit plans as earned income when: An amount for the plan is withheld from the employee’s gross wages even if the employee loses any money left over at the end of the year. A refund is issued to the employee because all the money for benefits covered by an employer-funded plan were not used.

Exclude employer contributions into the plan at the time they are made.

Note : These benefits are not displayed in the same way on all pay stubs. The best source of information regarding them is the employer.

Exclude all funds in a Medicare flexible plan or Medicare spendable plan.

Cafeteria or flexible benefits plans use either the employee’s or employer’s money to pay certain expenses, such as child care, medical expenses, health insurance, annual leave or sick leave.
heading: 1600.2.11 Cafeteria or Flexible Benefit Plans

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
Exclude cash donations based on need when the household gets them from one or more private, nonprofit, charitable organizations. Do not exclude more than $300 per federal fiscal quarter. Count any amount over the $300 limit as unearned income.

Exception : Crowdfunding accounts are treated differently than cash donations.
heading: 1600.2.12 Cash Donations

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
Chaffee Funds are given to youth aging out of foster care by the Vermont Youth Development Program.

Count as unearned income any recurring payments given to the household.

Exclude non-recurring lump sum payments and vendor payments .
heading: 1600.2.13 Chaffee Funds

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
Exclude the earned income of any member of the household age 17 or younger who both : Lives with a natural, adoptive, or stepparent or under the parental control of a household member other than a parent. See Minor Under Parental Control . Is a student at least half time attending elementary or high school, home schooling or a GED program recognized, operated or supervised by the state or local school district.

Continue to exclude the earnings during a temporary break in school attendance (like a semester or vacation break), as long as the child will go back to school after break.

Exclude earnings for the month the student turns 18, for both applicant and participant households.

Do not exclude the income if the student is living alone or living with others but not under Parental Control .

If it is not possible to separate out the child’s earnings or amount of work performed from that of other household members, prorate the total earnings equally among the working members, and exclude the child’s pro rata share.
heading: 1600.2.14 Child’s Earnings

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
Count child support (court-ordered or voluntary), excluding any fees withheld, as unearned income to the child for whom the payment is intended. Voluntary support payments are payments that are not legally ordered. They include payments made over and above the court-ordered amount.

For initial applications, count the child support received in the prior 30 days in the month of application and the following month. After that, count the child support received two months prior to.

Count, as unearned income, child support that is released to the 3SVT household for a Reach Up participant if: the amount is paid because it was over the Reach Up assistance grant; or the amount is paid on a regular schedule for months that Reach Up was not received. See also Lump Sum (Nonrecurring) .

Count, as unearned income, child support that is released to the 3SVT household for a Reach Ahead participant. See Adding Child Support Income for 3SVT and Reach Ahead Cases .

Exclude voluntary support payments that are paid to a third party for a household expense. These are vendor payments and are not legally obligated to the household.

Exclude arrearage payments as a lump sum (nonrecurring) unless they are received on a regular basis.

Exclude child support payments that are transferred to the OCS to maintain Reach Up eligibility. Child support income is excluded as of the date it is assigned.
heading: 1600.2.15 Child Support Income

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
Exclude additional payments received by members of the United States Armed Forces, including the Reserves and National Guard, due to being deployed to or serving in a designated combat zone. Exclude the payments for the duration of the person’s deployment, including hazard pay received after deployment to the combat zone. (Public Law 108-477) See Combat Zones current designated combat zones.

If the person was part of the 3SVT household before deployment, determine the person’s military take-home pay at that time. Count that amount as unearned income if it is less than what the person is making available to the household after deployment.

If the person was not a member of the 3SVT household before deployment, determine the amount of military pay the person made available to the household at that time. Count that amount as unearned income if it is less than what the person is making available to the household after deployment.

If the amount of military income before deployment is more than what is made available to the household after deployment, count as unearned income the amount made available after deployment.
heading: 1600.2.16 Combat Pay

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
Count CWT payments as earned income for 3SVT.

CWT is a therapeutic program of vocational and rehabilitative services under the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA). Unemployed, or underemployed, veterans are placed in a real work environment and are paid for their work via payments from the VA or through the employer.
heading: 1600.2.17 Compensated Work Therapy (CWT)

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
Count as earned income or unearned income depending on the source of the income. Examples : Teacher income is earned income, annuity income is unearned.

Contract income is a fixed salary for a contracted service paid to the earner over the duration of the contract. They are not paid hourly. Examples: teachers or other school employees who receive their annual pay in nine months, web designer who signs a contract to provide services over a certain period with agreed upon compensation. Calculation: Salary from contract divided by number of months contract covers regardless of payment schedule equals gross monthly income. Request a copy of the contract to review its terms, compensation and length.

Contract income paid hourly is normal Wages not contract income.

Contract income is self-employment income if the person works under contract to customers of their business.

Payments to persons who are called subcontractors for the convenience of the employer are not contract income, they are self-employed.
heading: 1600.2.18 Contract Income

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body: Exclude any reasonable income-producing costs withheld from gross unearned income if the income is not legally obligated to the household. “Costs” are the amount actually spent to produce the income, rather than the amount owed. The net amount remaining after deducting these costs is counted as unearned income. This includes, but is not limited to: Agency fees withheld from child support or alimony payments Attorney fees withheld from workers’ compensation
heading: 1600.2.19 Costs Withheld From Unearned Income

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body: Exclude payments received from a crime victim compensation program funded by the Crime Victims fund. (Public Law 103-322)
heading: 1600.2.20 Crime Victim Payments

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body: Exclude payments received from the account. Examples : Go Fund Me, Kickstarter, Indiegogo and Tilt
heading: 1600.2.21 Crowdfunding

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
Exclude the principal portion of payments made to the 3SVT household to repay a loan or promissory note.

Count any interest paid as unearned income.
heading: 1600.2.22 Debts Owed to a Household

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
Count the DCP payments (both the stipend and the room and board) as unearned income when the DCP recipient is included in the 3SVT household. Count the income as the DCP provider’s income. Exception : Exclude room and board payment if made by the DCP recipient.

Exclude the DCP payments (both the stipend and the room and board) to the household member providing the care when the DCP recipient is not included in the 3SVT household.

Note : DCP recipients are considered a foster adult .
heading: 1600.2.23 Difficulty of Care Program (DCP) Payments

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
Count an employee’s disability benefit as unearned income.

Count an employee’s disability payments as earned income when they are paid out of the employer’s funds.
heading: 1600.2.24 Disability Benefits

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
Exclude disaster and emergency assistance payments provided under the Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act, as amended (Public Law 100-707).

This exclusion applies to federal assistance provided to people directly affected and to comparable disaster assistance provided by states, local government, and disaster assistance organizations.

Most FEMA funds are excluded. However, payments made to homeless people to pay rent, mortgage, food and utilities are not excluded under this provision when there is no major disaster or emergency declared by the President.
heading: 1600.2.25 Disaster Assistance

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body: Count as unearned income dividends the household has the option of either receiving as income or reinvesting in a trust or other investment. Regardless of the payment schedule, annualize these dividends.
heading: 1600.2.26 Dividend Income

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
Exclude all payments under Title II.

Title II payments include those made through the National Senior Service Corps, including the RSVP , Foster Grandparents, Senior Companion and others.
heading: 1600.2.27 Domestic Volunteers Services Act Payments, Title II

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
Exclude as income any Federal or Vermont EITC .

These payments supplement the earned income of families with children.
heading: 1600.2.28 Earned Income Tax Credits

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body: Exclude any payment made to an E&T participant for costs that are reasonably necessary and directly related to participation in the Employment and Training Program. These costs may include, but are not limited to: Dependent care costs Transportation Other expenses related to work Training or education Uniforms Necessary equipment Books or training manuals
heading: 1600.2.29 Employment and Training (E&T) Payments

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body: Exclude payments or allowances made under any federal law for providing energy assistance. Exclude payments provided through: Seasonal Fuel Assistance Program (including the $21 payment) The Department of HUD even if the payment is received directly by the participant. The FmHA
heading: 1600.2.30 Energy Assistance

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
Count payments as unearned income.

Note : If a non-household member is paid for the service, the amount paid is an allowable medical expense. See Medical Expenses .

Essential person program payments are for a senior and/or disabled household member who needs in-home help.
heading: 1600.2.31 Essential Person Program Payments

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body: See Senior Community Services Employment Program
heading: 1600.2.32 Experience Works

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body: Exclude all utility payments made by FmHA .
heading: 1600.2.33 Farmers Home Administration Payments

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
Exclude fellowship payments if the following criteria are met: funded under Title IV-C or IV-E of the Higher Education Act; and funds are intended to cover room and board and/or educational expenses.

Count fellowship payments if they are used to cover normal living expenses.
heading: 1600.2.34 Fellowship

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
Exclude Flexible Family Funding if paid as a lump sum or as a reimbursement.

Note: Verification must be provided to confirm the funds were used as a reimbursement .

Count as income if paid over time and not a reimbursement .
heading: 1600.2.35 Flexible Family Funding

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body: Count payments received for participation in a focus group, survey or study unless the payment is a reimbursement or a gift certificate. Whether it is earned income or unearned income depends on how the payment is described by the entity providing it.
heading: 1600.2.36 Focus Group, Survey or Study Income

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
Count the foster care payment as unearned income when a foster child or foster adult is included in the 3SVT household.

Exclude the foster care payment when a foster child or foster adult is not included in the 3SVT household. This includes payments made to a respite provider.

Note : DCP recipients are considered a foster adult.
heading: 1600.2.37 Foster Care Payments

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body: Count gross winnings from gambling as unearned income. Do not offset the winnings with any amount lost.
heading: 1600.2.38 Gambling Winnings

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
Count the General Assistance personal needs payment as unearned income.

Exclude all other General Assistance payments as vendor payments .
heading: 1600.2.39 General and Emergency Assistance

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body: Count a cash gift (other than one specifically excluded under Cash Donations ) as unearned income if it can be anticipated.
heading: 1600.2.40 Gifts

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
Exclude establishment specific gift cards.

Count a credit card company gift card as unearned income if it can be anticipated.
heading: 1600.2.41 Gift Cards

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body: Exclude all utility payments made by the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD).
heading: 1600.2.42 HUD Utility Payments

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body: Exclude all income specifically excluded by any other federal statute. This includes, but is not limited to the following: Payments received under the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act Amendments. (Section 29 of Public Law 92-203 and Section 15 of Public Law 100-241) Payments of relocation assistance to members of the Hopi and Navajo Tribes under Public Law 93-531. Payments made under the Confederate Tribes of the Colville Reservation Grand Coulee Dam Settlement Act. (Public Law 103-436) Payments by the Indian Claims Commission to the Confederated Tribes and Bands of the Yakima Indian Nation or the Apache Tribe of the Mescalero Reservation. (Public Law 95-433) Payments to the Passamaquoddy Tribe, Maliseet Tribe and the Penobscot Nation or any of their members received per the Maine Indian Claims Settlement Act of 1980. (Public Law 96-420) Income received from certain sub marginal land of the United States which is held in trust for certain Indian tribes. (Public Law 94-114) Income received from the disposition of funds to the Grand River Band of Ottawa Indians. (Public Law 94-540) Payments made for children who suffer from birth defects and whose mothers are Vietnam veterans. (Public Law 106-419) Payments made to a child of a Vietnam Veteran for any disability of the child resulting from Spina Bifida. (Public Law 104-204, section 1805(d)) Payments made to Americans of Japanese or Aleut ancestry under Public Law 100-383. Payments made under Public Laws 101 and 201 for Agent Orange Settlement. Payments made to victims of Nazi Persecution under Public Law 102-286. Payments made under the Radiation Exposure Compensation Act to compensate injury or death resulting from exposure to radiation from nuclear testing and uranium mining. (Public Law 101-426)
heading: 1600.2.43 Income Excluded by Federal Statute

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body: Exclude as lump sum (nonrecurring) payments.
heading: 1600.2.44 Income Tax Refund

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
Exclude interest earned on IDA accounts. Also exclude any deposits made into the account by an outside source.

A savings account for lower income persons. An IDA is used for a specific purpose such as education, purchasing a first home, or starting a business. Savings are matched by private or public funds.
heading: 1600.2.45 Individual Development Account (IDA)

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body: Exclude any non-monetary or in-kind gain or benefit provided directly to the household, such as meals, free rent, clothing, housing, or produce from a garden. Example: working off rent is an in-kind benefit.
heading: 1600.2.46 In-Kind Benefits

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
Exclude from income all money received from lump sum (nonrecurring) insurance settlements.

Count recurring payments as unearned income.
heading: 1600.2.47 Insurance Settlements

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body: Count as unearned income.
heading: 1600.2.48 Interest Income

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body: Exclude up to $30 of income per federal fiscal quarter when the income is received too infrequently or irregularly to be reasonably anticipated.
heading: 1600.2.49 Irregular Income

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
Count salary and performance bonuses as earned income for Job Corps participants and former participants who are still receiving Job Corps payments.

Note : Most Job Corps participants are residents of an ineligible Institution , and therefore, not eligible for 3SVT. See Special Household Situations for eligible institutions.

Exclude any lump sum one-time payment of salary from prior periods as a lump sum (nonrecurring) payment. If a performance bonus is received after the person leaves the program and it is paid out in one payment, exclude it from income as a lump sum (nonrecurring).
heading: 1600.2.50 Job Corps

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body: Exclude reimbursements from the employer for job-related expenses including travel expenses and uniform allowances.
heading: 1600.2.51 Job Related Reimbursement

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body: See Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (WIOA) .
heading: 1600.2.52 Job Training Partnership Act

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body: Count compensation for jury duty as earned income unless it is a reimbursement such as for transportation, or meals and lodging.
heading: 1600.2.53 Jury Duty Payments

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
When legally obligated money is earned, count it as earned income.

When it is unearned, count it as unearned income.

Count legally-obligated money when it is garnished or diverted by the provider of the payment to a third party for a household expense. Do not exclude it as a vendor payment .

Legally obligated money includes money such as wages earned by a household member, Social Security, SSI, or a Reach Up payment to which the household is legally entitled, and support or alimony payments that legally must be paid to a household member.

Legally obligated money is different from a vendor payment because legally obligated money belongs to the household. If the money was not diverted, the household would get it. Vendor payments are made with money that does not belong to the household.
heading: 1600.2.54 Legally Obligated Money

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
Exclude loans from private persons and commercial institutions. A legally executed document is not required to verify the income is a loan.

Exception : Deferred payment loans for education. See Student Income .
heading: 1600.2.55 Loans

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body: Exclude payments made under the Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program. See Energy Assistance
heading: 1600.2.56 Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body: Exclude nonrecurring lump-sum payments, including but not limited to, payments received as a one-time payment for: Income tax refunds, rebates, or credits, including Vermont EITC Vermont Child Tax Credit and renter rebate. Retroactive lump sums for the past one month or more, such as Social Security, SSI , Reach Up , Unemployment Benefits, or other retroactive payments. Exception : SSI retroactive benefits may be paid out in more than one payment. Each retroactive payment is a nonrecurring lump sum and is excluded as income. The annual VA disability pension adjustment. Insurance Settlements. Reach Up parenting incentive payments. Refunds of security deposits on rental property or utilities including: A HUD rental refund. From the sale of a household’s countable or exempt resources (such as the household’s homestead, personal effects, household goods, or an automobile) that are not sold as part of a self-employment business. Retroactive pay raise of an employee whether employed or not at the time of receipt. A bonus received after employment has stopped. Back pay that was not withheld at the request of the employee. Payments through the Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program.
heading: 1600.2.57 Lump Sum (Nonrecurring)

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1600 Income

body:
Exclude all Medicare Advantage (MA) Supplemental Benefits.
