citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions
heading: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions

body:
New information in the manual is highlighted and noted with a . See Manual Updates and for details.

Section with expandable text are noted with a throughout the manual.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions 1
heading: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions

body:
Determining eligibility is a two-step process: Determine if the household meets the program rules; and If eligible, determine the benefit amount.

Determine eligibility for the entire month using the best estimate of income, expenses, and information available for that month.

The method used to calculate a monthly amount depends on knowing the following information for each income and expense source: when started; how often received; period of time intended for; when received; and known future changes.

Once known, use the information to budget current monthly circumstances and to identify future changes, if any.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions 2
heading: 1800.1 Procedures to Determine Monthly Benefit Amount

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions

body:
Count income as follows: The actual income the household received for the 30 days prior to the date of application. Note : The income is budgeted if received in the previous 30 days even if it is does not reflect ongoing income. Income received less often than monthly is averaged over the period the income is intended to cover. See Income Averaging . Income that stops in the application month is budgeted as the actual amount that will be received in that calendar month. Process a change for the following month to reflect zero income from that source. Income that starts in the application month is budgeted as the actual amount that will be received in that calendar month. Process a change in the following month to reflect a full month's income from that source.

See Migrants and Seasonal Farmworkers , Income for special month of application income treatment for Destitute migrant or seasonal farmworker households.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions 3
heading: 1800.2 Countable Income for the month of application

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions

body: Count allowable expenses that are incurred or anticipated to be incurred during the application month. Note : Recurring expenses billed less often than monthly are allowed if the expense will be billed in the benefit period. See Averaging Expenses .
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions 4
heading: 1800.3 Expenses for the Month of Application

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions

body:
Budget income that household members are reasonably certain to receive. Do not budget income if the amount or receipt date is uncertain.

Non-recurring lump sum income is counted as a resource, not as income.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions 5
heading: 1800.4.1 Budgeting Income

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions

body:
Count income only in the month it is anticipated to be received, unless the income is averaged .

Count income that stops until it is no longer received. Remove the income the following month.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions 6
heading: 1800.4.2 Income Only in the Month Received

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions

body:
Do not count wages withheld by the employer unless the wages were withheld at the request of the employee.

If wages are paid in advance, count only if they can be reasonably anticipated.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions 7
heading: 1800.4.3 Wages Held by the Employer

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions

body:
Income received monthly or semi-monthly is counted for the month it was intended when the income is received in a different month because of mailing cycles, holidays, or weekends.

Example : Income is paid on the 1st of the month except when the 1st falls on a weekend or holiday, then it is received on the previous workday. Count the income as received on the 1st day of the month.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions 8
heading: 1800.4.4 No Change of Month

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions

body: Determine income for the month using the best estimate of income available to arrive at a representative amount.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions 9
heading: 1800.5.1 Estimating Income

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions

body: Income amount that is within the range of what would be the typical or usual amount of the household’s income. Income that is significantly higher or lower would not be representative. Discussion with the household member may be needed to determine representative income.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions 10
heading: 1800.5.2 Representative Income

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions

body: Income amount that is an increase or decrease in earning that is not usual for the household’s income. Examples : Income paid because of nonrecurring overtime, nonrecurring bonuses, work hours missed due to emergencies, etc.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions 11
heading: 1800.5.3 Non-Representative Income

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions

body:
Income paid weekly or Bi-Weekly must be converted to a monthly amount. Conversion factor is:

The average weekly amount is multiplied by 4.3.

The average bi-weekly amount is multiplied by 2.15.

Income received twice a month is multiplied by 2.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions 12
heading: 1800.5.4 Income Conversion

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions

body: Income that is received at the same interval with minimal changes in amounts. Examples : Social Security benefits, SSI, maximum unemployment compensation benefits, state employee wages, etc.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions 13
heading: 1800.5.5 Stable Income

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions

body:
Average income when the income amounts, or receipt dates are inconsistent. To determine an average amount: add the gross income for the time period used; divide by the number of possible pay dates for the time period used; multiply by the conversion factor ; and the result is the gross monthly average.

Use whatever period of time is necessary to anticipate a representative amount accurately.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions 14
heading: 1800.5.6.1 Procedures to Average Income

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions

body: Income that is usually paid at a set interval but in different amounts. Examples include, but are not limited to, child support payments, wages with inconsistent hours, etc.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions 15
heading: 1800.5.6.2 Fluctuating Income

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions

body:
Average Contractual Income intended as annual income over a twelve-month period, even if the income is paid in a shorter time frame.

Average contractual income that is not the household’s annual income over the period the income is intended to cover.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions 16
heading: 1800.5.6.3 Contractual Income

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions

body: Average income received less often than monthly over the time period the income is intended to cover.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions 17
heading: 1800.5.6.4 Income That Covers More Than One Month

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions

body:
Income that is not paid regularly or starts and stops without a known schedule. Examples may include temporary jobs, on-call work, etc.

Irregular income also includes unexpected income that cannot reasonably be expected to recur, such as a cash gift. Exclude up to $30 of income per Federal Fiscal Quarter when the income is received too infrequently or irregularly to be reasonably anticipated. Average the income if over $30 per quarter and it can be reasonably anticipated to be received.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions 18
heading: 1800.5.6.5 Irregular Income

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions

body: See Self-Employment for budgeting procedures.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions 19
heading: 1800.5.6.6 Self-Employment Income

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions

body: Budget income for each eligible student , starting with the month the income is received in the following order: Exclude all student financial assistance received under programs in Title IV of the Higher Education Act or under Bureau of Indian Affairs student assistance programs. Exclude any student assistance earmarked or made available for allowable school expenses. Exclude any remaining student assistance the student states are used for allowable student expenses. Divide the remaining education assistance by the number of months the income is intended to cover.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions 20
heading: 1800.5.6.7 Student Income

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions

body: Monthly expenses used to determine deductions are: Dependent Care Earned Income Deduction Legally Obligated Child Support Medical Expenses Shelter Expenses Standard Deduction
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions 21
heading: 1800.6.1 Types of Deductions

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions

body:
Do not allow expenses paid by someone outside of the household. These payments may be reimbursements or vendor payments . Exception : Utility expenses are allowed if paid by the $21 Seasonal Fuel Assistance .

Do not allow an expense if the service is provided by someone residing in the household.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions 22
heading: 1800.6.2 Disallowed Expenses

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions

body:
Allow expenses only in the month they are billed or otherwise become due, regardless of when the household intends to pay the expense. Do not allow amounts from past billing periods, even if actually paid in the current month.

Exception : See Averaging Expenses .
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions 23
heading: 1800.6.3 Billed Expenses

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions

body: Calculate expenses that are reasonably certain to be billed during the benefit period.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions 24
heading: 1800.6.4 Anticipating Expenses

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions

body: Convert expenses paid weekly or Bi-Weekly to a monthly amount. Conversion factor is: The average weekly amount is multiplied by 4.3. The average bi-weekly amount is multiplied by 2.15. Expenses billed twice a month are multiplied by 2.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions 25
heading: 1800.6.5 Expense Conversion

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions

body:
Households may choose to have fluctuating expenses averaged. These are expenses that are usually billed at a set interval but in different amounts.

Households may also choose to have expenses which are billed less often than monthly averaged forward over the interval between scheduled billings, or, if there is no scheduled billing, averaged forward over the period the expense is intended to cover.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions 26
heading: 1800.6.6.1 Types of Expenses that May be Averaged

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions

body: Households may choose to have one-time only expenses averaged over the entire benefit period in which they are billed.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions 27
heading: 1800.6.6.2 One-Time Expenses

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions

body:
Households that are not using the standard medical expense may choose to have one-time only medical expenses: used in one month; or averaged over the remaining months of their benefit period. Averaging begins the month the change is effective.

Exception : If the household is certified for a 24-month or 36-month period and the expense is reported in the first 12-months, the household has the option of averaging the expense over the first 12-months of the benefit period or over the remaining months of the 24-month or 36-month benefit period. If the household is certified for a 36-month period and the expense is reported in the second 12-months, the household has the option of averaging the expense over the second 12-months of the benefit period or over the remaining months of the 36-month benefit period.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions 28
heading: 1800.6.6.3 One-Time Medical Expenses

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions

body:
Standard Categorical Eligibility (SCE) households do not have a gross or net income limit.

Expanded Categorical Eligible (ECE) households’ gross income must be below 185% of the FPL . ECE household’s do not have a net income limit.

Senior/Disabled households do not have a gross income limit.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions 29
heading: 1800.7.1 Categorical Eligibility

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions

body:
The total of all Gross Income , non-excluded income of all household members must not be greater than the gross income limit gross income limit for that household size unless : Income Limits and Maximum Benefit 3SVT Household Size Expanded Categorical Eligibility ( ECE ) Senior & Disabled Seeking Separate Household Status Income Change Reporting Threshold And Gross Income Limit For Non- SCE or ECE Eligible Net Income Limit For Non- SCE or ECE eligible Maximum Monthly Benefit 3SVT House-hold Size Gross Monthly Income Limit (185% FPL ) Gross Monthly Income Limit (165% FPL) Gross Monthly Income Limit (130% FPL) Net Monthly Income Limit (100% FPL) Thrifty Food Plan 1 $2,413 $2,152 $1,696 $1,305 $298 2 $3,261 $2,909 $2,292 $1,763 $546 3 $4,109 $3,665 $2,888 $2,221 $785 4 $4,957 $4,421 $3,483 $2,680 $994 5 $5,805 $5,177 $4,079 $3,138 $1,183 6 $6,653 $5,934 $4,675 $3,596 $1,421 7 $7,501 $6,690 $5,271 $4,055 $1,571 8 $8,349 $7,446 $5,867 $4,513 $1,789 9 $9,197 $8,203 $6,463 $4,972 $2,007 10 $10,044 $8,960 $7,059 $5,431 $2,225 Each Additional Member +$848 +$757 +$596 +$459 +$218

the household is categorically eligible ; or

a household member is Senior or disabled disabled . A person who meets one or more of the following: Receives disability or blindness benefits from any of these programs: Social Security, SSI , or MABD . A person meets this even when the full benefit is withheld or deferred due to recoupment. Receives a federally or state administered SSI supplement based on disability or blindness, or section 212(a) of PL 93-66. Receives a disability retirement benefit from a government agency for a disability considered permanent by SSA disability considered permanent by SSA . Any of the following conditions: Permanent loss of use of both hands, both feet, or one hand and one foot. Amputation of leg at hip. Amputation of leg or foot because of diabetes or peripheral vascular diseases. Total deafness, not correctable by surgery or hearing aid. Statutory blindness, unless caused by cataracts or detached retina. IQ of 59 or less, established after the person becomes 16 years old. Spinal cord or nerve root lesions resulting in paraplegia or quadriplegia. Multiple sclerosis with damage to the nervous system caused by scattered areas of inflammation. The inflammation recurs and has progressed to varied interference with the function of the nervous system, including severe muscle weakness, paralysis, and vision and speech defects. Muscular dystrophy with irreversible wasting of the muscles, impairing the ability to use the arms or legs. Impaired renal function caused by chronic renal disease, resulting in severely reduced function which may require dialysis or kidney transplant. Amputation of a limb of a person at least 55 years old. Is a veteran the VA considers totally (100%) disabled or permanently housebound or in need of regular aid and attendance. Is a veteran’s surviving spouse who the VA considers: in need of regular aid and attendance, or permanently housebound, or approved for benefits because of the veteran’s death and has a disability considered permanent by SSA. Is a veteran’s surviving child who the VA considers: incapable of self-support, or approved for benefits because of the veteran’s death and has a disability considered permanent by SSA

When the household’s circumstances change and they become entitled to a different income limit, apply the different standard whenever the household’s eligibility, benefit level, or benefit period changes, whichever is first.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions 30
heading: 1800.7.2 Gross Income Limit

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions

body: The household’s Net Income must not be greater than the net income limit net income limit for that household size unless the household is categorically eligible. Income Limits and Maximum Benefit 3SVT Household Size Expanded Categorical Eligibility ( ECE ) Senior & Disabled Seeking Separate Household Status Income Change Reporting Threshold And Gross Income Limit For Non- SCE or ECE Eligible Net Income Limit For Non- SCE or ECE eligible Maximum Monthly Benefit 3SVT House-hold Size Gross Monthly Income Limit (185% FPL ) Gross Monthly Income Limit (165% FPL) Gross Monthly Income Limit (130% FPL) Net Monthly Income Limit (100% FPL) Thrifty Food Plan 1 $2,413 $2,152 $1,696 $1,305 $298 2 $3,261 $2,909 $2,292 $1,763 $546 3 $4,109 $3,665 $2,888 $2,221 $785 4 $4,957 $4,421 $3,483 $2,680 $994 5 $5,805 $5,177 $4,079 $3,138 $1,183 6 $6,653 $5,934 $4,675 $3,596 $1,421 7 $7,501 $6,690 $5,271 $4,055 $1,571 8 $8,349 $7,446 $5,867 $4,513 $1,789 9 $9,197 $8,203 $6,463 $4,972 $2,007 10 $10,044 $8,960 $7,059 $5,431 $2,225 Each Additional Member +$848 +$757 +$596 +$459 +$218
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions 31
heading: 1800.7.3 Net Income Limit

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions

body:
Benefits are calculated automatically by ACCESS. See Net Income and Benefit Calculation for the calculation.

Benefits are prorated for the application month based on the date the application is filed.
citation_label: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1800 Determining Income and Deductions 32
heading: 1800.8 Calculating Net Income and Benefit Levels

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1900 3SquaresVT in a SNAP

body:
New information in the manual is highlighted and noted with a . See Manual Updates and for details.

Section with expandable text are noted with a throughout the manual.
heading: 1900 3SquaresVT in a SNAP

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1900 3SquaresVT in a SNAP

body: The following notes the differences between 3SVT and 3SquaresVT in a SNAP: Simplified application 36-month certification period No interim report Self-declared eligibility information except: Non-citizen status; Child support paid; Medical expenses; Questionable information; and General Work Requirement Exemptions that Require Verification and Time Limited Benefits Work Requirement Verification . Reporting Requirements No recertification interview except when: Eligibility results in a denial; Information supplied by the household is questionable, incomplete, or contradictory; or Household requests an interview.
heading: 1900.1 General Information

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1900 3SquaresVT in a SNAP

body:
Only the following households are eligible for 3SquaresVT in a SNAP: All household members are Senior or disabled disabled ; and A person who meets one or more of the following: Receives disability or blindness benefits from any of these programs: Social Security, SSI , or MABD . A person meets this even when the full benefit is withheld or deferred due to recoupment. Receives a federally or state administered SSI supplement based on disability or blindness, or section 212(a) of PL 93-66. Receives a disability retirement benefit from a government agency for a disability considered permanent by SSA disability considered permanent by SSA . Any of the following conditions: Permanent loss of use of both hands, both feet, or one hand and one foot. Amputation of leg at hip. Amputation of leg or foot because of diabetes or peripheral vascular diseases. Total deafness, not correctable by surgery or hearing aid. Statutory blindness, unless caused by cataracts or detached retina. IQ of 59 or less, established after the person becomes 16 years old. Spinal cord or nerve root lesions resulting in paraplegia or quadriplegia. Multiple sclerosis with damage to the nervous system caused by scattered areas of inflammation. The inflammation recurs and has progressed to varied interference with the function of the nervous system, including severe muscle weakness, paralysis, and vision and speech defects. Muscular dystrophy with irreversible wasting of the muscles, impairing the ability to use the arms or legs. Impaired renal function caused by chronic renal disease, resulting in severely reduced function which may require dialysis or kidney transplant. Amputation of a limb of a person at least 55 years old. Is a veteran the VA considers totally (100%) disabled or permanently housebound or in need of regular aid and attendance. Is a veteran’s surviving spouse who the VA considers: in need of regular aid and attendance, or permanently housebound, or approved for benefits because of the veteran’s death and has a disability considered permanent by SSA. Is a veteran’s surviving child who the VA considers: incapable of self-support, or approved for benefits because of the veteran’s death and has a disability considered permanent by SSA All household members have no Earned Income in the month of application.

Note : Non-countable earned income including in-kind and self-employment that results in a loss or net zero is earned income.

Note: The following is not earned income: unpaid volunteer, unpaid employment and training program participation, ICAN, unpaid workfare participation.

Note : RUFA and EP households are not eligible for 3SquaresVT in a SNAP.
heading: 1900.2 Eligible Household Criteria

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1900 3SquaresVT in a SNAP

body:
Screen all 3SVT applications to see if they meet 3SquaresVT in a SNAP Eligible Household Criteria .

3SquaresVT in a SNAP households have the option to complete a simplified application, the ESD 202 3SNP. If a 3SquaresVT in a SNAP application is submitted for a household that is not 3SquaresVT in a SNAP eligible, the ES will CATN all 202 questions asked during the interview that are not already included on the 202 3SNP.
heading: 1900.3.1 Overview

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1900 3SquaresVT in a SNAP

body:
3SquaresVT in a SNAP households must complete an interview at initial application. The ADPC does not send the ESD 202C when a 3SquaresVT in a SNAP application is received by them. The ES will send the ESD 202C after two cold calls are unsuccessful.

3SquaresVT in a SNAP households are entitled to Expedited Service if expedited service criteria is met.
heading: 1900.3.2 Interviews

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1900 3SquaresVT in a SNAP

body:
Use data matches to verify income. Allow self-declaration of income that cannot be verified through data matches. Only verify income if questionable.

Allow the household to self-declare all information except for: Non-Citizen status, Child support paid, Medical expenses greater than $35 per month Questionable information , and General Work Requirement Exemptions that Require Verification and Time Limited Benefits Work Requirement Verification .

Complete data matches including but not limited to: SVES , eDRS , SAVES , PVS .

Required verification must be provided before approving the application.

Exception : Expedited Service .
heading: 1900.3.3 Verification

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1900 3SquaresVT in a SNAP

body:
Assign a 36-month certification period to 3SquaresVT in a SNAP households where all household members are: Senior or disabled disabled , and A person who meets one or more of the following: Receives disability or blindness benefits from any of these programs: Social Security, SSI , or MABD . A person meets this even when the full benefit is withheld or deferred due to recoupment. Receives a federally or state administered SSI supplement based on disability or blindness, or section 212(a) of PL 93-66. Receives a disability retirement benefit from a government agency for a disability considered permanent by SSA disability considered permanent by SSA . Any of the following conditions: Permanent loss of use of both hands, both feet, or one hand and one foot. Amputation of leg at hip. Amputation of leg or foot because of diabetes or peripheral vascular diseases. Total deafness, not correctable by surgery or hearing aid. Statutory blindness, unless caused by cataracts or detached retina. IQ of 59 or less, established after the person becomes 16 years old. Spinal cord or nerve root lesions resulting in paraplegia or quadriplegia. Multiple sclerosis with damage to the nervous system caused by scattered areas of inflammation. The inflammation recurs and has progressed to varied interference with the function of the nervous system, including severe muscle weakness, paralysis, and vision and speech defects. Muscular dystrophy with irreversible wasting of the muscles, impairing the ability to use the arms or legs. Impaired renal function caused by chronic renal disease, resulting in severely reduced function which may require dialysis or kidney transplant. Amputation of a limb of a person at least 55 years old. Is a veteran the VA considers totally (100%) disabled or permanently housebound or in need of regular aid and attendance. Is a veteran’s surviving spouse who the VA considers: in need of regular aid and attendance, or permanently housebound, or approved for benefits because of the veteran’s death and has a disability considered permanent by SSA. Is a veteran’s surviving child who the VA considers: incapable of self-support, or approved for benefits because of the veteran’s death and has a disability considered permanent by SSA all household members have no Earned Income .

See Certification Periods .
heading: 1900.3.4 Certification Periods

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1900 3SquaresVT in a SNAP

body:
3SquaresVT in a SNAP households are required to report fewer changes during their certification period than other programs. 3SquaresVT in a SNAP reporting has two parts: Reduced 3SquaresVT in a SNAP reporting requirement , and No Interim Report .

Households are not required to report any other changes that may occur at the same time.
heading: 1900.4.1 Overview

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1900 3SquaresVT in a SNAP

body: Provide the following reporting requirement information to the household, both verbally and in writing at application and recertification. How 3SquaresVT in a SNAP reporting works, and Reporting requirements, including: What to report What to verify
heading: 1900.4.2 3SquaresVT in a SNAP Reporting Requirement Notification

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1900 3SquaresVT in a SNAP

body:
Households must report the following changes: Changes to Household Composition , Earned Income

Households must report these changes within ten days of the date the change occurred. Note : If they have reported by the tenth day of the month following the month of the change, they have reported timely.

Changes may be reported: In writing, By telephone, or In-person

Unscheduled reporting of changes can be done at any time but is not required. See Change Processing .

Households may also report these changes using form ESD 200, Change Report .
heading: 1900.4.3 Reduced 3SquaresVT in a SNAP Reporting Requirements

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1900 3SquaresVT in a SNAP

body: See Benefit Increase . Note : 3SquaresVT in a SNAP households can self-declare income unless questionable.
heading: 1900.5.1 Benefit Increase

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1900 3SquaresVT in a SNAP

body: See Benefit Decrease/Case Closure .
heading: 1900.5.2 Benefit Decrease/Case Closure

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1900 3SquaresVT in a SNAP

body: See Unclear Change Information 3SquaresVT in a SNAP .
heading: 1900.5.3 Unclear Change

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1900 3SquaresVT in a SNAP

body: Convert 3SVT households that meet 3SquaresVT in a SNAP eligibility at interim report or recertification.
heading: 1900.6.1 Overview

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1900 3SquaresVT in a SNAP

body:
Convert 3SVT households that meet 3SquaresVT in a SNAP eligibility at interim report. Extend the certification period to a total of 36-months from last application and interview.

No interview is required.
heading: 1900.6.2 Conversion at Interim Report

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1900 3SquaresVT in a SNAP

body: Complete a cold call to explain 3SquaresVT in a SNAP and 3SquaresVT in a SNAP Reporting Requirements . If the participant does not answer the phone, CATN the call attempt. Convert the case to 3SquaresVT in a SNAP. Extend the original certification date by 24 months for a total of 36 months from last application and interview. Extend the existing FMED panel to line up with the new certification date.
heading: 1900.6.2.1 12-month Certification Period

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1900 3SquaresVT in a SNAP

body: Complete a cold call to explain 3SquaresVT in a SNAP and 3SquaresVT in a SNAP Reporting Requirements . If the participant does not answer the phone, CATN the call attempt. Convert the case to 3SquaresVT in a SNAP. Extend the original certification date by 12 months for a total of 36 months from last application and interview. Extend the existing FMED panel to line up with the new certification date.
heading: 1900.6.2.2 24-month Certification Period

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1900 3SquaresVT in a SNAP

body:
When a 3SVT household completes a recertification, screen the household for 3SquaresVT in a SNAP eligibility. Follow the steps in 3SquaresVT in a SNAP Application .

Note : An Interview is required to convert to 3SquaresVT in a SNAP at 3SVT recertification.
heading: 1900.6.3 Conversion at Recertification

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1900 3SquaresVT in a SNAP

body: At conversion to 3SquaresVT in a SNAP, the notice of decision will state: interview information; reminder of Reporting Requirements ; reminder of no Interim Report ; and information on the new certification period .
heading: 1900.6.4 Notification

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1900 3SquaresVT in a SNAP

body:
When a household previously certified for 3SquaresVT in a SNAP reports a change that makes them no longer eligible for 3SquaresVT in a SNAP, the household will be converted to 3SVT. Converted households will: receive an Interim Report at six-month intervals; receive a conversion to 3SVT notice; maintain their current 36-month certification period; and are subject to Simplified Reporting .

At the end of the 36-month certification period, the household will receive a new Certification Period based on current circumstances. See Recertification .
heading: 1900.7 Conversion from 3SquaresVT in a SNAP to 3SVT

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1900 3SquaresVT in a SNAP

body:
Households will receive a notice to reapply the month prior to the end of their current period. All applications received within 60 days prior to the end of the certification will be used as a recertification. The ESD 202RL, Reminder Letter is sent six weeks prior to its due date. It is computer generated, printed, and mailed by the ADPC. The 202RL includes: ESD 202RL ESD 202 3SNP REV

The household will auto close if by the 15th of the last month of certification it fails to complete the recertification.
heading: 1900.8.1 Overview

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1900 3SquaresVT in a SNAP

body:
Interviews are not required unless: Eligibility results in a denial; Information supplied by the household is questionable, incomplete, or contradictory; or Household requests an interview.

If an interview is required, see Interviews .

The Oral Explanation of the work requirements is provided to households that include individuals subject to the general and time limited benefits work requirements. One collateral call to the household is required and must be documented in CATN.
heading: 1900.8.2 Interviews

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 1900 3SquaresVT in a SNAP

body: Households must verify the following: Legally obligated child support changes. Medical expenses for households claiming actual medical expenses more than $191, or who wish to switch from the standard. The first $35.01 of medical expenses when the household wishes to use the standard medical expense deduction. CATN the household’s decision to use the standard deduction if their actual expenses are more than $191. Note : Accept the household’s statement regarding their medical expenses if they wish to continue to receive the standard medical expense deduction, and verification of their medical expenses is visible in OnBase. The first $35.01 in medical expenses must be verified if not visible in OnBase. Questionable information. Time limited participant's work hours. Work status and exemptions, if not previously verified.
heading: 1900.8.3 Verification at Recertification

metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2000 Self Employment

body:
New information in the manual is highlighted and noted with a . See Manual Updates and for details.

Section with expandable text are noted with a throughout the manual.
heading: 2000 Self Employment

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2000 Self Employment

body:
Self-employment income is earned directly from a business or profession, rather than a specified salary or wages from an employer. A self-employment business exists when there is no employer/employee relationship. Tax and Social Security payments are the business owner’s responsibility. Examples include, but are not limited to: independent contractors, landscapers, dependent care providers, newspaper carriers, farmers, landlords, etc.

Income received from a Partnership is self-employment income.

Income received from a Corporation is self-employment income. Wages and profits paid from a corporation follow normal wage and dividend income calculations.

Self-employment income is generally reported on income tax forms. See income tax forms. Follow the Procedural Handbook for Determining Self-Employment Income Using Tax Forms . If tax forms are not filed, determine the income from the: business accounts, bookkeeping records, sales and expenditures records, or other records maintained by the business owner that allows a calculation of the income and expenses.
heading: 2000.1 Overview

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2000 Self Employment

body:
Exclude self-employment income from resource calculation during the period for which the income is averaged/annualized.

Exclude property needed for Income Producing Property and Farm Property self-employment.
heading: 2000.2 Resources

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2000 Self Employment

body: Budget self-employment income over a 12-month period if the income represents a household’s annual support, even if the income is received in a shorter period of time. If the income is not intended for the household’s annual support, average over the number of months the income is intended to cover. Exception : If a business had a substantial change , budget based on current and Anticipate amounts.
heading: 2000.3.1 Annualizing Income

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2000 Self Employment

body:
Add all self-employment and Capital Gain Income , subtract allowable self-employment expenses . This is net self-employment income. Determine the monthly net by dividing by 12 months for annualized income or dividing by the number of months the income is intended to cover.

Fact sheets, ESD 204 B, Business Income and Expenses; , ESD204F, Farm Income and Expenses , and ESD 204R, Rental Income and Expenses Budgeting , may be used in the income determination.

Verbal self-declaration may be used in the income determination. CATN the customer's statement.
heading: 2000.3.2.1 Procedure to Calculate Income

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2000 Self Employment

body: If the business has not yet received income, budget zero. For a new business that has received income, average the income and expenses over the period of time the business has been operating.
heading: 2000.3.2.2 New Business

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2000 Self Employment

body:
If the household reports the averaged or annualized amount does not accurately reflect the household’s actual monthly amounts because of a substantial increase or decrease in the business, budget income based on anticipated amounts.

Use income and expense amounts for the months after the substantial change and, if needed, add additional amounts the household anticipates, and divide by the number of months used.
heading: 2000.3.2.3 Substantial Change

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2000 Self Employment

body:
Most costs of producing self-employment income are deducted from the gross business income.

When the business is located in the home or on the home property, allow only the business portion of the total shelter expense. The self-employment expense is a prorated amount based on the percent of the property used for an office in home or on the home property.

Exception : Do not allow utilities as a self-employment expense if the business is in the home or on the home property unless the business has a separate meter for the utility.

Note : Allow the household its shelter expenses as a deduction on ACCESS.
heading: 2000.3.2.4 Allowable Self-Employment Expenses

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2000 Self Employment

body:
Income received for a Commercial Boarding House is earned income, after allowed self-employment expenses are deducted.

Income received for non-commercial boarding houses is earned income if the boarder is not included in the 3SVT household. Use the information in Self-Employment Expenses of Providing Room and Board Self-Employment Expenses of Providing Room and Board , to determine the allowed self-employment expenses. Self-Employment Expenses of Providing Room and Board The following table shows the monthly self-employment expenses allowed for room and board income. If the customer provides documentation of separate identifiable expenses of providing room and/or board that exceed the applicable table amount, allow the higher amount. In no case, however, may the business expense allowed exceed the amount of gross income received from the roomer and boarder. Access Code Type 1 Person 2 Person 3 Person 4 Person 5 Person 6+ Person 1 Room Only $254 $465 $669 $847 $1008 $1,211 2 2/3 Board $199 $364 $523 $663 $789 $947 3 Board Only $298 $546 $785 $994 $1,183 $1,421 4 Room and 2/3 Board $453 $829 $1,192 $1,510 $1,797 $2,158 5 Room and Board $552 $1011 $1,454 $1,841 $2,191 $2,632 If five or more roomers/boarders are in one payment group, the RBIN income should be handled in a BUSI panel not a RBIN panel. Use the expense from the table above as the expense in the BUSI panel. Splitting one payment onto two RBIN panels results in an incorrect business expense deduction. The RBIN panel only allows four roomers/boarders per panel. Splitting one payment onto two RBIN panels results in an incorrect business expense deduction.
heading: 2000.3.2.5 Boarders

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2000 Self Employment

body: Income received for providing dependent care in the household’s own residence is earned income.
heading: 2000.3.2.6 Dependent Care Providers

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2000 Self Employment

body: Farm income is derived from cultivating land or crops, or raising animals. Examples include, but are not limited to; stock, dairy, poultry, fish, fruit, ranches, crops, nurseries.
heading: 20900.3.2.7 Farm Income

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2000 Self Employment

body:
Income received from rental property is budgeted as earned income unless a third party is employed to manage the property.

Income from a Roomer is rental income. Many separate households live together and Shared Households expenses. This is not self-employment income.
heading: 2000.3.2.8 Rental Income

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2000 Self Employment

body: When the expenses are greater than income, the result is a loss. Do not deduct non-farm losses from any other income. Deduct farm losses from other net self-employment income. Deduct any remaining farm loss from the total amount of earned income (minus the earned income deduction) and unearned income. Exception : Do not budget as a farm loss if the annual gross farm income is less than $1000.
heading: 2000.3.2.9 Self-Employment Loss

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2000 Self Employment

body: If the household reports the self-employment has stopped, remove the income. Verbal self-declaration may be used. CATN the household's statement.
heading: 2000.3.2.10 Income Stops

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2000 Self Employment

heading: 2000.5 Last Update

body:
New information in the manual is highlighted and noted with a . See Manual Updates and for details.

Section with expandable text are noted with a throughout the manual.
heading: 2100 Special Household Situations

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body: Eligible Addiction Treatment Center s are exempt from institution rules.
heading: 2100.1.1 Exemption from Institution Rules

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
Eligible addiction treatment centers must be: certified by the USDA FNS as a retailer, or a private nonprofit organization or institution, or a publicly operated community mental health center funded or eligible for funding under Part B of Title XIX of the Public Health Service Act.

The following addiction treatment centers are eligible: Grace House Magee House Mandala House Maple Leaf Phoenix House Rise Serenity House Valley Vista Teen Challenge Note : Teen Challenge participants may move between Vermont, New Hampshire and Massachusetts. Verify the participant is not receiving benefits in New Hampshire or Massachusetts before issuing 3SVT. Brattleboro Retreat Note : Brattleboro Retreat offers several treatment programs. If a customer reports living at Brattleboro Retreat, we must confirm they are in an addiction treatment program. If at Brattleboro Retreat for another program, then the customer is not eligible for 3SVT.

If the treatment center’s authorization isn’t known, email AOPS . AOPS will determine if the treatment center is eligible.
heading: 2100.1.2 Eligible Addiction Treatment Centers

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
Residents of eligible drug or alcohol addiction treatment centers, regularly participating, qualify for 3SVT as separate households from others living in the facility. Exception : Children living with their parent who is a resident of the treatment center must be included as part of the resident parent’s 3SVT household.

The resident and their household must meet all 3SVT eligibility requirements.

People regularly participating in a drug or alcohol treatment program are exempt from work requirements .
heading: 2100.1.3 Resident Eligibility

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body: A designated employee of the treatment center must apply as the authorized representative and act for the resident while the resident is in the treatment center.
heading: 2100.1.4 Applying for 3SVT

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
Residents of treatment centers are eligible for the same service and processing standards as other 3SVT recipients.

Treatment center households must meet the same eligibility requirements as other 3SVT recipients.
heading: 2100.1.5 Processing

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body: The center as authorized representative must report as required by the Simplified Reporting .
heading: 2100.1.6 Reporting Requirements

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
The treatment center must provide DCF with a monthly list of currently participating residents, including a statement signed by a responsible center official attesting to the validity of the list.

DCF conducts periodic, random on-site visits to the center to assure the accuracy of the list and that DCF's records are consistent and up to date.
heading: 2100.1.7 Treatment Center Information Requirements

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body: When the resident leaves the treatment center it will: End acting as the authorized representative; Return the EBT card to the resident; Advise the resident of their reporting requirement by providing form ESD 200, Change Report ; If spent, return half of the resident’s 3SVT benefit according to DCF procedures when the resident leaves the facility before the 16th of the month; Return the EBT card to the State if unable to return the EBT card to the resident.
heading: 2100.1.8 Center Requirements When the Resident Leaves

body:
These are people who are not allowed to be household members because of one of the following: Fleeing felons and probation/parole violators Failure to comply with work requirements Intentional program violation (IPV)

Count the disqualified member’s non-excluded income and resources.

See Income and Resources .

Allow all income deductions and expenses of the disqualified member. See Deductions .

Do not count the disqualified member in the household size when: Determining the standard deduction standard deduction ; Standard Deduction Household Size Amount 1 $209 2 $209 3 $209 4 $223 5 $261 6 $299 Over 6 $299 Comparing the household’s income with the income limits income limits ; Income Limits and Maximum Benefit 3SVT Household Size Expanded Categorical Eligibility ( ECE ) Senior & Disabled Seeking Separate Household Status Income Change Reporting Threshold And Gross Income Limit For Non- SCE or ECE Eligible Net Income Limit For Non- SCE or ECE eligible Maximum Monthly Benefit 3SVT House-hold Size Gross Monthly Income Limit (185% FPL ) Gross Monthly Income Limit (165% FPL) Gross Monthly Income Limit (130% FPL) Net Monthly Income Limit (100% FPL) Thrifty Food Plan 1 $2,413 $2,152 $1,696 $1,305 $298 2 $3,261 $2,909 $2,292 $1,763 $546 3 $4,109 $3,665 $2,888 $2,221 $785 4 $4,957 $4,421 $3,483 $2,680 $994 5 $5,805 $5,177 $4,079 $3,138 $1,183 6 $6,653 $5,934 $4,675 $3,596 $1,421 7 $7,501 $6,690 $5,271 $4,055 $1,571 8 $8,349 $7,446 $5,867 $4,513 $1,789 9 $9,197 $8,203 $6,463 $4,972 $2,007 10 $10,044 $8,960 $7,059 $5,431 $2,225 Each Additional Member +$848 +$757 +$596 +$459 +$218 Comparing the household’s resources with the resource limits ; Determining the benefit amount.
heading: 2100.2.1 Disqualified Members

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body: People disqualified for IPV are not allowed to act as the authorized representative for the household, unless the agency determines no one else is available to serve as the AR .
heading: 2100.2.1.1 Authorized Representative

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
Ineligible members are people who did not meet one of the following eligibility requirements: Non-citizens SSN Time Limited Benefits

Count the ineligible member’s resources. See Resources .

Prorate and budget the ineligible member’s income and expenses. See Income and Deductions .

Do not count the ineligible member in the household size when: Determining the standard deduction standard deduction ; Standard Deduction Household Size Amount 1 $209 2 $209 3 $209 4 $223 5 $261 6 $299 Over 6 $299 Comparing the household’s income with the income limits income limits ; Income Limits and Maximum Benefit 3SVT Household Size Expanded Categorical Eligibility ( ECE ) Senior & Disabled Seeking Separate Household Status Income Change Reporting Threshold And Gross Income Limit For Non- SCE or ECE Eligible Net Income Limit For Non- SCE or ECE eligible Maximum Monthly Benefit 3SVT House-hold Size Gross Monthly Income Limit (185% FPL ) Gross Monthly Income Limit (165% FPL) Gross Monthly Income Limit (130% FPL) Net Monthly Income Limit (100% FPL) Thrifty Food Plan 1 $2,413 $2,152 $1,696 $1,305 $298 2 $3,261 $2,909 $2,292 $1,763 $546 3 $4,109 $3,665 $2,888 $2,221 $785 4 $4,957 $4,421 $3,483 $2,680 $994 5 $5,805 $5,177 $4,079 $3,138 $1,183 6 $6,653 $5,934 $4,675 $3,596 $1,421 7 $7,501 $6,690 $5,271 $4,055 $1,571 8 $8,349 $7,446 $5,867 $4,513 $1,789 9 $9,197 $8,203 $6,463 $4,972 $2,007 10 $10,044 $8,960 $7,059 $5,431 $2,225 Each Additional Member +$848 +$757 +$596 +$459 +$218 Comparing the household’s resources with the resource limits ; Determining the benefit amount.

If the ineligible member is a child they can still provide the household with a TLBWR exemption .

Note : If the ineligible member is correctly coded on ACCESS, ACCESS will do the proration.
heading: 2100.2.2 Ineligible Members

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
Step 1: Determine the ineligible member’s monthly countable Earned Income and Unearned Income separately. See Income and Determining Income and Deductions .

Step 2: Total the number of eligible and ineligible household members.

Step 3: Divide the earned/unearned amount in step 1 by the number of people in the household.

Step 4: Multiply the amount in step 3 by the number of eligible household members.
heading: 2100.2.2.1 Prorating Income

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
Prorate the following expenses paid by or billed to the ineligible member. Allow only the eligible household members’ portion of: Court ordered child support . Dependent care Shelter

Exception : Allow the full SUA if paid by or billed to the ineligible member.

Prorate as follows: Determine the ineligible member’s monthly allowable expenses. See Deductions and Determining Income and Deductions . Total the number of eligible and ineligible household members. Divide the earned/unearned amount in 1 by the number of people in Multiply the amount in 3 by the number of eligible household members.

Note : If the ineligible member is correctly coded on ACCESS, ACCESS will do the proration.
heading: 2100.2.2.2 Prorating Expenses

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
Non-members are people who live with and may share food with the household but are not included as part of the household: Boarders Live-in attendants Residents of commercial boarding houses Exception : Residents of institutions identified in this item Roomers Strikers Students

For a full description, see Non-Members in Household Composition .

Exclude income and resources of non-members when determining eligibility and benefits for the household. Exception : Cash payments from the non-member to the household are income to the household.

Allow only the amount the household paid or contributed when expenses are shared with a non-member. If the household’s amount cannot be determined prorate the expense and allow the household’s share.
heading: 2100.2.3 Non-members

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
Households do not meet categorical eligibility rules when one of the following occurs: A member is disqualified for IPV The head of household is disqualified for failing to comply with 3SVT work requirements ;

Note : A household member who fails to comply with workfare is ineligible for categorical eligibility. Remaining household members remain eligible for categorical eligibility.
heading: 2100.2.4 Categorical Eligibility

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

Effective Date: 07/08/2025

Legal Base Food and Nutrition Act of 2008, as amended. 7 CFR § 273.2(j)(2) 7 CFR § 273.2(n)(4)(B) 7 CFR § 273.11(c) 7 CFR § 273.24
heading: 2100.2.5 Last Update

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
Do not increase 3SVT benefits when the household’s RU / RF benefits end or decrease because the household/person intentionally failed to comply with a RU/RF requirement.

Note : Do not end 3SVT benefits when the household’s benefits from the other program end due to the household’s failure to comply with a requirement of that program.
heading: 2100.3.1 Effects on 3SVT Benefits

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body: Do not apply this policy when: Information regarding the other program’s penalty cannot be obtained. CATN the actions taken to try to obtain the information. The household/person did not comply with a requirement at application for the other program. The household/person is subject to a disqualification/penalty in both the 3SVT program and RU or RF, apply the 3SVT disqualification first. Example : The RU and 3SVT participant refuses to accept a job offer. This is also a 3SVT work requirement. Apply the 3SVT disqualification first. If the 3SVT disqualification ends for any reason, and the penalty is still being applied in the RU program, the full RU amount is budgeted. See Budgeting below.
heading: 2100.3.2 Exceptions

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body: ACCESS keeps the full RU/RF benefit amount in the 3SVT budget for work requirement or child support sanctions.
heading: 2100.3.3.1 Retention of Full RU/RF Benefit Amount

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body: Budget the penalty until the: household/person complies with the other program’s requirements, other program ends, amount of the penalty cannot be determined, or household/person is ineligible for the other program for a reason not related to the penalty.
heading: 2100.3.4 How Long to Budget the Penalty

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
Continue to act on changes for 3SVT not related to the other program’s penalty.

Example 1 : The household reports and verifies they now pay dependent care for their 1-year-old child. Add the dependent care and budget as a deduction. Continue to budget the other program’s penalty amount.

Example 2 : The household’s earned income decreases and the Reach Up benefit increases because of the decrease in earned income. Budget the: new amount of earned income, new amount of Reach Up benefits, and the penalty.
heading: 2100.3.5 Acting on Changes

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
Fleeing felons are people trying to escape or avoid custody for a crime, or attempted crime, classified as a felony (or high misdemeanor in New Jersey) under a Federal or State law.

Probation or parole violators are people who have violated a condition of their probation or parole ordered under a Federal or State law.

Only people verified through law enforcement as fleeing felons or probation or parole violators are disqualified members for 3SVT. See Disqualified Members .
heading: 2100.4.1 Summary

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body: A person is a fleeing felon or a probation or parole violator if they meet the following criteria.
heading: 2100.4.2.1 Status Determination

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body: A person is a fleeing felon if: there is an outstanding felony warrant; the person is aware of or should be aware of the felony warrant; the person is avoiding being arrested or jailed; and law enforcement is actively seeking the person.
heading: 2100.4.2.2 Fleeing Felon

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body: A person is a probation violator if: a Court of law rules that the person violated a condition of their probation ordered under a Federal or State law, and local law enforcement is actively seeking the person to enforce the conditions of the probation.
heading: 2100.4.2.3 Probation Violator

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body: A person is a parole violator if: the Vermont Parole Board (for state offenses) or a Court of law (for Federal offenses) rules that the person violated a condition of their parole ordered under a Federal or State law, and local law enforcement is actively seeking the person to enforce the conditions of the parole.
heading: 2100.4.2.4 Parole Violator

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
Actively seeking means a Federal, State, or local law enforcement agency: states an intent to enforce an outstanding felony warrant, or arrest a person for a probation or parole violation within 20-days of the date the law enforcement agency requested information about the person; presents a felony arrest warrant ; or states an intent to enforce an outstanding felony warrant, or arrest a person for probation or parole violation within 30-days of the date of a status information request from DCF.

A person is not a fleeing felon or a probation or parole violator if the law enforcement agency does not intend to enforce the felony warrant or to arrest the person for a probation or parole violation within 30-days of the status information request from DCF.
heading: 2100.4.2.5 Actively Seeking

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
Verify the person’s status as a fleeing felon or a probation or parole violator before disqualifying them for 3SVT.

Law enforcement requests for information do not verify the status of the felony warrant or probation or parole violation status.

Verify the status of a felony warrant or probation or parole violation only if: a law enforcement agency presents a felony arrest warrant, or states intent to arrest a person for probation or parole violation, or the customer self-declares the information.

Request the following status information from the law enforcement agency: the conditions of a felony warrant or a probation or parole violation, and whether the law enforcement agency intends to actively seek the person.

Allow the law enforcement agency 20-days to respond to a status information request.

If the law enforcement agency intends to enforce the felony warrant, or arrest the person for a probation or parole violation within 30-days of the date of the status request: delay any actions on the case until the 30-day period ends, and verify with the law enforcement agency if an attempt was made to execute the felony warrant or arrest the probation or parole violator. If action was taken within the 30-days, deny or end the person’s eligibility. If no action was taken within the 30-days, the person is not a fleeing felon or probation or parole violator.
heading: 2100.4.3 Verification

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body: Continue to process the application, including expedited services, while waiting for verification of the status of the fleeing felon or probation or parole violator. Process the application without considering the person’s fleeing felon or probation or parole violation status if needed to meet the required 3SVT processing timeframes.
heading: 2100.4.4 Application Processing

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body: Follow change processing policy to act on decreases in household size when a member has been verified as a fleeing felon or a probation or parole violator. See Change Processing, Action on Unscheduled Changes .
heading: 2100.4.5.1 Change Processing

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families

body:
Eligible Group Living for the Disabled facilities are exempt from Institution rules. Eligible group living for the disabled: is a public or private Nonprofit residential setting, serving no more than 16 residents, and certified by the appropriate agency or agencies of the State under regulations or comparable standards issued under Section 1616(e) of the Social Security Act.

If the facility’s eligibility isn’t known, email the Agency of Human Services-Economic Services Division-Assistant Operations (AHS-ESD-AOPS). AOPS will determine if the facility is eligible.
heading: 2100.5.1 Exemption from Institution Rules

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
Residents of any age must be disabled disabled . A person who meets one or more of the following: Receives disability or blindness benefits from any of these programs: Social Security, SSI , or MABD . A person meets this even when the full benefit is withheld or deferred due to recoupment. Receives a federally or state administered SSI supplement based on disability or blindness, or section 212(a) of PL 93-66. Receives a disability retirement benefit from a government agency for a disability considered permanent by SSA disability considered permanent by SSA . Any of the following conditions: Permanent loss of use of both hands, both feet, or one hand and one foot. Amputation of leg at hip. Amputation of leg or foot because of diabetes or peripheral vascular diseases. Total deafness, not correctable by surgery or hearing aid. Statutory blindness, unless caused by cataracts or detached retina. IQ of 59 or less, established after the person becomes 16 years old. Spinal cord or nerve root lesions resulting in paraplegia or quadriplegia. Multiple sclerosis with damage to the nervous system caused by scattered areas of inflammation. The inflammation recurs and has progressed to varied interference with the function of the nervous system, including severe muscle weakness, paralysis, and vision and speech defects. Muscular dystrophy with irreversible wasting of the muscles, impairing the ability to use the arms or legs. Impaired renal function caused by chronic renal disease, resulting in severely reduced function which may require dialysis or kidney transplant. Amputation of a limb of a person at least 55 years old. Is a veteran the VA considers totally (100%) disabled or permanently housebound or in need of regular aid and attendance. Is a veteran’s surviving spouse who the VA considers: in need of regular aid and attendance, or permanently housebound, or approved for benefits because of the veteran’s death and has a disability considered permanent by SSA. Is a veteran’s surviving child who the VA considers: incapable of self-support, or approved for benefits because of the veteran’s death and has a disability considered permanent by SSA

The resident or the household must meet all 3SVT eligibility requirements.
heading: 2100.5.2 Resident Eligibility

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
Residents may apply as a: group of one, or any group of residents applying as a household.

Residents may: apply themselves Note : The facility must decide if the resident can apply for 3SVT on his/her own behalf based on the resident's physical and mental ability to handle his/her affairs. choose their own AR , or use the designated employee of the facility as their authorized representative.

Residents applying themselves do not lose their eligibility if the facility: loses its approval from the appropriate agency or agencies of the State, or its status as an authorized representative is suspended due to FNS disqualifying the facility as a retailer.
heading: 2100.5.3 Applying for 3SVT

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
Residents of group living for the disabled facilities are eligible for the same service and processing standards as other 3SVT recipients.

Facility households must meet the same eligibility requirements as other 3SVT recipients, with the following exception : Allow any identified shelter expenses paid by the resident. If a single payment covers multiple expenses, deduct the following identified costs from the payment: medical expenses (allow toward the medical deduction) amount returned to the resident for personal use, and the maximum food stamp benefit for the number of persons in the household. Allow any remaining amount as the shelter expense.
heading: 2100.5.4.1 3SVT Eligibility Determination

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
Residents certified as a household of one may: give their benefits to the facility to purchase food for meals served to the group or person, purchase and prepare their own food, or purchase meals prepared and served by the facility.

The facility will ensure each resident's 3SVT benefits are used for meals intended for that resident when the resident chooses personal meals instead of group meals.
heading: 2100.5.5 Use of Benefits

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
Residents of group living for the disabled facilities are in Simplified Reporting .

Residents applying themselves are responsible to report their own changes.

If an employee of the facility acts as the authorized representative, the facility must report as required by Simplified Reporting.
heading: 2100.5.6 Reporting Changes

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
The facility must provide DCF with a monthly list of currently participating residents, including a statement signed by a responsible facility official attesting to the validity of the list.

DCF conducts periodic, random onsite visits to assure the accuracy of the list, and that DCF's records are consistent and up to date.
heading: 2100.5.7 Facility Information Requirements

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
When the resident leaves, the facility will: End acting as the authorized representative (if applicable); Return the EBT card to the resident when an employee of the facility is the authorized representative; Advise the resident of their reporting requirement by providing an ESD 200, Change Report ; If spent, return half of the resident’s 3SVT benefit according to DCF procedures when the resident leaves the facility before the 16th of the month; Return the EBT card to the State if unable to return the EBT card to the resident.

Facilities acting as authorized representative for the household are responsible for all: losses or misuse of benefits, and overpayments occurring while acting as authorized representative.
heading: 2100.5.8 Facility Requirements When the Resident Leaves

metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
Homeless shelters are public or private nonprofit facilities that provide temporary housing for homeless people. They are exempt from Institution rules.

Homeless people are people or groups without a fixed and regular nighttime residence, or whose primary nighttime residence is a: Halfway house or similar institution providing temporary residences for people intended to be institutionalized; Temporary accommodations in hotels, motels, or shelters; Temporary accommodations in the residence of another person, for not more than 90 days; or Place not designed for or ordinarily used as a regular sleeping accommodation for human beings. Examples : hallways, bus stations, lobbies or similar places.

If the homeless shelter’s eligibility isn’t known, email the Agency of Human Services-Economic Services Division-Assistant Operations (AHS-ESD-AOPS). AOPS will determine if the homeless shelter is eligible.
heading: 2100.6.1 Definition of Homeless Shelters

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
Homeless households may apply for benefits separately from others living in the homeless shelter. See Household Composition, Homeless Shelters .

Homeless households may pick an AR to act on the household’s behalf.
heading: 2100.6.2 Applying for 3SVT

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
Homeless households are eligible for the same service and processing standards as other 3SVT recipients.

Homeless households must meet the same eligibility requirements as other 3SVT recipients.

Homeless households are not required to have a permanent dwelling or fixed residence. Ask the household to provide a mailing address where notices can be mailed. See Residency .
heading: 2100.6.3 Processing

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body: Homeless participants who are time limited must meet the requirements in 3SquaresVT Time Limited Benefits .
heading: 2100.6.4 Work Requirements for Homeless Time Limited Participants

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body: Homeless households may use 3SVT benefits to purchase prepared meals from authorized grocery stores.
heading: 2100.6.5 Use of Benefits
