body:
Migrant farmworkers temporarily leave a principal place of residence in another state to come to Vermont to accept seasonal employment in the planting, cultivating, raising, harvesting, handling, drying, packing, packaging, processing, freezing, grading or storing of any agricultural or horticultural commodity in its unmanufactured state.

Seasonal farmworkers accept seasonal employment in the planting, cultivating, raising, harvesting, handling, drying, packing, packaging, processing, freezing, grading or storing of any agricultural or horticultural commodity in its unmanufactured state.
heading: 2100.7.1 Definition of Migrants and Seasonal Farmworkers

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
The migrant or seasonal farmworker may apply for 3SVT for their household or choose an authorized representative.

In the event the migrant or seasonal farmworker’s employer acts as the AR , ensure the: household freely requested the employer to act as their authorized representative, employer correctly represents the household’s circumstances, household receives the correct amount of benefits, and authorized representative properly uses the benefits for the household.
heading: 2100.7.2 Applying

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body: Migrant or seasonal farmworker households are eligible for the same service and processing standards, and must meet the same eligibility requirements as other households, with the following exceptions listed throughout this section.
heading: 2100.7.3.1 Eligibility Requirements

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body: Migrant or seasonal farmworker households may qualify for expedited benefits. See Expedited Service . Additionally, migrant or seasonal farmworker households can qualify for expedited processing if the household meets destitute requirements.
heading: 2100.7.3.2 Expedited Processing

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
Only migrant or seasonal farmworker households can be determined as destitute. Destitute means the household’s liquid resources do not exceed $100 and their only income for the month of application: was received from a stopped source before the date of application, Income received on a monthly or more frequent basis is stopped from that source if the income will not be received again from the source during the remainder of the month. Income received less often than monthly is stopped from that source if the income is not received in the month the next payment would normally be received. is from a new source for $25 or more but will not be received by the 10th calendar day after the date of application, Income received on a monthly or more frequent basis is from a new source when income has not been received from that source 30-days prior to the date of application. Income received less often than monthly is from a new source if the income was not received from that source during the last normal interval between payments.

Migrant or seasonal farmworker households can be Destitute even if they receive both stopped income prior to the date of application, and income from a new source after the date of application, provided the household: will not receive any other income in the month of application, and any income of $25 or more from a new source will not be received by the 10th day after the date of application.

Household members changing jobs but continuing to work for the same employer/grower are receiving continuing income, not income from a new source. The employer/grower is the source, not the crew chief. Consider migrant or seasonal farmworker households traveling with a crew chief from one job source to another source as moving from a stopped source to a new source.

Verify destitute status but only if verification will not delay meeting expedited processing requirements.
heading: 2100.7.3.3 Destitute Defined

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
Residency verification may be waived if verification cannot be done.

Attempt to verify with the state the migrant or seasonal farmworker’s household moved from to determine the case closure date and the date benefits were last issued.

See Residency
heading: 2100.7.3.4 Residency

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
Determine a Destitute migrant or seasonal farmworker household’s eligibility and benefits for the month of application using all sources of income received between the first of the month and the date of application. Disregard income from a new source that will be received after the date of application.

Exclude General Assistance (GA) vendor payments for Emergency Assistance including but not limited to housing and transportation payments for migrant or seasonal farmworkers households while they are in the job stream.

Exclude reimbursements for travel expenses incurred by migrant or seasonal farmworkers. Count travel expense advances included in a written work agreement or statement that will be subtracted later from the farmworker’s income.

Budget the household’s income for the period the income is intended to cover.

Note : Exclude a child’s earnings . If the child’s earnings cannot be identified separately from the rest of the household’s earnings, divide the total earnings by the number of members that work, and exclude the child’s share of the income.

Do not average contract or self-employment income over a 12-month period even when the migrant or farmworker’s annual income is earned over a shorter period of time.
heading: 2100.7.3.5 Income

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body: Exempt migrant or seasonal farmworkers working 30-hours per week or more or under contract, or a similar agreement, with an employer or crew chief to begin work within 30-days. See work registration exemptions .
heading: 2100.7.3.6 Work Registration

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body: Farmworkers leaving one job to move to another meet good cause for leaving or reducing employment, even if work at the new job site has not begun.
heading: 2100.7.3.7 Good Cause for Leaving Employment

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
Migrant or seasonal farmworkers must not be targeted for more intensive verifications than other 3SVT households. See Verification and Case Notes (CATN) .

A collateral contact may be made with a person outside the farmworker’s household, such as but not limited to: employers/growers, crew chiefs, or migrant services agencies.
heading: 2100.7.3.8 Verification

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body: Proration of benefits is required for the initial month of approval. The initial month for migrant or seasonal farmworker households is the first month the household is certified following a period of more than one month since the last approval. The last approval may have been in Vermont or another state. See Initial Month .
heading: 2100.7.3.9 Proration of Benefits

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body: Migrant or seasonal farmworker households are in Simplified Reporting .
heading: 2100.7.4 Reporting

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body: Disregard income from a new source in the first month of the new certification period when the income: is $25 or more, and will not be received from this new source by the 10th calendar day after the date of the households normal issuance cycle.
heading: 2100.7.5 Recertification

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
Hearing requests from migrant or seasonal farmworker households must be processed faster than normal hearing decisions when the household plans to move from the jurisdiction of the hearing official.

Process the hearing request to ensure the household receives a decision, and has benefits restored if applicable, before the household leaves the area. See Fair Hearings .
heading: 2100.7.6 Hearings

metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
Shelters for battered persons are exempt from Institution rules.

Shelters for battered persons are public or private nonprofit residential facilities providing temporary housing for battered persons. If the shelter serves other people, a portion of the shelter must be set aside on a long-term basis to serve only battered persons.

If the shelter’s status as a facility isn’t known, email the Agency of Human Services-Economic Services Division-Assistant Operations (AHS-ESD-AOPS). AOPS will determine if the shelter is eligible. Exception : If the shelter is authorized by FNS to redeem as a wholesaler, it is an eligible facility. No further inquiry is needed.
heading: 2100.8.1 Status of Shelters

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
Shelter residents and their children may apply for benefits as a separate household from others living in the shelter for battered persons.

Shelter households may pick an AR to act on the household’s behalf.
heading: 2100.8.2 Applying for 3SVT

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
Ensure the abuser’s household eligibility and benefit reflect the change in household composition due to the shelter household’s departure.

Shelter resident households are eligible for the same service and processing standards, and must meet the same eligibility requirements as other households, with the following exceptions : Duplicate benefits may be issued the first month the household moved into the shelter if the household was part of a SNAP household that included the abuser. Count only the income of the battered person and their children. Allow only the deductions of the battered person and their children.
heading: 2100.8.3 Processing

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body: Residents of shelters for battered persons may use their 3SVT benefits to: purchase and prepare their own food, or purchase meals prepared and served at the shelter.
heading: 2100.8.4 Use of Benefits

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
People who join other employees in stopping or interrupting work to protest work conditions, or to force the employer to accept the employees’ demands are strikers. Consider people strikers until they: return to work, are fired, or quit their job.

People who are not strikers includes but is not limited to: Employees whose workplace is closed because of a Lockout ; People exempt from work registration the day before the strike began; Exception : Does not include the exemption for being employed 30 hours or more. Employees not part of the bargaining unit on strike who do not want to cross the picket line due to fear of injury or death; and People unable to work due to striking employees. Examples : Truck drivers not working because products are not being produced. Employees laid off or furloughed due to the strike.
heading: 2100.9.1 Definition of Strikers

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body: The striker household may apply for 3SVT or choose an authorized representative.
heading: 2100.9.2 Applying for 3SVT

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
Households with striking members are not eligible for 3SVT unless the household: was eligible for benefits the day before the strike began, and are eligible for benefits when they apply.

Striker households receive the same service and processing standards and must meet the same eligibility requirements as other households, with the following exceptions listed throughout this section.
heading: 2100.9.3 Processing

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body: Households are not categorically eligible if the household is ineligible due to a striker.
heading: 2100.9.3.1 Categorical Eligibility

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
Determine the gross income by budgeting the: current income of the non-striking members, and the higher of the striker’s income from the: monthly income before the strike began ( Pre-strike Income ), or current income .

Determine the household’s net income by allowing: deductions for the current month, and the earned income deduction for all household members, including the striker.

Continue to follow steps a and b above as long as the person is a striker.

Count strike benefits including picket pay as Unearned Income .

Households are not eligible for increased benefits due to decreased income because of the strike.
heading: 2100.9.3.2 Income

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body: Federal, State, or local government employees who go on strike have voluntarily quit their job without good cause.
heading: 2100.9.3.3 Voluntary Quit

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2100 Special Household Situations

body:
New information in the manual is highlighted and noted with a . See Manual Updates and for details.

Section with expandable text are noted with a throughout the manual.
heading: 2200 Simplified Reporting

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2200 Simplified Reporting

body:
All 3SquaresVT and 3SquaresVT in a SNAP households have simplified reporting requirements. SR households are required to report fewer changes during their certification period than other programs. Simplified reporting has two parts: Reduced reporting , and Interim report .

3SquaresVT in a SNAP households are subject to their own simplified reporting requirements. See 3SquaresVT in a SNAP Simplified Reporting .
heading: 2200.1 Overview

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2200 Simplified Reporting

body: Provide the following reporting requirement information to the household, both verbally and in writing at application and recertification. How SR works Reporting requirements, including: What to report What to verify When the ESD 202IR, Interim Report is due How to get assistance What happens when the ESD 202IR isn’t completed and returned Help is available to complete and file the ESD 202IR for disabled disabled or non-English speaking households. A person who meets one or more of the following: Receives disability or blindness benefits from any of these programs: Social Security, SSI , or MABD . A person meets this even when the full benefit is withheld or deferred due to recoupment. Receives a federally or state administered SSI supplement based on disability or blindness, or section 212(a) of PL 93-66. Receives a disability retirement benefit from a government agency for a disability considered permanent by SSA disability considered permanent by SSA . Any of the following conditions: Permanent loss of use of both hands, both feet, or one hand and one foot. Amputation of leg at hip. Amputation of leg or foot because of diabetes or peripheral vascular diseases. Total deafness, not correctable by surgery or hearing aid. Statutory blindness, unless caused by cataracts or detached retina. IQ of 59 or less, established after the person becomes 16 years old. Spinal cord or nerve root lesions resulting in paraplegia or quadriplegia. Multiple sclerosis with damage to the nervous system caused by scattered areas of inflammation. The inflammation recurs and has progressed to varied interference with the function of the nervous system, including severe muscle weakness, paralysis, and vision and speech defects. Muscular dystrophy with irreversible wasting of the muscles, impairing the ability to use the arms or legs. Impaired renal function caused by chronic renal disease, resulting in severely reduced function which may require dialysis or kidney transplant. Amputation of a limb of a person at least 55 years old. Is a veteran the VA considers totally (100%) disabled or permanently housebound or in need of regular aid and attendance. Is a veteran’s surviving spouse who the VA considers: in need of regular aid and attendance, or permanently housebound, or approved for benefits because of the veteran’s death and has a disability considered permanent by SSA. Is a veteran’s surviving child who the VA considers: incapable of self-support, or approved for benefits because of the veteran’s death and has a disability considered permanent by SSA The toll-free telephone number, 1-800-479-6151, to ask questions or get help completing the ESD 202IR.
heading: 2200.2 SR Requirement Notification

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2200 Simplified Reporting

body:
Households must report the following changes: When the household’s gross income goes over the gross income limit gross income limit (130% of the FPL ) based on the number of eligible household members as of the most recent: approval, or recertification Income Limits and Maximum Benefit 3SVT Household Size Expanded Categorical Eligibility ( ECE ) Senior & Disabled Seeking Separate Household Status Income Change Reporting Threshold And Gross Income Limit For Non- SCE or ECE Eligible Net Income Limit For Non- SCE or ECE eligible Maximum Monthly Benefit 3SVT House-hold Size Gross Monthly Income Limit (185% FPL ) Gross Monthly Income Limit (165% FPL) Gross Monthly Income Limit (130% FPL) Net Monthly Income Limit (100% FPL) Thrifty Food Plan 1 $2,413 $2,152 $1,696 $1,305 $298 2 $3,261 $2,909 $2,292 $1,763 $546 3 $4,109 $3,665 $2,888 $2,221 $785 4 $4,957 $4,421 $3,483 $2,680 $994 5 $5,805 $5,177 $4,079 $3,138 $1,183 6 $6,653 $5,934 $4,675 $3,596 $1,421 7 $7,501 $6,690 $5,271 $4,055 $1,571 8 $8,349 $7,446 $5,867 $4,513 $1,789 9 $9,197 $8,203 $6,463 $4,972 $2,007 10 $10,044 $8,960 $7,059 $5,431 $2,225 Each Additional Member +$848 +$757 +$596 +$459 +$218 When a time limited customer work hours are less than 80 hours per month (20 hours per week averaged monthly).

Households are not required to report any other changes that may occur at the same time.

Households must report these changes within ten days of the date the change occurred. Note : Most households will not know if either of these changes has occurred until the end of the month. This means, if they have reported by the tenth day of the month following the month of the change, they have reported timely.

Changes may be reported: In writing, By telephone, or In-person

Unscheduled reporting of changes can be done at any time but is not required. See Change Processing .

Households may also report these changes using form ESD 200, Change Report , which is provided to report changes for other programs, such as: Reach Up , Fuel Assistance, PSE Green Mountain Health Care Plans (Medicaid).
heading: 2200.3 Reduced Reporting Requirements

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2200 Simplified Reporting

body:
Households that report when their gross household income goes over 130% FPL , and who remain eligible for 3SVT benefits after benefits are adjusted, have met their 130% gross income reporting requirement. The household does not have to report their income changes again until the interim report or the next recertification, whichever comes first.

Note : Households with income over 130% FPL at application, who are found eligible do not have a reporting requirement until Interim Report .
heading: 2200.4 Gross Income Reporting Requirement After Exceeding 130% FPL

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2200 Simplified Reporting

body: See Certification Periods .
heading: 2200.5 Certification Periods

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2200 Simplified Reporting

body:
Households must complete form ESD 202IR once during the certification period to report and verify specific changes. Exception : 3SquaresVT in a SNAP households do not receive interim reports.

See Conversion from 3SquaresVT to 3SquaresVT in a SNAP .

See Conversion from 3SquaresVT in a SNAP to 3SquaresVT .
heading: 2200.6.1 Interim Report Requirement

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2200 Simplified Reporting

body: Households must report: When Residency changes. When Shelter Expenses change. When Household Composition changes. When there is a change in employment that results in a change in income. Note : A change in earned income does not need to be reported unless due to a change in employment or the income change meets the reduced reporting requirements . When Unearned Income changes by more than $100. When Resources or Vehicles change. Note : Categorically eligible households do not need to report resources. When a time limited customers work hours are less than 80 hours per month (20 hours per week averaged monthly).
heading: 2200.6.2 Interim Report Reporting Requirements

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2200 Simplified Reporting

body: ACCESS automatically generates an ESD 202IR list for each district on the 15th of the month before the household’s ESD 202IR is due. The ESD 202IR is printed and sent from the ADPC .
heading: 2200.6.3.1 ACCESS Automatic Processing

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2200 Simplified Reporting

body: SR households with 12-month certification periods receive their ESD 202IR and a self-addressed postage paid return envelope during the 5th month of their certification period. The complete report and required verifications must be returned by the 2nd day of the 6th month.
heading: 2200.6.3.2 12-Month Certification Periods

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2200 Simplified Reporting

body: SR households with 24-month certification periods receive their ESD 202IR and a self-addressed postage paid return envelope during the 11th month of their certification period. The complete report and required verifications must be returned by the 2nd day of 12th month.
heading: 2200.6.3.3 24-Month Certification Periods

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2200 Simplified Reporting

body:
3SquaresVT in a SNAP households do not receive interim reports.

3SquaresVT in a SNAP households that have converted to 3SVT are SR households. SR households with 36-month certification periods receive their ESD 202IR and a self-addressed postage paid return envelope in six-month intervals. See chart below.

Interim Report Chart:

Month IR Sent Month IR Due 5th month 6th month 11th month 12th month 17th month 18th month 23rd month 24th month 29th month 30th month
heading: 2200.6.3.4 36-Month Certification Periods

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2200 Simplified Reporting

body:
ESD 202IR is complete when: each question is answered, the form is signed and dated, and Note : All electronic signatures are allowed. required verifications are provided.

Reports are complete if the only missing verification is required for an expense. If verification is not received do not allow the expense.

Process reported changes to take effect the month after the report month.

Notify the household of the effect of the change. Adequate notice of adverse action is used for decreases.

Code ACCESS with a “C” showing the ESD 202IR is complete.

Note : The report is complete for 3SVT purposes if Reach Up and Reach Ahead participants do not provide proof of unchanged income.
heading: 2200.6.4 Complete Interim Report

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2200 Simplified Reporting

body:
Code ACCESS with an “I” to show the report is incomplete when the household fails to provide a complete ESD 202IR . Enter any verified changes in ACCESS.

Within 10 days of the date the IR was due, create an ESD 202RL2 , Verification Review Letter advising the household: that the case will end at the end of the month if the missing information and/or verification is not received by the due date, what information or verification is missing, and the due date to provide the missing information and/or verification.

ESD 202RL2 is due within ten days or by the last day of the month, whichever is sooner.

Any case with an “I” in ACCESS on the IR due date will be sent an IR Reminder Notice advising the household they have 10 days to provide all required verification or benefits will close.
heading: 2200.6.6.1 Processing Incomplete IR

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2200 Simplified Reporting

body:
Code ACCESS with a “C” for complete when the missing information and/or verifications are returned. Process the ESD 202IR within ten days from when the complete ESD 202IR is returned.

Determine eligibility and reinstate the case when the missing information or verification is received after the Adverse Action but before the last day of the report month.

Process reported changes to take effect the month after the report month.

Notify the household of the effect of the change. Adequate notice of adverse action is used for decreases. See Change Processing, Notification .
heading: 2200.6.6.2 Household Completes the Process

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2200 Simplified Reporting

body:
Households failing to complete missing information and/or provide verifications by the ESD 202RL2 due date are sent form ESD IR NOAA, Interim Report Notice of Adverse Action.

ACCESS will close the 3SVT case at adverse action if still coded with an “I”.

Information or verifications received in the month following the month of IR closure are too late, and the household must reapply.
heading: 2200.6.6.3 Household Fails to Complete the Process

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2200 Simplified Reporting

body:
Households failing to provide an ESD 202IR are coded by ACCESS with an “S” to show the report has not been returned by the 2nd day of the report month. Households are sent an IR Reminder Notice on the 3rd by ACCESS advising their case will close unless they complete and return the ESD 202IR and required verifications by the due date shown on the notice.

ACCESS will close the 3SVT case at adverse action if still coded with an “S”.
heading: 2200.6.7 No Interim Report

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2200 Simplified Reporting

body:
See Item relating to the reported change.

When verification is required, the ESD 202RL2 is due within ten days or by the last day of the month, whichever is sooner.
heading: 2200.7 Verification

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2200 Simplified Reporting

body:
Release # 26-1

Effective Date: 04/17/2026

Legal Base Food and Nutrition Act of 2008, as amended. 7 CFR § 273.12 Vermont Elderly Simplified Application Project, Approved Waivers.
heading: 2200.8 Last Update

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2200 Simplified Reporting

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2300 Change Processing

body:
New information in the manual is highlighted and noted with a . See Manual Updates and for details.

Section with expandable text are noted with a throughout the manual.
heading: 2300 Change Processing

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2300 Change Processing

body: ES must act on all known changes. Changes in a household’s situation become known to the agency when: reported by the household, or information is received from other sources within the agency or outside the agency.
heading: 2300.1 Action on Known Changes

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2300 Change Processing

body:
Unscheduled changes are those that are reported outside of the Interim Report or Recertification .

Allow the household ten days from the date the change is reported to provide required verifications.

Act on changes within ten days of the date the change was reported.

The date a change is reported is the date: the household reports the change by telephone, the household reports the change in-person, information is received from another source regarding a household change, or a written notice is received in the office, including the ESD 200, Change Report .

ACCESS generates the ESD 220, Notice of Decision, to inform the household of the effect of the change on their benefits.

CATN the date the change is reported, and the actions taken.
heading: 2300.2.1 Process to Take Action on Unscheduled Changes

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2300 Change Processing

body:
Increase the benefit amount no later than the first issuance that is ten days after the change was reported or verified , whichever occurs last .

Example : The household’s normal issuance occurs on the 1st of every month. The household reports and verifies on August 10th their income decreased by $40. With this timeframe the household’s benefit increase must be effective for September. If the household reported and verified the change on August 28th the change must be effective by October.

See Underpayments when the benefits were not increased timely.
heading: 2300.2.2.1 Procedure to Increase Benefit Amount

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2300 Change Processing

body:
Benefit increases due to: adding a new household member, who is not a member of another household, or an income decrease is effective: the first issuance that is ten days after the change was reported or verified, but no later than the month following the month the change is reported.

Process an underpayment if benefits cannot be increased by the following month. The underpayment issuance must be made available to the household by: the tenth day of the following month, or the household’s normal issuance date, whichever is later.
heading: 2300.2.2.2 Adding Household Members and Income Decreases

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2300 Change Processing

body: Determine the household’s benefits without allowing the change when the household fails to provide required verifications. Obtain required verifications at recertification.
heading: 2300.2.2.3 Household Fails to Provide Required Verification

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2300 Change Processing

body: Households failing to provide verification within ten days, who provide the verification later, must have their benefits increased by: the first issuance that is ten days after the change was verified, but no later than the month following the month the change is verified.
heading: 2300.2.2.4 Household Verifies After the 10-day Period

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2300 Change Processing

body:
Take action to decrease the benefit within ten days after the change is reported. Use the information provided by the household. Verification is not required for decreases until interim report or recertification, whichever comes first.

Exception : When a household is active RUFA and 3SVT send a VCR for RUFA only, requesting the income verification. If the verification is received process the case according to the verification received. If verification is not received, update the 3SVT case with the self-reported information and process the case accordingly.

Households must receive advance notice , when benefits are decreased or the case is closed. Benefit decreases or case closures are effective no later than the month following the month in which the adverse action period ends.

Exception : Adequate notice is allowed in some situations.
heading: 2300.2.3 Benefit Decrease/Case Closure

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2300 Change Processing

body:
Unclear change information is information that is received from the household or another source that requires Clarification with a member of the household or verification before acting on the change. The following scenarios are the only unclear changes that should be clarified and/or verified. Information received that: is the result of a PVS match or a DOC Match ; is the result of a deceased person match; is the result of a NDNH match; Note : NDNH match is run only at application and recertification. is the result from a DET hit and within 60 days after the receipt date of an application or recertification; Note : It takes 60 days from the start of employment for the DET hit to be received by ESD. is less than 60 days old relative to the current month of participation and was required to have been reported based on simplified reporting ; or presents significantly conflicting information from what was provided by the household at application or recertification; or Note : The reported information must be about circumstances that existed at the time of the last application or recertification. See Overpayments and Claims . is a change of address reported by the household. See Change of Address .

Clarify and/or verify the unclear information by attempting to call the household or a collateral contact (such as an employer). If unsuccessful: Send an ESD 202VCR to the household requesting information. Include in the ESD 202VCR what will happen if the household fails to respond. Allow the household ten days to: verify, or complete actions to clarify the change information. Households failing to clarify and/or verify the unclear information will receive an ESD 220 notice of adverse action NOAA .

Do not clarify or verify any unclear change scenario other than those listed at 2200.2.4(a) . Instead: provide a clear explanation in CATN of what you did and why you did it; create a future TODO for the end of the interim report or recertification month, whichever comes first, alerting of the unclear information that needs to be verified; and obtain required clarification and/or verification at the next interim report or recertification, whichever comes first.

Exception: Households reporting a change of address must also report the shelter expenses for the new address. See Change of Address .
heading: 2300.2.4 Unclear Change Information 3SquaresVT

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2300 Change Processing

body:
Unclear change information is information that is received from the household or another source that requires Clarification with a member of the household or verification before acting on the change. The following scenarios are the only unclear changes that should be clarified and/or verified. Information received that: is the result of a PVS match, or a DOC Match ; is the result a deceased person match; is the result of a NDNH match; is the result of a DET hit; is less than 60 days old relative to the current month of participation and was required to have been reported based on 3SquaresVT in a SNAP Reporting Requirements ; presents significantly conflicting information from what was provided by the household at application or recertification; or Note : The reported information must be about circumstances that existed at the time of the last application or recertification. See Overpayments and Claims . is a change of address reported by the household. See Change of Address .

Clarify and/or verify the unclear information by attempting to call the household or a collateral contact (such as an employer). If unsuccessful: Send an ESD 202VCR to the household requesting information. Include in the ESD 202VCR what will happen if the household fails to respond. Allow the household ten days to: verify, or complete actions to clarify the change information. Households failing to clarify and/or verify the unclear information will receive an ESD 220 notice of adverse action, NOAA .

Do not clarify or verify any unclear change scenario other than those listed at 2200.2.5(a). Instead: provide a clear explanation in CATN of what you did and why you did it; create a future TODO for the end of the interim report or recertification month, whichever comes first, alerting of the unclear information that needs to be verified; and obtain required clarification and/or verification at the next interim report or recertification, whichever comes first.

Exception: Households reporting a change of address must also report the shelter expenses for the new address. See Change of Address .
heading: 2300.2.5 Unclear Change Information 3SquaresVT in a SNAP

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2300 Change Processing

body:
Households reporting a change of address must also report the shelter expenses for the new address. Verification of shelter expenses is not required unless questionable.

Act within ten days to change the address and determine the new benefit amount based on the shelter expenses for the new address, when all information is provided.
heading: 2300.2.6.1 Change of Address Procedures

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2300 Change Processing

body:
If shelter expense information is not provided with the address change: Call the household to get the needed information about the new shelter expenses. If the household cannot be reached or the information provided is questionable, send the household form ESD 202VCR to request the shelter expense information. Allow the household ten days to respond.

Budget the shelter expenses for the new address within ten days from the date the information is provided.
heading: 2300.2.6.2 Shelter Expense Information Needed

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2300 Change Processing

body: Act within ten days to determine the household’s benefits without allowing the shelter expenses when the household failed to provide the requested information by the ESD 202VCR due date.
heading: 2300.2.6.3 Household Fails to Provide Shelter Expense Information

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2300 Change Processing

body:
Information from data matches must be acted on within ten days of receipt. Follow policy in benefit increase , benefit decrease/case closure , or unclear change information when acting on changes reported through data matches.

The following data matches are verified upon receipt : BENDEX SDX SAVE UCB
heading: 2300.2.7 Data Matches

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2300 Change Processing

body:
Medical expense changes reported outside of application , interim reporting , or recertification , are acted on only when reported by the household or by a source that is verified upon receipt.

Changes in medical expenses are not acted on when: the change is reported by a source other than the household, and the change action cannot be completed without contacting the household for verification.
heading: 2300.2.8 Medical Expenses

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2300 Change Processing

body: This is an unclear change . Do not send an ESD 202VCR. Update the mailing address on the ADDR panel. Create a future TODO to verify the change at the next interim report or recertification, whichever comes first.
heading: 2300.2.9.1 Forwarding Address Provided

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2300 Change Processing

body: This is an unclear change . Do not send an ESD 202VCR. Create a future TODO to verify the change at the next interim report or recertification, whichever comes first.
heading: 2300.2.9.2 Forwarding Address Not Provided

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2300 Change Processing

body:
Changes reported for other programs are reported for 3SVT, including when reported using the ESD 200. Other programs include, but are not limited to: Reach Up Reach First Reach Ahead Green Mountain Care (Medicaid) Dr. Dynasaur, only if it is determined in ACCESS

Act on any changes reported through other programs.

Note : 3SVT benefits cannot be ended solely due to the other program’s benefits ending. 3SVT continued eligibility must be determined based 3SVT eligibility requirements.

Notify the household and VDOL of any changes in work registration or time limited status resulting from the other program’s reported change.

Certification periods terminated based on a reported change end the month following the determination. Send an ESD 220 advising the household their case is closing and that they may reapply.

Send an ESD 220 to the household stating what actions were taken on their 3SVT case because of changes reported by other programs.
heading: 2300.2.10 Reporting Through Other Programs

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2300 Change Processing

body:
Desk reviews are updates run to apply mass changes. Mass changes are changes to eligibility or benefits on all 3SVT cases, or a large number of cases, based on State or Federal government regulations or laws. The change is effective at the same time for all cases.

These changes include, but are not limited to: income eligibility standards income eligibility standards Income Limits and Maximum Benefit 3SVT Household Size Expanded Categorical Eligibility ( ECE ) Senior & Disabled Seeking Separate Household Status Income Change Reporting Threshold And Gross Income Limit For Non- SCE or ECE Eligible Net Income Limit For Non- SCE or ECE eligible Maximum Monthly Benefit 3SVT House-hold Size Gross Monthly Income Limit (185% FPL ) Gross Monthly Income Limit (165% FPL) Gross Monthly Income Limit (130% FPL) Net Monthly Income Limit (100% FPL) Thrifty Food Plan 1 $2,413 $2,152 $1,696 $1,305 $298 2 $3,261 $2,909 $2,292 $1,763 $546 3 $4,109 $3,665 $2,888 $2,221 $785 4 $4,957 $4,421 $3,483 $2,680 $994 5 $5,805 $5,177 $4,079 $3,138 $1,183 6 $6,653 $5,934 $4,675 $3,596 $1,421 7 $7,501 $6,690 $5,271 $4,055 $1,571 8 $8,349 $7,446 $5,867 $4,513 $1,789 9 $9,197 $8,203 $6,463 $4,972 $2,007 10 $10,044 $8,960 $7,059 $5,431 $2,225 Each Additional Member +$848 +$757 +$596 +$459 +$218 shelter deductions benefit amounts utility standards Reach Up benefits COLA s for Federal program payments, such as but not limited to, RSDI SSI

Desk reviews for mass changes are usually run on a weekend, and as early as possible in the month to allow enough time to notify affected households and ES.
heading: 2300.2.11.1 Desk Review Procedures

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2300 Change Processing

body: Households are sent a notice when a mass change affects their eligibility or benefit amount. Additionally, the notice advises the household of their fair hearing rights and when they may receive continued benefits . Notices are mailed the month before the change is effective.
heading: 2300.2.11.2 Household Notice of Mass Change

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2300 Change Processing

body: ESs are sent reports prior to running a desk review and after the update occurs. Mass change ES reports include: What changes are occurring, When the desk review will be run, What ACCESS will complete, What ES must complete, Instructions for ES required actions, and Due dates for ES required actions.
heading: 2300.2.11.3 ES Notifications of Mass Changes

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2300 Change Processing

body: Mass change updates fully completed by ACCESS are called abbreviated transactions. ACCESS sends an ESD 220 to all households whose eligibility or benefits were updated in an abbreviated transaction. ESD 220 is sent to households the month before the change occurs, but no later than the month when benefits are first affected by the mass change.
heading: 2300.2.11.4 Abbreviated Transactions

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2300 Change Processing

body:
Cases that cannot be updated through an abbreviated transaction have: Edits that stop eligibility, Unapproved results, such as: pending cases, ongoing cases with unapproved results, or FIAT eligibility results.

Districts are sent reports identifying cases not completed through abbreviated transactions that require full transactions. Full transactions require ES to: Update the benefits, or Close the case, and Notify the household of the results using the ESD 220.
heading: 2300.2.11.5 Full Transactions

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2300 Change Processing

body: Households have the right to request a fair hearing when they disagree with a change made by a mass change.
heading: 2300.2.11.6 Fair Hearings

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2300 Change Processing

body: Households requesting a fair hearing are eligible to receive continued benefits, at the amount received before the mass update, if the household: did not waive its right to continued benefits, and makes a timely hearing request , and claims benefits were: computed incorrectly, or the rules were misapplied/misinterpreted.
heading: 2300.2.11.7 Continued Benefits

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2300 Change Processing

body: Benefits may be suspended for one month if the household is temporarily ineligible because of a change not expected to continue in future months. Close the case if the household will not be eligible after suspending benefits for one month.
heading: 2300.3 Benefit Suspension

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2300 Change Processing

body: File a claim against the household following Overpayment and Claims and/or Intentional Program Violation (IPV) policies when the household: fails to report a required change, and received benefits they were not eligible to receive.
heading: 2300.4 Failure to Report

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2300 Change Processing

body: Notice of adverse action advises the household that their benefits will decrease or close. NOAAs may be issued allowing: adequate notice, or advance notice.
heading: 2300.5.1 Notice of Adverse Action (NOAA)

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2300 Change Processing

body:
Adequate notice means the household receives an ESD 220 advising them of a decrease in benefits or case closure no later than the date the household would normally receive their next benefit.

Provide adequate notice for decreases or case closures when the change was reported using the ESD 202IR, Interim Report.

Households receiving adequate notice retain the right to: a fair hearing , and continued benefits when a fair hearing is timely requested . Note : Benefits must be returned to the previous level within five days of the household’s request for a fair hearing.
heading: 2300.5.1.1 Adequate Notice

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2300 Change Processing

body:
Advance notice means the household receives an ESD 220 at least ten days plus two days for mailing (12 days) before the decrease in benefits or case closure. This is the adverse action period.

Use advance notice for all decreases except when the decrease is the result of information reported on the ESD 202IR.
heading: 2300.5.1.2 Advance Notice

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2300 Change Processing

body: See item relating to the reported change.
heading: 2300.6.1 Refer to the Reported Change

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2300 Change Processing

body:
Some information is considered verified upon receipt - meaning that once it is given to ESD, it is considered verified, and no further verification is required. Verified upon receipt is information received directly from the source - be it the customer's statement or data matches in ACCESS. Act on verified upon receipt information unless it is questionable or inconsistent with known facts. See What is Questionable Desk Aid .

Items verified by customer's verbal or written statement include but are not limited to: Age; Death of household member; Dependent care expenses; End of self-employment; Household members; Move to an ineligible institution; Relationship; Residency; Resources; School enrollment; Shelter expenses; Social Security Number (SSN); and Utility expenses.

Data matches that are verified upon receipt only include: Beneficiary Data Exchange (BENDEX); Changes in the amount paid in another program within ACCESS: RU , RF , PSE , GA , and EP .; Numident match; Office of Child Support (OCS) interface; State Data Exchange (SDX); Systematic Alien Verification for Entitlements (SAVE); Unemployment Compensation Benefits (UCB) tape match.

Documents that are verified upon receipt include but are not limited to: VA award notice Rental agreement Railroad retirement board notice Property tax statement Federal income tax return
heading: 2300.6.2 Verified Upon Receipt

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2300 Change Processing

body:
Release # 25-6

Effective Date: 12/15/2025

Legal Base Food and Nutrition Act of 2008, as amended. 7 CFR § 273.2(f)(8)(ii) 7 CFR § 273.12 7 CFR § 273.13(a) 7 CFR § 273.13(b) Vermont Elderly Simplified Application Project, Approved Waivers.
heading: 2300.8 Last Update

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2300 Change Processing

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2400 Recertification

body:
New information in the manual is highlighted and noted with a . See Manual Updates and for details.

Section with expandable text are noted with a throughout the manual.
heading: 2400 Recertification

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2400 Recertification

body:
Recertification is a review of the household’s eligibility factors to determine their continued eligibility for 3SVT benefits.

A recertification is complete when the household: completes and files a signed ESD 202, Application, completes an interview, and provides all required verifications.

Exception : 3SquaresVT in a SNAP households may not require an interview. See Interviews .
heading: 2400.1 Overview

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2400 Recertification

body:
Households must receive a notice to reapply in the month before the last month of their current certification period. The ESD 202RL, Reminder Letter, is the notice to reapply. It is computer generated, printed and mailed by ADPC six weeks before its due date.

Mail the following forms to the Head of Household : ESD 202RL, ESD 201A, Agreement to Report Change , and ESD 202, Review, Application .

Exception : 3SquaresVT in a SNAP households do not receive the ESD 201A.

The household has failed to co-operate if by the 15th of the last month of their certification it fails to: complete the ESD 202 Review, Application, or complete an ESD 202, Application , and contact the office to indicate a problem obtaining verification.

The case will auto-close at the end of the certification period. ACCESS will trigger the auto-closure letter.
heading: 2400.2 Notice to Reapply

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2400 Recertification

body:
The household must submit either a signed ESD 202REV, ESD 202, or ESD 202 3SNP REV by the 15th of the last month of their certification period.

Note : In the rest of this item when an ESD 202 is referenced it means either form.

Note : Use applications received within 60 days prior to the end of the certification period as a recertification. Do not use applications received prior to this as a recertification.
heading: 2400.3 Timely Filing

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2400 Recertification

body:
Complete a telephone interview for all households at recertification. Send the ESD 202C, ESD Benefits Interview Appointment Notice, when you receive the ESD 202. Allow the household five calendar days to complete the interview. This will give the household ten days to provide verification before the end of the certification period.

Note : The ADPC sends the ESD 202C for applications received by them.

Exception : Complete a face-to-face interview for the following reasons: Household or authorized representative request. Needed to resolve or clarify specific issues.

CATN if the interview was completed by telephone or face-to-face.

Do not complete an interview for 3SquaresVT in a SNAP households at recertification unless: Eligibility results in a denial; Information supplied by the household is questionable, incomplete, or contradictory; or Household requests an interview.
heading: 2400.4 Interviews

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2400 Recertification

body:
A household that completes all of the following actions and is found eligible is entitled to Uninterrupted Benefits : Submits a signed ESD 202 no later than the 15th of the last month of their certification period. Completed the interview at least ten days before the end of the last month of their certification period. Note : This may not apply to a 3SquaresVT in a SNAP household. Provided all required verifications before the end of the last month of their certification period.

Notify the household of eligibility or denial by the end of the current certification period. 3SquaresVT in a SNAP eligibility resulting in a denial requires an interview prior to denying the case.

Note : See Agency Caused Delays if timely processing is not met.
heading: 2400.5 Timely Processing

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2400 Recertification

body: Households that submitted an application timely for recertification, but were not timely processed, must be: previously sent an ESD 202RL, authorized benefits the same day they are determined eligible; issued a full month’s benefit; issued underpayments when the household could not participate the first month of the new certification period.
heading: 2400.6.1 Agency Caused Delays

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2400 Recertification

body: Households lose their right to uninterrupted benefits when they fail to: submit a signed ESD 202 by the 15th of the last month of their certification period, or complete their interview, or Note : This may not apply to 3SquaresVT in a SNAP households. timely submit required verifications.
heading: 2400.6.2.1 Loss of Uninterrupted Benefits

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2400 Recertification

body:
Households who submit their ESD 202 but do not complete their interview must have been sent: ESD 202RL; ESD 202C, allowing five days to complete their interview; ESD 202D, Notice of Missed Interview (NOMI), within 24 hours of the missed interview. The household is responsible to reschedule any missed interviews.

When issuing a NOMI, the "call before date" is: the last day of the certification period if issuing on or before adverse action, or the 30th day from the application date if issuing after adverse action.

Exception : 3SquaresVT in a SNAP households may not require an interview. See Interviews .

ACCESS closes the case for failure to complete the recertification if the interview is not completed. If the household then completes the interview and provides all verification before the end of the certification period: Determine eligibility. If eligible, reinstate the case and provide a full month’s benefits within 30 days of the application date, or ten days from the date the interview is completed, whichever is later.

Note : A new application for recertification is not required.
heading: 2400.6.2.2 Failure to Complete the Interview

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2400 Recertification

body:
Households who submit their ESD 202 and complete their interview, but do not provide all required verification by the ESD 202RL2, Verification Review Letter, due date are denied the day after the due date, provided the due date: allowed ten days after the interview and, was before the end of the certification period.

If the household then provides the required verification before the end of the certification period: Determine eligibility. If eligible, reinstate the case and provide a full month’s benefits within 30 days of the application date, or ten days from the date the verification is provided, whichever is later.

Note : A new application for recertification is not required.
heading: 2400.6.2.3 Failure to Provide Verification

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2400 Recertification

body:
Households that: applied before the end of their certification period, and completed the interview during the certification period or within 30 days after the end of the certification period, and Note : This may not apply to 3SquaresVT in a SNAP households. provides required verification within 10 days of the interview or within 30 days after the end of the certification period, whichever is later, must be reopened, if eligible. Provide a full month’s benefits.

Process applications for recertification received within 30 days of the end of the certification period like an initial application except : use verification at recertification requirements, and enter “R” as the FS Receipt Type, and follow 3SquaresVT in a SNAP interview requirements at recertification.

Screen for and provide expedited service , if eligible. Prorate benefits from the date of application.

Exception : Do not prorate benefits for Migrant Worker or Seasonal Farmworker households who complete the recertification process or apply for recertification within 30 days of the end of the certification period.

Note : If the household wants to complete the recertification more than 30 days after the end of the certification period, it must reapply. Process the application as an initial application, including screening for expedited service. See Application and Expedited Service .
heading: 2400.6.2.4 Actions Completed After the Certification Period Expires

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2400 Recertification

body:
Determine eligibility based on circumstances anticipated for the certification period starting the month following the last month of the current certification period.

Review CATN and TODO for case information, inconsistencies, or verification not previously provided that are due at recertification.

Use information reported on the ESD 202, obtained during the interview, and any required verifications to determine the household’s eligibility for the new certification period.

Clear any outstanding data matches.

Budget the household’s income and deductions following Determining Income and Deductions policy.

Keep the Head of Household at recertification, unless the household selects someone else.

Disqualify members who voluntarily quit or reduce work hours , without good cause , in the last month of the certification period. Begin the disqualification the first month of the recertification period.

Benefits are not issued for the recertification period before the previous certification period ends. Households are not eligible for expedited service if the household files an application for recertification before the end of their current certification period. Households that file applications after the end of their current certification period must receive expedited service, if eligible. See Expedited Service .
heading: 2400.7.1 Procedure for Determining Eligibility

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2400 Recertification

body:
Destitute household rules apply for the first month of the recertification period for migrant or seasonal farmworkers.

Exclude income from a new source the first month of the recertification period when the income: is less than $25, and will not be received by the tenth calendar day from the 3SVT issuance.

See Destitute Defined .
heading: 2400.7.2 Destitute Households

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2400 Recertification

body:
Review the following at recertification: General work requirement exemptions , including but not limited to: Children who turned 16 years of age during the current certification period. Parents caring for a child who turned six during the current certification period. Time Limited benefit status , including but not limited to: Customers who met their work requirement with a work program or workfare must verify monthly compliance for each month of the last certification period. Complete an ICAN referral for any participant not enrolled.

Update general work requirement and time limited benefit work requirement exemptions, if needed.

Complete the Oral Explanation for required households.
heading: 2400.7.3 3SVT Work Requirements

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2400 Recertification

body:
Assign a new certification period to eligible households. See Certification Periods .

Begin the new certification period the month following the last month of the previous certification period.
heading: 2400.8 Certification Periods

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2400 Recertification

body: Explain the household’s reporting requirement. See Reduced Reporting Requirement s or 3SquaresVT in a SNAP Reporting Requirements .
heading: 2400.9 Reporting Requirements

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2400 Recertification

body:
Households must verify the following: Time limited participant’s work hours . Work status and exemptions , if not previously verified. SSN or proof of application for an SSN for newborns older than six months of age, if not previously provided. Unearned income that has changed by $50 or more per household Earned Income. Income from a new source. Income stopped. Income and resource information of the non-citizen sponsor and sponsor’s spouse. Legally obligated child support changes. Medical expenses for households claiming actual medical expenses more than $151, or who wish to switch from the standard. The first $35.01 of medical expenses when the household wishes to use the standard medical expense deduction. CATN the household’s decision to use the standard deduction if their actual expenses are more than $151. Note : Accept the household’s statement regarding their medical expenses if they wish to continue to receive the standard medical expense deduction, and verification of their medical expenses is visible in OnBase. The first $35.01 in medical expenses must be verified if not visible in OnBase. Questionable information. Income changes due to unscheduled changes benefit decreases.

Use form 202RL2 to request required verification if the application was received on or before the last day of the certification month.

Use form 202V to request required verification if the application was received after the last day of the certification month.
heading: 2400.10 Verification at Recertification for Non-3SquaresVT in a SNAP

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2400 Recertification

body:
3SquaresVT in a SNAP households must verify the following: Legally obligated child support changes. Medical expenses for households claiming actual medical expenses more than $151, or who wish to switch from the standard. The first $35.01 of medical expenses when the household wishes to use the standard medical expense deduction. CATN the household’s decision to use the standard deduction if their actual expenses are more than $151. Note : Accept the household’s statement regarding their medical expenses if they wish to continue to receive the standard medical expense deduction, and verification of their medical expenses is visible in OnBase. The first $35.01 in medical expenses must be verified if not visible in OnBase. Questionable information. Time limited participant's work hours. Work status and exemptions, if not previously verified.

Use form 202RL2 to request required verification if the application was received on or before the last day of the certification month.

Use form 202V to request required verification if the application was received after the last day of the certification month.
heading: 2400.11 Verification at Recertification for 3SquaresVT in a SNAP

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2400 Recertification
