metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2500 Benefits

body:
New information in the manual is highlighted and noted with a . See Manual Updates and for details.

Section with expandable text are noted with a throughout the manual.
heading: 2500 Benefits

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2500 Benefits

body: Benefits are 3SVT assistance issued to a household through an EBT card or through Cash Out .
heading: 2500.1 Overview

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2500 Benefits

body:
An initial month is the first month the household receives benefits for the first time or following any time the household was not approved.

Exception : Initial month for migrant or seasonal farm workers is the first month the household is approved following a period of more than 30 days since the last approval.

Households applying on the first of the month and found eligible receive a full initial month’s benefits. Households applying after the first of the month and found eligible receive prorated benefits. See Prorated Benefits, Initial Month .
heading: 2500.2 Initial Month

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2500 Benefits

body: An issuance of benefits for both the initial month and the following month at the same time. Issue combined benefits when the: date of application is after the 15th of the month, and the initial month’s prorated benefits are approved after the adverse action deadline in the month of application, and the household is eligible to receive benefits the following month.
heading: 2500.3 Combined Benefit

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2500 Benefits

body:
Suspend benefits, do not deny or close 3SVT when an eligible household qualifies for zero benefits.

Follow change processing requirements when the household reports a change that results in a benefit. See Change Processing, Benefit Increase .

Issue prorated benefits from the date the change was reported.
heading: 2500.4 Suspended Benefits

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2500 Benefits

body: Issuance of less than a full month’s benefits.
heading: 2500.5.1 Definition of Prorated Benefits

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2500 Benefits

body: Issue prorated benefits for initial month benefits when the household: Applies after the first day of the month. Benefits are issued from the date of application through the last day of the month. Was at fault for a delay in the application process. Benefits are issued from the date the household takes the required action to the end of that month. See Application, Household Caused Delay .
heading: 2500.5.2 Initial Month

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2500 Benefits

body:
Issue prorated benefits at recertification if the household: Files for recertification within 30 days after the end of the certification period. Benefits are issued from the date of application through the last day of the month. Was at fault for a delay in the recertification process. Benefits are issued from the date the household takes the required action to the end of that month.

See Recertification, Household Caused Delays .
heading: 2500.5.3 Recertification

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2500 Benefits

body: Issue prorated benefits when a household in suspended status reports a change that results in a benefit. Benefits are issued from the date the household reported the change.
heading: 2500.5.4 Activation After Suspension

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2500 Benefits

body: Determine the prorated benefit amount using the 3SquaresVT Allotment Proration Tables : Locate the full initial month’s benefit amount from the column on the left. Next find the date of application at the top. The corresponding amount is the household’s prorated amount. Note : Prorated benefits of less than $10 are not issued for the initial month only.
heading: 2500.5.5 Determining Prorated Benefits

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2500 Benefits

body:
Except during an initial month all eligible one and two-person households receive a minimum benefit minimum benefit . Minimum Benefits Household Size Minimum Benefit 1 or 2 $24

Eligible households of three or more may be entitled to zero benefits. Suspend the case for these households.
heading: 2500.6 Minimum Benefits

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2500 Benefits

body:
Follow the steps below to determine net income. Total gross monthly earned income. Add total gross monthly unearned income. Subtract farm self-employment losses, if any. If the result of step 3 is over the gross income limit gross income limit for that household size, stop here and deny the case. Exceptions : Expanded categorical eligibility (ECE) households must be below 185% gross income. Standard categorical eligibility (SCE) households or households with a Senior or disabled disabled member do not have a gross income limit. A person who meets one or more of the following: Receives disability or blindness benefits from any of these programs: Social Security, SSI , or MABD . A person meets this even when the full benefit is withheld or deferred due to recoupment. Receives a federally or state administered SSI supplement based on disability or blindness, or section 212(a) of PL 93-66. Receives a disability retirement benefit from a government agency for a disability considered permanent by SSA disability considered permanent by SSA . Any of the following conditions: Permanent loss of use of both hands, both feet, or one hand and one foot. Amputation of leg at hip. Amputation of leg or foot because of diabetes or peripheral vascular diseases. Total deafness, not correctable by surgery or hearing aid. Statutory blindness, unless caused by cataracts or detached retina. IQ of 59 or less, established after the person becomes 16 years old. Spinal cord or nerve root lesions resulting in paraplegia or quadriplegia. Multiple sclerosis with damage to the nervous system caused by scattered areas of inflammation. The inflammation recurs and has progressed to varied interference with the function of the nervous system, including severe muscle weakness, paralysis, and vision and speech defects. Muscular dystrophy with irreversible wasting of the muscles, impairing the ability to use the arms or legs. Impaired renal function caused by chronic renal disease, resulting in severely reduced function which may require dialysis or kidney transplant. Amputation of a limb of a person at least 55 years old. Is a veteran the VA considers totally (100%) disabled or permanently housebound or in need of regular aid and attendance. Is a veteran’s surviving spouse who the VA considers: in need of regular aid and attendance, or permanently housebound, or approved for benefits because of the veteran’s death and has a disability considered permanent by SSA. Is a veteran’s surviving child who the VA considers: incapable of self-support, or approved for benefits because of the veteran’s death and has a disability considered permanent by SSA Income Limits and Maximum Benefit 3SVT Household Size Expanded Categorical Eligibility ( ECE ) Senior & Disabled Seeking Separate Household Status Income Change Reporting Threshold And Gross Income Limit For Non- SCE or ECE Eligible Net Income Limit For Non- SCE or ECE eligible Maximum Monthly Benefit 3SVT House-hold Size Gross Monthly Income Limit (185% FPL ) Gross Monthly Income Limit (165% FPL) Gross Monthly Income Limit (130% FPL) Net Monthly Income Limit (100% FPL) Thrifty Food Plan 1 $2,413 $2,152 $1,696 $1,305 $298 2 $3,261 $2,909 $2,292 $1,763 $546 3 $4,109 $3,665 $2,888 $2,221 $785 4 $4,957 $4,421 $3,483 $2,680 $994 5 $5,805 $5,177 $4,079 $3,138 $1,183 6 $6,653 $5,934 $4,675 $3,596 $1,421 7 $7,501 $6,690 $5,271 $4,055 $1,571 8 $8,349 $7,446 $5,867 $4,513 $1,789 9 $9,197 $8,203 $6,463 $4,972 $2,007 10 $10,044 $8,960 $7,059 $5,431 $2,225 Each Additional Member +$848 +$757 +$596 +$459 +$218 Subtract 20% of the total earned income from the result of step 3. Subtract standard deduction standard deduction for the household’s size. Standard Deduction Household Size Amount 1 $209 2 $209 3 $209 4 $223 5 $261 6 $299 Over 6 $299 Subtract the allowed net medical deduction (after the $35 is deducted). Subtract the allowed dependent care deduction . Subtract the allowed child support payment . The result, thus far, is the remaining income. Determine BSD : Total allowed shelter expenses, then subtract 50% of the remaining income (determined in Step 9) to arrive at a net shelter amount. Compare the net shelter amount (determined in Step 10i) to the maximum shelter deduction, the BSD is the lower of the two amounts. Exception: Households that include a senior or disabled member is not subject to the maximum shelter deduction. Allow the total excess shelter. Screen for the Homeless Shelter Deduction HSD . Household qualifies for the HSD if: a household is Homeless and the household incurs or expects to incur shelter, utility or basic living cost . If the household is not homeless use the basic shelter deduction in the budget. If a household is homeless, compare the BSD to the HSD . Allow the larger deduction in the budget. Subtract the appropriate shelter deduction amount (determined in step 10, 11, 12) from the remaining income (determined in step 9) to arrive at the net income for the household. If the total is over the net income limit income limit for that household size, stop here and deny the case. Income Limits and Maximum Benefit 3SVT Household Size Expanded Categorical Eligibility ( ECE ) Senior & Disabled Seeking Separate Household Status Income Change Reporting Threshold And Gross Income Limit For Non- SCE or ECE Eligible Net Income Limit For Non- SCE or ECE eligible Maximum Monthly Benefit 3SVT House-hold Size Gross Monthly Income Limit (185% FPL ) Gross Monthly Income Limit (165% FPL) Gross Monthly Income Limit (130% FPL) Net Monthly Income Limit (100% FPL) Thrifty Food Plan 1 $2,413 $2,152 $1,696 $1,305 $298 2 $3,261 $2,909 $2,292 $1,763 $546 3 $4,109 $3,665 $2,888 $2,221 $785 4 $4,957 $4,421 $3,483 $2,680 $994 5 $5,805 $5,177 $4,079 $3,138 $1,183 6 $6,653 $5,934 $4,675 $3,596 $1,421 7 $7,501 $6,690 $5,271 $4,055 $1,571 8 $8,349 $7,446 $5,867 $4,513 $1,789 9 $9,197 $8,203 $6,463 $4,972 $2,007 10 $10,044 $8,960 $7,059 $5,431 $2,225 Each Additional Member +$848 +$757 +$596 +$459 +$218

Exception : SCE and ECE households do not have a net income test.
heading: 2500.7.1 Net Income Calculation

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2500 Benefits

body:
Follow the steps below to determine the benefit calculation. Multiply the net income times 30%. Round up to the nearest dollar if the 30% amount ends in cents (example: $100 X 30% = $30.30, rounded to $31). Deduct the rounded 30% figure from the household’s maximum benefit maximum benefit . No benefit is issued if the initial month benefits are less than $10. After the initial month , one or two person households are eligible for the minimum benefit if the end result is less than that amount. After the initial month , three or more person households are eligible for the actual amount. Income Limits and Maximum Benefit 3SVT Household Size Expanded Categorical Eligibility ( ECE ) Senior & Disabled Seeking Separate Household Status Income Change Reporting Threshold And Gross Income Limit For Non- SCE or ECE Eligible Net Income Limit For Non- SCE or ECE eligible Maximum Monthly Benefit 3SVT House-hold Size Gross Monthly Income Limit (185% FPL ) Gross Monthly Income Limit (165% FPL) Gross Monthly Income Limit (130% FPL) Net Monthly Income Limit (100% FPL) Thrifty Food Plan 1 $2,413 $2,152 $1,696 $1,305 $298 2 $3,261 $2,909 $2,292 $1,763 $546 3 $4,109 $3,665 $2,888 $2,221 $785 4 $4,957 $4,421 $3,483 $2,680 $994 5 $5,805 $5,177 $4,079 $3,138 $1,183 6 $6,653 $5,934 $4,675 $3,596 $1,421 7 $7,501 $6,690 $5,271 $4,055 $1,571 8 $8,349 $7,446 $5,867 $4,513 $1,789 9 $9,197 $8,203 $6,463 $4,972 $2,007 10 $10,044 $8,960 $7,059 $5,431 $2,225 Each Additional Member +$848 +$757 +$596 +$459 +$218

Net income may also be calculated using the ESD 203C, 3SquaresVT, Monthly Eligibility Worksheet .

See 3SquaresVT Allotment Tables for benefit amounts.
heading: 2500.7.2 Benefit Calculation

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2500 Benefits

body: 3SVT benefits are issued once a month and made available to the household on the first of every month.
heading: 2500.8 Issuance Schedule

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2500 Benefits

body:
Households access benefits using reusable plastic cards (EBT cards) to purchase eligible foods at an authorized point of sale. Authorized points of sale include but are not limited to: Retail food stores Farmer's markets Homeless Shelter Shelters for battered persons Group living for the disabled facilities Residential addiction treatment centers

Direct retailers with questions to USDA at 1-877-823-4369.
heading: 2500.9.1 Using EBT Cards

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2500 Benefits

body:
EBT cards are replaced through the EBT unit.

Follow the steps below when households report their EBT card was lost, stolen or damaged: Verify the household’s mailing address is correct in EBT Edge . If the address is correct, give the household the EBT customer service number (1-800-914-8605) to report their card was lost, stolen or damaged. If the mailing address is incorrect, send an email to the EBT unit with the household’s correct mailing address. Write "LAST NAME Address Change & Card Request" in the subject line of the email. Request the EBT Unit: update the mailing address, and replace the EBT card for the household. CATN your actions.
heading: 2500.9.2 Replacing EBT Cards

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2500 Benefits

body:
3SVT benefits will be expunged when an EBT card has not been used by the household in 274 days. Expunged benefits are returned to the federal SNAP program. The household cannot access or have benefits returned to their account once the benefits are expunged.

Exception : See Pay a Claim .
heading: 2500.9.3 Expunged EBT Benefits

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2500 Benefits

body:
Households in which all members are 65 years of age or older and/or receive SSI are eligible for Cash Out 3SVT benefits.

Cash Out benefits are issued to eligible households through direct deposit into their checking or savings account, or by a check. Households without a bank account receive their benefits through an EBT card.

Authorize issuance of a check for cash out households; eligible for expedited service for the first month of eligibility, or when the initial month’s issuance can’t be issued by computer to meet application processing timeframes .
heading: 2500.10 Cash Out

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2500 Benefits

body: The EBT card can be used to purchase eligible food at any retailer or wholesaler authorized to accept 3SVT benefits. EBT benefits or cash out benefits can be used to pay a 3SVT claim .
heading: 2500.11.1 Eligible Locations and Uses of 3SVT Benefits

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2500 Benefits

body: Households can use 3SVT EBT cards to buy eligible foods, such as but not limited to: Breads Cereals Fruits Vegetables Meats Fish Poultry Dairy products Seeds and plants which produce food to eat Authorized prepared meals
heading: 2500.11.2 Eligible Food Types

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2500 Benefits

body: Households cannot use their 3SVT EBT cards to purchase non-food items, such as but not limited to: Beer Wine Liquor Cigarettes Tobacco Pet food Soaps Paper products Household supplies Vitamins Medicines Hot foods Deposit fees Energy drinks classified by the FDA as supplements (have a supplement facts label rather than a nutrition facts label)
heading: 2500.11.3 Ineligible Food

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2500 Benefits

body:
Households can pay a 3SVT claim using benefits from their 3SVT account. Households can use their: active, expunged benefits

The household must provide written or verbal permission to repay a claim using their 3SVT active benefits.

The initial month’s benefits must not be reduced to offset a claim, unless the household agrees to the reduction.

See Overpayments and Claims for more information about claims establishment and collection.
heading: 2500.11.4 Pay a Claim

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2500 Benefits

body:
3SVT households that lost food due to a Household Misfortune , such as a flood or fire, may qualify to have the value of their lost food replaced. The household must report the loss within ten days of the misfortune.

Complete the following steps to determine the household’s eligibility and replacement amount: Determine the date of the misfortune. If the loss is due to a power outage the ten days starts the day the power is restored. Give the household Form 271, Attestation of Loss and Request for Replacement of 3SquaresVT Benefits. The household must : complete and sign the form, and return the form within ten days of reporting the loss. If the tenth day falls on a weekend or holiday the form is due the first workday following the weekend or holiday. Verify the loss through: a collateral contact, documentation from a community agency, such as the: fire department, power company, Note: When calling to confirm an outage, provide an address only. Do not give a specific customer name. Red Cross, or by completing a home visit. CATN the details of the verification. Check ELIG/D/FS to determine the benefit amount. Use the “month to date paid” amount as the allowable total for replacement. Replace the amount equal to the value of the food actually lost, but not more than the household’s “month to date paid” amount. Send an email to COPS and cc: AOPS to request the replacement. The email must contain the following information: Case name Case number Type of household misfortune Date of the loss Date power was restored (provided the misfortune was due to a power outage) Month being requested for replacement Requested replacement amount Attach completed Form 271.

AOPS reviews all situations.

COPS issues all replacements.

BPS will CATN the details and outcome of the replacement request.
heading: 2500.12 Replacement of Value of Lost Food

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2500 Benefits

body:
3SVT households that have their card physically stolen are n ot eligible for replacement benefits.

Do the following when a household reports their card was physically stolen; Advise it is a crime, and a police report may be helpful but there is no guarantee they will get their benefits back. Provide printout of EBT transaction history to the household to supplement the police report. If cash out, discuss direct deposit. Discuss possible payee. Provide tips for EBT security: Keep EBT card number and PIN a secret from everyone besides people in the 3SVT household. Check card readers before using them to avoid card skimming. Shield the PIN pad when entering the PIN. Do not write the PIN on the card sleeve. Change the PIN frequently and do not share it. Lock the card immediately after use on the EBT Edge Website . Check the EBT account regularly for unauthorized charges. If a transaction is unexpectedly declined or you receive an error code, contact the EBT Customer Services Helpline to verify the transactions on the account.
heading: 2500.13 Physically Stolen EBT card

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2500 Benefits

body:
Release # 25-5

Effective Date: 11/01/2025

Legal Base Food and Nutrition Act of 2008, as amended. 7 CFR § 271.2 7 CFR § 273.10(a) 7 CFR § 273.18(g) 7 CFR § 274.1(b) 7 CFR § 274.2(c) 7 CFR § 274.2(h) 7 CFR § 274.6(a) 7 CFR § 274.7 7 CFR § 282
heading: 2500.14 Last Update

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2500 Benefits

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2600 Underpayments

body:
New information in the manual is highlighted and noted with a . See Manual Updates and for details.

Section with expandable text are noted with a throughout the manual.
heading: 2600 Underpayments

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2600 Underpayments

body:
Restore underpaid benefits to households when the underpayment was not caused by the household. Complete the ESD 203R, Lost 3SquaresVT Benefits Restoration Worksheet .

Note : The FACU now completes the ESD 203R. See Overpayments and Underpayments SOP .
heading: 2600.1 Procedure to Restore Underpaid Benefits

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2600 Underpayments

body:
Act within ten days to restore benefits due to an underpayment because of: an agency error , changes in State or Federal regulations or laws , or a fair hearing decision reversing an action taken on the case.

Restore underpayments even if the household is not currently eligible. The household is not required to request issuance of the underpayment.
heading: 2600.2 When to Restore Underpaid Benefits

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2600 Underpayments

body:
Restore underpaid benefits for not more than 12-months before the date: the household requests the underpayment be restored, including requesting a Fair Hearing , the underpayment is discovered, or the agency is notified of the underpayment, including fair hearing decisions reversing agency actions.

Exception : A federal regulation or court case may specify a longer restoration period.

Note : If more than one occurs at the same time restore benefits from the date that occurred first.
heading: 2600.3 Timeframes

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2600 Underpayments

body: Underpayments due to agency error occur when the agency failed to provide the household with the correct amount of benefits.
heading: 2600.4.1 Agency Error

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2600 Underpayments

body: Calculate underpayments for the time the household was eligible and had an open case. Issue the difference between the amount the household received and the correct amount.
heading: 2600.4.1.1 Incorrect Amount

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2600 Underpayments

body: Reopen the case and restore benefits beginning with the first month benefits were not received after closure.
heading: 2600.4.1.2 Case Closed in Error

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2600 Underpayments

body:
Restore benefits for the month of application and any other months when the application was: not processed timely, or denied in error.

See Initial Months
heading: 2600.4.1.3 Application Delay or Denial

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2600 Underpayments

body: Timely recertification applications denied in error or delayed must have benefits restored beginning the first month following the end of the previous certification period.
heading: 2600.4.1.4 Recertification Delay or Denial

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2600 Underpayments

body: Restore any underpaid benefits when required and as directed by State or Federal laws or regulations.
heading: 2600.4.2 Changes in State or Federal Laws or Regulations

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2600 Underpayments

body: Restore benefits when a Fair Hearing decision states the agency’s actions regarding the household’s benefit amount or eligibility were incorrect. Issue underpaid benefits for not more than 12-months from whichever timeframe date occurred first.
heading: 2600.4.3 Fair Hearing Decision

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2600 Underpayments

body: Restore underpaid benefits for any months a person was disqualified for IPV when their disqualification is reversed. Households who disagree with the IPV determination at the administrative disqualification hearing are providing notice of their request to have the underpayment restored. See Intentional Program Violation .
heading: 2600.4.4 Reversed IPV Determination

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2600 Underpayments

body:
Determine the months that benefits were potentially underpaid when the household was eligible or should have been eligible. Exclude months: when the household was ineligible, or more than 12-months before the timeframes .

Compare the amount of benefits: the household received, to what the household should have received.

If the household received less than they were due, the difference between the amounts is the underpayment.

Subtract any claims to be offset from the underpayment amount.
heading: 2600.5.1 Procedure to Determine the Amount to Restore

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2600 Underpayments

body: The household must provide any missing information needed to determine a potential underpayment when the case record doesn’t contain all necessary information. Send the household form ESD 202VCR, Verification Change Request , requesting the missing information for each month needed. Deny restored benefits due to an underpayment for the months the household fails to provide the missing information.
heading: 2600.5.2 Information Needed for Determination

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2600 Underpayments

body:
Restored benefits for underpayments must be used to offset any unpaid 3SVT claims including compromised overpayments . Amounts remaining after offsetting the claim are issued to the household. Exception : Restored underpayments are not used to offset a claim when restoring benefits due to an underpayment for an initial month’s benefit, unless the household agrees to the reduction.

Apply underpayments to a prior claim by faxing the ESD 203R, Lost 3SquaresVT Benefits Restoration Worksheet , to the Fraud and Claims Unit at 802-241-0960, ATTN: Supervisor.
heading: 2600.6 Offsetting Overpayment Claims

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2600 Underpayments

body:
Issue underpayments to the household’s EBT account in addition to any amounts the household normally received.

Cash-Out households are issued the underpayment by: check, direct deposit to their checking or savings account, or through an EBT card for households without a bank account.

If the household’s composition changed, issue benefits to the household containing the majority of members at the time the underpayment occurred. When the majority of household members can’t be located or determined, issue benefits to the household containing the person who was the Head of Household at the time the underpayment occurred.
heading: 2600.7 Issuing Benefits

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2600 Underpayments

body:
Send the household form ESD 220, Notice of Decision, advising the household of the underpayment being restored including the: amount of the underpayment, reason for the underpayment, and household’s right to a fair hearing if they disagree.

When underpayments are used to offset a claim, the notice must also include: an explanation that the claim amount was adjusted, the amount of the underpayment used to offset the claim, the new claim amount, and the amount of underpayment to be issued, if any.
heading: 2600.8 Notification

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2600 Underpayments

body: Households that do not agree with the underpayment amount have the right to request a fair hearing within 90-days of the date they are notified of the restored underpayment. Issue the original underpayment amount to the household, pending the hearing decision. Do not adjust the underpayment amount unless the hearing decision rules in favor of the household and requires an additional underpayment issuance.
heading: 2600.9.1 Disagrees with the Amount

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2600 Underpayments

body: When a determination is made finding the household was not underpaid, and the household disagrees with the decision, they have 90-days to request a fair hearing. Restore benefits to the household only when the fair hearing rules in favor of the household.
heading: 2600.9.2 Disagrees with the Decision

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2600 Underpayments

body: CATN: the reason for the underpayment, the calculation, the months restored, if benefits were used to offset a claim, and the amount of the underpayment before and after an offset.
heading: 2600.10 CATN

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2600 Underpayments

body:
Release # 18-2

Effective Date: 07/18/2018

Legal Base Food and Nutrition Act of 2008, as amended. 7 CFR § 272.1(g) 7 CFR § 273.2(k)(1)(iii)(E) 7 CFR § 273.17 7 CFR § 273.18(g)(3).
heading: 2600.12 Last Update

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2600 Underpayments

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2700 Overpayments and Claims

body:
New information in the manual is highlighted and noted with a . See Manual Updates and for details.

Section with expandable text are noted with a throughout the manual.
heading: 2700 Overpayments and Claims

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2700 Overpayments and Claims

body:
Complete ESD 108FS, 3SquaresVT Claim , when a household: received more benefits than they were eligible to receive; or has been found to have trafficked benefits .

Note : FACU completes the ESD 108FS. See Overpayments and Underpayments SOP and Overpayment Guide for referral process.

In addition to overpayments discovered through case management, other potential sources include, but are not limited to: Quality Control review findings Information reported by the household or other sources Supervisory case reviews Automated resources
heading: 2700.1 Processing Overpayments and Claims

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2700 Overpayments and Claims

body: There are three types of overpayments: IPV IHE Agency Errors
heading: 2700.2.1 List of Overpayment Types

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2700 Overpayments and Claims

body: An overpayment was intentionally caused by a household member or authorized representative. See Intentional Program Violation .
heading: 2700.2.2 Intentional Program Violation (IPV)

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2700 Overpayments and Claims

body: An overpayment resulted from a misunderstanding or unintended error on the part of the household. Examples include but are not limited to: Incorrect or incomplete information was provided. Required changes were not provided to the agency; or if reported, they were not reported timely. Fair hearing decision upheld the agency’s actions and continued benefits were issued to the household.
heading: 2700.2.3 Inadvertent Household Error (IHE)

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2700 Overpayments and Claims

body:
An overpayment caused by the agency’s action or failure to take action. Examples include but are not limited to: Failure to take prompt action on a reported change. Incorrect computation of 3SVT benefits amount. Benefits were issued after the certification period expired. Reach Up grant changes were not budgeted.

Note : When an Inadvertent Household Error and Agency Error occur at the same time, consider the error as an Agency Error if it is not possible to determine the overpayment amount for each error. If it is possible, establish two overpayments.
heading: 2700.2.4 Agency Error

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2700 Overpayments and Claims

body:
Determine the correct amount of 3SVT benefits from the Date of Discovery back to the month the overpayment occurred, but not beyond 12 months for inadvertent household and agency overpayments.

Intentional Program Violation overpayments are calculated by the FACU from the date of discovery back to the month the overpayment occurred, but not beyond 6 years.
heading: 2700.3.1 Determining the Applicable Dates of Overpayment

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2700 Overpayments and Claims

body: Correct only those items that were incorrect when calculating the overpayment amount. For example , if the overpayment was caused by incorrect unearned income, use the corrected unearned income amount but don’t change any other items from the original budget. Obtain verification. Determine the correct amount of benefits for each month that an overpayment occurred. Use the ESD 203C, Food Stamp Eligibility Worksheet , or the Quick Eligibility Check function. Attach copies of the worksheet to the ESD 108FS filed in the case record. Send a copy of the worksheets along with the ESD 108FS to the FACU and to OnBase. Remember to use the rules and benefit tables that were in effect at the time of the overpayment. Use the actual income for the month received, when correcting income. Do not average or convert income being corrected. Note : Do not correct income if the payer determines that the income paid out was incorrect, budget the income amount actually received. For example , if the SSA determines that SSI was underpaid, do not correct the past 3SVT budgets. Change the budget when the household begins to receive the correct SSI amount. When correcting earned income, allow the 20% earned income deduction only for Agency Error claims. Do not allow the 20% earned income deduction for earned income that was reported incorrectly or not reported which results in an IHE or IPV claim. Subtract the correct amount of benefits from the benefits actually received. Reduce the overpayment amount by any Expunged Benefits .
heading: 2700.3.2 Calculating Overpayment Amounts Except for Trafficking

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2700 Overpayments and Claims

body:
The amount of the overpayment is the value of the trafficked benefits as determined by: the person’s admission; Adjudication ; or the information that forms the basis for the trafficking determination.

The FACU establishes the overpayment amount and claim.
heading: 2700.3.3 Overpayment Amounts Related to Trafficking

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2700 Overpayments and Claims

body: Do not establish an overpayment for the following reasons: The application form is not signed. The change was not required to be reported. Exception : Complete an overpayment as an Agency Error if a change was reported by the household and the agency should have acted on the change but did not . The amount of the overpayment is less than $125 for Inadvertent Household Error (IHE) or less than $600 for Agency Errors . Exception : Overpayments must be completed whenever it is determined there is an overpayment as a result of a Quality Control review.
heading: 2700.3.4 When Not to Establish Overpayments

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2700 Overpayments and Claims

body:
Complete an ESD 108FS, 3SquaresVT Claim , within 180 days from Date of Discovery . The form must be signed by the supervisor as part of the completion process.

Complete an entry in RECO. The FACU generates the form from RECO and sends with notice to the household.
heading: 2700.3.5 Overpayment Establishment

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2700 Overpayments and Claims

body:
The following members are responsible for repaying the overpayment: Each adult receiving 3SVT benefits when the overpayment occurred; or Persons connected to the household, such as an authorized representative, who actually trafficked or otherwise caused an overpayment or trafficking.

The FACU initiates collection action against the household.

When an adult member of an overpayment household moves to a different household, ACCESS issues an informational message to the ES. Notify FACU if this message is received. FACU will transfer the overpayment in RECO to the new case (DISP panel) if the overpayment will be pursued in the new household.
heading: 2700.4.1 Members Responsible for Repayment of Overpayment

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2700 Overpayments and Claims

body:
The ESD 108FSA, 3SquaresVT Claim Notice , is automatically sent the day after RECO is updated. Refer all repayment questions to the FACU .

Exception : The ES must resolve all questions regarding why the overpayment occurred or how the amount was calculated.
heading: 2700.4.2 Notice Procedures

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2700 Overpayments and Claims

body: The FACU may suspend collection action when there is no usable address to contact the client. The overpayment is written off after twelve months if a reapplication has not been submitted or the agency hasn’t located a usable address.
heading: 2700.4.3 Suspended Collection

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2700 Overpayments and Claims

body: The overpayment amount for Agency Error and Inadvertent Household Error may be reduced if the household’s economic circumstances dictate the overpayment will not be paid in three years. The FACU is responsible for compromising overpayment amounts.
heading: 2700.4.4 Compromised Overpayments

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2700 Overpayments and Claims

body: The fraud supervisor may terminate and write off the overpayment if: All household members are deceased; A fair hearing finds no overpayment; The overpayment has a balance less than $25 and no payment has been received in the last 90 days; or There has not been a payment on the overpayment for 3 years or more.
heading: 2700.4.5 Terminated Overpayments

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2700 Overpayments and Claims

body: If the participating household does not make full repayment, the household’s 3SVT benefits will automatically be reduced by: IHE and Agency Error overpayments: The greater of $10 per month or 10% of the household’s monthly benefit, unless the household requests a larger amount. IPV overpayments: The greater of 20% of the household’s Monthly Entitlement for households with a disqualified member or $20. The recoupment changes to 20% of the benefit when the disqualification period is over. The household may request a larger amount. Exception : Benefits are not recouped from the initial month benefits. ACCESS is programmed to start recoupment the month following the month of application approval.
heading: 2700.4.6.1 Recoupment

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2700 Overpayments and Claims

body: The overpayment amount is reduced by any Expunged Benefits .
heading: 2700.4.6.2 Benefits Expungement

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2700 Overpayments and Claims

body: The household may pay the overpayment partially or in full with cash, check, money order, or with unused EBT benefits. FACU is responsible for obtaining a written agreement from the household prior to using active EBT benefits to repay an overpayment. If paid by cash or its equivalent, issue the household an ESD 140 receipt, and Give the payment to the assigned staff member. Send to Accounts Receivable, Administrative Services.
heading: 2700.4.6.3 Household Repayment

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2700 Overpayments and Claims

body: Underpaid benefits must first offset the overpayment amount. Amounts remaining after offsetting the claim are issued to the household.
heading: 2700.4.6.4 Underpaid Benefits

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2700 Overpayments and Claims

body: A delinquent overpayment may be transferred to TOP by the FACU . The referral allows reduction of any federal payment due to the client from the US Treasury. Example : Income tax refunds, federal salary, etc.
heading: 2700.4.6.5 Treasury Offset Program (TOP)

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2700 Overpayments and Claims

body:
Release # 19-2

Effective Date: 07/19/2019

Legal Base Food and Nutrition Act of 2008, as amended. 7 CFR § 273.18
heading: 2700.6 Last Update

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2700 Overpayments and Claims

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2800 Intentional Program Violation

body:
New information in the manual is highlighted and noted with a . See Manual Updates and for details.

Section with expandable text are noted with a throughout the manual.
heading: 2800 Intentional Program Violation

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2800 Intentional Program Violation

body: An IPV occurs when a household member intentionally: made a false or misleading statement; misrepresented, concealed, or withheld facts; or trafficked benefits .
heading: 2800.1.1 Definition of Intentional Program Violation

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2800 Intentional Program Violation

body:
Trafficking means: Exchanging 3SVT benefits or EBT cards for non-food items or cash; Exchanging 3SVT benefits for firearms, ammunition, explosives, or Controlled Substance ; Returning a container for a deposit if the product was purchased with 3SVT benefits when the contents were intentionally discarded; or Reselling a product purchased with 3SVT benefits for non-food items or cash.

See Overpayment Amounts Related to Trafficking .
heading: 2800.1.2 Trafficked Benefits

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2800 Intentional Program Violation

body: Review the file to determine if the client may have committed an IPV to obtain incorrect benefits. If so, complete the ESD 108SFR, Suspected Fraud Referral . The FACU completes the ESD 108FS, 3SquaresVT Claim , after the investigation is completed.
heading: 2800.2.1 Fraud Referral

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2800 Intentional Program Violation

body: Complete an ESD 108SFR, Suspected Fraud Referral , within 60 days from the Date of Discovery . Send the form to the supervisor as part of the completion process. The supervisor will: return the form to the ES asking for additional verification (if necessary); and/or electronically sign and send the form to the Fraud and Claims Unit and return a signed copy to the ES; and notify the ES of the FACU decision regarding the referral.
heading: 2800.2.2 Overpayment Referral

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2800 Intentional Program Violation

body:
The Fraud and Claims Unit reviews the case circumstances and supporting information and: makes a decision on referral acceptance or rejection; and sends a memo to the supervisor with their decision: No overpayment, or Claim established by FACU as an IHE or agency error (ESD 108FS is completed by FACU), or Accepted for investigation

Once the investigation is completed, the Fraud and Claims unit determines the appropriate course of action. The case may be referred to the AAG 's office for an Administrative Disqualification Hearing or to the State’s Attorney for criminal prosecution. The FACU makes the appropriate referrals and notifications.

Note : If the IPV is referred for court action, the Fraud and Claims Unit will issue a memo detailing the court findings and restitution requirements.
heading: 2800.2.3 Fraud and Claims Unit Process

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2800 Intentional Program Violation

body:
ADH s are held, if a hearing waiver is not signed, to determine if an accused person committed an IPV .

The hearing is heard before the HSB and conducted by a Hearing Officer , appointed by the HSB. The hearing officer completes a fair and impartial review of the suspected violation. After the hearing, a hearing decision is issued confirming or denying the IPV.
heading: 2800.3.1 Hearings Procedures

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2800 Intentional Program Violation

body: The Fraud and Claims Unit will provide the accused person with an ESD 108FSHW, Administrative Disqualification Hearing Waiver , notifying them of the option to waive an administrative disqualification hearing. If they do not sign the waiver, FACU refers the case to the AAG to schedule the hearing. A hearing notice is then sent with a date, time and another copy of the waiver. The signed waiver is accepted by the: Fraud and Claims Unit; or AAG ; or Human Services Board officer during the hearing process.
heading: 2800.3.2 Waived Hearings

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2800 Intentional Program Violation

body: The hearing officer issues an ESD 108FSHN, Administrative Disqualification Hearing Notice, to the household and the Fraud and Claims Unit at least 30 days in advance of the hearing date. The notice informs the household when, where, and how the hearing will be conducted.
heading: 2800.3.3 Hearing Notice

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2800 Intentional Program Violation

body: The household may request a postponement of the hearing at least ten days in advance of the date of the scheduled hearing. The hearing must be rescheduled within 30 days. Only one postponement is allowed.
heading: 2800.3.4 Postponement

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2800 Intentional Program Violation

body:
Within 90 days of the date the accused person is notified in writing that a hearing has been scheduled, the hearing officer shall: conduct the hearing; arrive at a decision; and notify the accused person and the Fraud and Claims Unit of the decision.

The accused person is allowed to participate in the program during the hearing process, if all 3SVT eligibility rules are met.

The hearing is conducted by the rules of procedure outlined in Administrative Disqualification Hearing Process .
heading: 2800.3.5 Hearing Process

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2800 Intentional Program Violation

body: The hearing officer issues a decision notice to the household and the Fraud and Claims Unit. The Fraud and Claims Unit completes the ESD 108FS, 3SquaresVT Claim , and issues written notification to the supervisor.
heading: 2800.3.6 Hearing Decision

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2800 Intentional Program Violation

body: The intentional program violation is final when the accused person: is convicted by Federal, state, or local court; is convicted by the ADH decision; or signs an ESD 108FSHW, Administrative Disqualification Hearing Waiver , waiving their right to an ADH. Note : See Disqualified Members to determine how to treat the income and resources for the individual with the IPV.
heading: 2800.4.1 Final Determination of Intentional Program Violation

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2800 Intentional Program Violation

body:
The FACU takes all actions necessary to implement the disqualification, establish the overpayment amount and claim. The supervisor receives a Fraud Disposition Report that summarizes the actions taken.

The FACU sends the ESD 108FSDN, Administrative Disqualification Notice , to the household after the accused person’s intentional program violation is finalized. The notice informs the household that: The disqualification will start the first of the month, 10 days after the date the notice is mailed. The penalty is imposed for the accused person, even if the accused person is not currently receiving 3SVT benefits. The penalty continues uninterrupted until the time period expires. A new penalty time period may not be imposed for any action that occurred prior to the written notification of the disqualification period.
heading: 2800.4.2 Disqualification and IPV Claim

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2800 Intentional Program Violation

body:
The disqualification penalty for intentional program violations (other than those described below) are: One year for the first violation; Two years for the second violation; and Permanently for the third violation. Note : 1 or more violations that occurred before June 1, 1983 are counted as one previous disqualification when determining the above penalties.

If the IPV is a result of trafficking benefits of $500 or more, the disqualification penalty is: Permanently for the first violation.

If the IPV is a result of trafficking benefits for a Controlled Substance , the disqualification penalty is: Two years for the first violation; Permanently for the second violation.

If the IPV is a result of trafficking benefits for firearms, ammunition, or explosives, the disqualification penalty is: Permanently for the first violation.

If the IPV is a result of a person fraudulently reporting their identity or residence to receive multiple benefits at the same time, the disqualification penalty is: Ten years for each violation.
heading: 2800.4.2.1 Penalty Time Periods

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2800 Intentional Program Violation

body:
A household loses categorical eligibility for 3SVT benefits when any household member is found guilty of an Intentional Program Violation (IPV). Even if the household previously qualified for benefits based on categorical eligibility criteria, they will no longer be automatically eligible. Instead, the household’s eligibility will be re-screened based on standard eligibility. Update the EITC on the STAT/STAT panel from a "Y" to a "N" to ensure categorical eligibility is lost.

The loss of categorical eligibility applies to the entire household, not just the individual who committed the IPV.

The disqualified member’s income and resources will be treated according to Excluded Members .
heading: 2800.4.3 Loss of Categorical Eligibility

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2800 Intentional Program Violation

body: When a household loses categorical eligibility because a household member has received an IPV the following steps will be taken: Actions by the Fraud and Claims Unit (FACU): Apply the IPV to the household member's MBR2 panel in ACCESS. Change the EITC flag on STAT/C/STAT to "N". Enter a WARN stating the household is not eligible to receive the "Y" in the EITC field until the IPV has expired. Include the expiration date in the warn. Responsibilities of the district: When a household loses categorical eligibility, ACCESS will automatically re-screen the household using standard eligibility requirements. See section below.
heading: 2800.4.3.1 Transitioning a Household to Standard Eligibility Due to IPV

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2800 Intentional Program Violation

body:
The gross income limit is 130% FPL .

The net income limit is 100% FPL .

Households are subject to the Resource Limit .

If the household fails the re-screening for standard eligibility, the case will appear on the district daily report, requiring manual processing for closure by the ES.
heading: 2800.4.3.2 Standard Eligibility Requirements

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2800 Intentional Program Violation

body: The Fraud and Claims Unit initiates collection action against the household and monitors collections. See Repayment for additional information on the collection process.
heading: 2800.5 Repayment Process

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2800 Intentional Program Violation

body:

Effective Date: 08/22/2025

heading: 2800.6 Last Update

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2900 Fair Hearings

body:
New information in the manual is highlighted and noted with a . See Manual Updates and for details.

Section with expandable text are noted with a throughout the manual.
heading: 2900 Fair Hearings

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2900 Fair Hearings

body:
Fair hearings are held when the household, or a representative chosen by the household, makes a hearing request to object to actions taken against the household’s case. The fair hearing is heard before the HSB and conducted by a Hearing Officer , appointed by HSB. The hearing officer provides an impartial and objective review of the agency’s actions. The hearing officer confirms or denies the agency’s actions.

See: Fair Hearing Overview .
heading: 2900.1 Overview

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2900 Fair Hearings

body:
Households must be notified in writing at application and at an Adverse Action their right to: request a hearing, how to request a hearing, their right to be represented, and the names of persons or organizations providing free legal services.

Remind the household of these rights any time they tell the agency they disagree with an action taken on their case.
heading: 2900.2 Notification of Hearing Rights

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2900 Fair Hearings

body:
Hearing requests are clear verbal or written notices by the household or representative of their wish to appeal an action taken by the agency.

Households have the right to request a fair hearing when they disagree with an action taken by the agency.

Ask the household or representative to clarify their hearing request when it is unclear what action is at issue.

Do not limit or interfere with the household or representative’s right to request a hearing.

Never coerce or try to influence the household or representative to withdraw their hearing request.
heading: 2900.3 Hearing Requests

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2900 Fair Hearings

body:
Households have 90-days to request a hearing from the notice date of: any action to reduce or terminate benefits, a restoration of underpayments , or a denial of a restoration of underpayments .

In addition, hearings may be requested anytime during the certification period to dispute the current level of benefits.

Treat hearing requests that are not timely the same as timely requests. Follow the policy for actions after receipt of the request . HSB will determine if the household had good cause for submitting their hearing request late. If so, they will allow a fair hearing on the household’s issue.
heading: 2900.4 Timeframes to Request a Hearing

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2900 Fair Hearings

body: The receipt date for the hearing request is the date: the household verbally requests the hearing, the date stamp date if the request is mailed to the agency, the date stamp date if the request is faxed, or the household requests to reinstate their hearing request.
heading: 2900.5 Receipt Date of the Hearing Request

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2900 Fair Hearings

body: For steps to follow after receipt of a hearing request, see: ESD Responsibilities , and Human Services Board Rules .
heading: 2900.6 Actions After Receipt of the Request

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2900 Fair Hearings

body:
Continue benefits at the amount the household received immediately before the adverse action if the household: requested a fair hearing before the advance notice effective date, and if their certification period has not ended.

Provide continued benefits even when the household did not indicate if they wish to waive their right to continued benefits.

Exception: Continued benefits are only allowed for disputed Federal Rule/Regulation or Mass Changes when the household believes their benefits were: computed incorrectly, or the rules were misapplied/misinterpreted.

Establish a claim if continued benefits were issued when the household or representative withdraws the hearing request before the hearing.
heading: 2900.7.1 Household Requirements

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2900 Fair Hearings

body: Continued benefits are not provided for the following reasons: Household or their representative waives their right to have the benefits continued. Note : CATN if the household chooses to waive continued benefits. Actions caused by a Federal rule change or mass change. Exception: Continued benefits are only allowed for disputed Federal Rule/Regulation or Mass Changes when the household believes their benefits were: computed incorrectly, or the rules were misapplied/misinterpreted. Households who, without good cause as determined by HSB, didn’t request a hearing before the adverse action became effective.
heading: 2900.7.2 When Not to Provide Continued Benefits

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2900 Fair Hearings

body:
Do not reduce or end continued benefits prior to receipt of the hearing decision, except for one of the following reasons: The certification period ends. The household may reapply / recertify and be determined eligible for a new certification period and new benefit amount. A preliminary decision is made in writing at the hearing by the Hearing Officer when the sole issue is on a Federal rule and the agency acted on the rule correctly. A change caused an Adverse Action to the household’s eligibility or benefit amount while the hearing decision is pending, and the household or their representative did not request a hearing for this change action. A mass change occurs that affects the household’s eligibility or benefit amount, or The household or its representative verbally withdraws their hearing request and doesn’t reinstate their hearing request.

Send the household an ESD 220, Notice of Decision, if their benefits are reduced or ended when any of the above reasons occurs while the hearing decision is pending.
heading: 2900.7.3 Benefit Changes While the Decision is Pending

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2900 Fair Hearings

body: Within 60-days of receipt of the fair hearing, unless delayed , the hearing must be: held, a decision reached, and notices of the decision provided to: the agency, and the household and/or their representative.
heading: 2900.8.1 Fair Hearing Procedures

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2900 Fair Hearings

body:
The HSB clerk will notify the household and agency of the scheduled hearing at least ten days before the hearing date, including the: Date, Time, and Place of the hearing.

The hearing will be scheduled between seven and 30-days from the receipt date of the hearing request.
heading: 2900.8.2 Scheduling Fair Hearing

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2900 Fair Hearings

body: Expedite hearing requests from Migrant Worker or Seasonal Farmworker households who may not be in the area very long. Process these hearing requests to ensure a decision is made and underpayments are issued, if required, before the household leaves the area.
heading: 2900.8.3 Migrant and Seasonal Farmworker Households

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2900 Fair Hearings

body: Households can request to delay the scheduled hearing, but not for more than 30-days.
heading: 2900.8.4 Household Requests a Delay

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2900 Fair Hearings

body: Time limits to act on the decision are extended for as many days as the hearing is delayed at the household’s request. Example : Household or its representative requests a ten-day extension. Notification of the decision must be made within 70-days of the hearing request. (60-days normal action period plus the ten-day extension given to the household.)
heading: 2900.8.4.1 Decision Action Delayed

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2900 Fair Hearings

body:
HSB will deny hearing requests when: The hearing request was not made timely , and HSB decides the household does not have good cause for submitting their request untimely. The hearing issue is a Federal rule that: reduces, suspends, or cancels benefits.

Exception : Hearings regarding Federal Rule/Regulation or Mass Changes are only allowed when the household believes their benefits were: computed incorrectly, or the rules were misapplied/misinterpreted.
heading: 2900.9 Denial of a Hearing Request

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2900 Fair Hearings

body:
Hearing requests are dismissed when the household or representative: Fails to: appear for the hearing, and did not respond within seven days to the HSB clerk’s written inquiry about the household/representative missing the hearing. Withdraws the hearing request in writing. Verbally withdraws the hearing request.

Note : Send form ESD 113W, 3SquaresVT Fair Hearing Withdrawal to the household and its representative within ten days of a withdrawal. This confirms the withdrawal request and provides the household the chance to reinstate their hearing request.
heading: 2900.10 Dismissal of a Hearing Request

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2900 Fair Hearings

body:
Households and/or their representative may request to have their withdrawn hearing request reinstated. The household must request to reinstate their hearing within ten days of receipt of the ESD 113W confirming the household’s withdrawn hearing request.

A hearing request may be reinstated once .
heading: 2900.11 Reinstated Hearing Request

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2900 Fair Hearings

body: Consolidated hearings mean multiple hearing requests from different households regarding the same issue, held together in one hearing, at the same time. Each household or representative must approve having a consolidated hearing. The household is allowed an opportunity to present their own case at the consolidated hearing.
heading: 2900.12.1.1 Multiple Households with the Same Issue

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2900 Fair Hearings

body: The hearing officer may choose to consolidate a single household’s multiple hearing requests into one hearing.
heading: 2900.12.1.2 Multiple Issues for the Same Household

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2900 Fair Hearings

body:
Hearings must be attended by: AAG , and the household and/or their representative.

Additionally, the following people may attend the hearing: agency witnesses, as requested, household witnesses, as requested, or friends or family chosen by the household. Note : Hearing officers have the authority to limit the number of persons attending the hearing when space is limited.

Do not introduce confidential information at the hearing, such as the names and addresses of people who provided information about the hearing issue without the household’s knowledge.
heading: 2900.12.2 Agency Actions

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2900 Fair Hearings

body: Allow the household or representative the following rights during the hearing: Examine all documents and records being used in the hearing. Present their case or have a representative present their case. Bring witnesses. Argue issues without undue interference. Cross-examine agency witnesses. Disagree with testimony or evidence. Submit their own evidence and facts.
heading: 2900.12.3 Household’s Rights

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2900 Fair Hearings

body: Decisions by the hearing officer comply with Federal law and regulations and become part of the hearing record. Notice of the decision will be provided to the household, their representative and the agency. AAG will email a copy of the decision to the district office. AOPS will send a copy of form ESD 113C, Notice to Comply to the district handling the fair hearing, providing the date to comply with the hearing decision.
heading: 2900.13.1 Hearing Decisions Procedure

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2900 Fair Hearings

body:
AAG will email the supervisor and ES with the decision if the household is upheld. AOPS will send a copy of form ESD 113C. After receipt of the ESD 113C, send the household and their representative the ESD 220FH, Notice of Decision .

Benefits must be updated within ten days of receipt of the ESD 113C when the decision increases the household’s benefits. Restore benefits for any months where benefits were underpaid , including if the household waived continued benefits.

Complete and return the ESD 113C to AOPS when the decision’s ordered actions are completed.

Remove any WARN for this fair hearing issue.

CATN: your actions, and that an ACCESS generated ESD 220 was not mailed.
heading: 2900.13.2 Decision Upholds the Household

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2900 Fair Hearings

body:
Benefits must be updated to decrease or end by the next scheduled issuance that follows receipt of the ESD 113C. Send the household an ESD 220FH informing the household of: the action that will be taken based on the hearing decision, and the effective date.

Enter closure code 14 in FIAT section of ACCESS when closing the case based on the hearing decision.

Remove any WARN for this fair hearing issue.

CATN: your actions, and that an ACCESS generated ESD 220 was not mailed.
heading: 2900.13.3 Decision Upholds the Agency

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2900 Fair Hearings

body: Establish a claim when: the household did not waive their right to continued benefits, the hearing decision upheld the agency’s actions, and benefits were overpaid.
heading: 2900.13.4 Establishing a Claim

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2900 Fair Hearings

body:
Notify FACU that the household requested a hearing on the claim. FACU will suspend the claim.

Claims waiting on a fair hearing decision are not delinquent.

Notify the FACU of the hearing decision. FACU will take action to: Delete the claim if the household is upheld. Re-establish the claim and send a manual letter to notify the household, if the claim is upheld.
heading: 2900.14 Fair Hearings Regarding Claims

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 2900 Fair Hearings

heading: 2900.15 Last Update
