metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 3000 Program Review

body:
New information in the manual is highlighted and noted with a . See Manual Updates and for details.

Section with expandable text are noted with a throughout the manual.
heading: 3000 Program Review

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 3000 Program Review

body: ME reviewers monitor program access and operations. The reviewers: Review case files for accuracy and completeness Interview staff, community agencies, and recipients Review application access and office procedures Complete a final report identifying best practices and recommendations for improvements
heading: 3000.1 Management Evaluation

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 3000 Program Review

body:
Active 3SVT cases are randomly selected for quality control review. The review determines: Eligibility and benefit accuracy. Timeliness of the action for certification within the current Federal Fiscal Year . Corrective action recommendations.

Cases denied, closed, suspended or approved for zero benefits are also randomly selected for review. The review is called CAPER . The review determines: Eligibility reason accuracy. Timeliness of the recertification action within the current Federal Fiscal Year . Accuracy of notice content. Corrective action recommendations.
heading: 3000.2.1 Random Selection for Quality Control Review

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 3000 Program Review

body: The QCR process includes: An electronic and case record review. A field investigation including an in-person interview with the household for active reviews. Verification of all eligibility factors. A report of the case review findings provided to the district management team.
heading: 3000.2.2 Quality Control Review

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 3000 Program Review

body: Upon receipt of the QCR findings, the Supervisor of the District who has the case must : Thoroughly review the QCR findings and the case file for accuracy, conferring with the worker if necessary; Within ten days, send any disagreement (based on policy only) to OPS ; Assign ES to make case corrections and establish an overpayment or underpayment , when appropriate.
heading: 3000.2.3 District Responsibility

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 3000 Program Review

body:
Quality Control will notify the agency of the household/member’s failure to cooperate with a QC requirement. The household must provide verification of all eligibility elements prior to its next approval.

If the household does not provide verification: Deny the application/recertification if a mandatory item (e.g., income) was not verified. Do not allow any expense that is not verified.
heading: 3000.2.4 Failure to Cooperate

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 3000 Program Review

body: The household is ineligible for 3SVT benefits when a household member refuses to cooperate with the Quality Control review. Quality Control will notify the agency of the household’s refusal to cooperate. Follow advance notice advance notice time frames. Advance notice is sent to a household at least ten days before: Decreasing the household’s benefits, Closing the case, or Shortening the certification period. The notice must tell the household: the reason for the action, the effective date of the action, their right to request a hearing, their right to continue to receive unchanged benefits pending the hearing, duty to repay benefits over issued pending the hearing, information on receiving free legal advice, and a telephone number to call for questions.
heading: 3000.2.5 Disqualification for Refusal to Cooperate

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 3000 Program Review

body:
End eligibility in the next possible month after you receive the notice of refusal to cooperate. The penalty lasts through the end of the Federal Fiscal Quarter , plus another 125 days or until the member(s) cooperate, whichever occurs first.

Example : You are notified on July 11, 2018 of refusal to cooperate with state QC. Advance notice expires July 22, 2018. The penalty begins August 1, 2018. If the member does not cooperate, the penalty will end February 2, 2019 (125 days after the end of the federal fiscal year – September 30, 2018).
heading: 3000.2.5.1 Penalty for Refusing to Cooperate with the State QC Review

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 3000 Program Review

body:
End eligibility in the next possible month after you receive the notice of refusal to cooperate. The penalty lasts through the end of the federal fiscal year, plus another 9 months, or until the member(s) cooperate, whichever occurs first.

Example : You are notified on July 11, 2018 of refusal to cooperate with federal QC. Advance notice expires July 22, 2018. The penalty begins August 1, 2018. If the member does not cooperate, the penalty will end June 30, 2019 (9 months after the end of the federal fiscal year – September 30, 2018).
heading: 3000.2.5.2 Penalty for Refusing to Cooperate with the Federal QC Review

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 3000 Program Review

body:
If the household cooperates with the Quality Control review, Quality Control staff will notify the ES. The household must reapply.

The disqualification follows the member who refused to cooperate. The household may reapply when the non-cooperating member leaves the household. If the non-cooperating member enters another 3SVT household, that household is disqualified until the penalty period expires or the member cooperates, whichever occurs first.

If the household reapplies before the penalty period ends, inform the household it must contact QC before benefits can be granted. Proceed with the application. Do not approve benefits until the household complies with QC.

If the household reapplies after the penalty period ends, the household is not required to cooperate with the prior Quality Control review. The household must meet all application verification requirements .
heading: 3000.2.5.3 Disqualification Ends

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 3000 Program Review

body: In addition to the federally mandated reviews identified above, supervisory case reviews are also completed. If the SCR has no findings, the ES will get a copy of the SCR to file. If a correction is needed, the ES has 7 days to correct the case and inform their supervisor of actions taken.
heading: 3000.3 Supervisory Case Reviews

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 3000 Program Review

body:

Effective Date: 12/01/2017

heading: 3100 Tables

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 3100 Tables

body:
A qualifying work quarter was earned for each Calendar Quarter a person was paid $50 or more in wages (including Migrant Worker and Seasonal Farmworker wages).

Four quarters were earned for each taxable year in which self-employment earnings were $400 or more.

A quarter was earned for each $100 (limited to four) of migrant or seasonal farmworker wages for years 1955-1977.
heading: 3100.1.1 Qualifying Work Quarters Prior to 1978

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 3100 Tables

body:
Based solely on total yearly earnings.

Divide the non-citizen’s total earned income by the increment amount for the year. (All types of earnings)

No more than four quarters can be earned in each year.

The following chart shows the increment amount for each year.
heading: 3100.1.2 Qualifying Work Quarters for 1978 and Later

metadata.source_authority: Vermont Department for Children and Families
metadata.title: Vermont 3SquaresVT Manual: 3100 Tables

heading: 3100.2.3 Amount Needed to Earn a Qualifying Quarter

body:
Self-Employment Expenses of Providing Room and Board

The following table shows the monthly self-employment expenses allowed for room and board income. If the customer provides documentation of separate identifiable expenses of providing room and/or board that exceed the applicable table amount, allow the higher amount. In no case, however, may the business expense allowed exceed the amount of gross income received from the roomer and boarder.

Access Code Type 1 Person 2 Person 3 Person 4 Person 5 Person 6+ Person 1 Room Only $254 $465 $669 $847 $1008 $1,211 2 2/3 Board $199 $364 $523 $663 $789 $947 3 Board Only $298 $546 $785 $994 $1,183 $1,421 4 Room and 2/3 Board $453 $829 $1,192 $1,510 $1,797 $2,158 5 Room and Board $552 $1011 $1,454 $1,841 $2,191 $2,632

If five or more roomers/boarders are in one payment group, the RBIN income should be handled in a BUSI panel not a RBIN panel. Use the expense from the table above as the expense in the BUSI panel. Splitting one payment onto two RBIN panels results in an incorrect business expense deduction. The RBIN panel only allows four roomers/boarders per panel. Splitting one payment onto two RBIN panels results in an incorrect business expense deduction.
heading: 3100.8.1 Self-Employment Expenses of Providing Room and Board Chart
